## 2026-07-08 (ULTRACODE ON): THE TERRAN-CLEAN CHEMISTRY ARC BUILT (C1/A/B/C2/F1/F4) plus the folded Cluster-I arcs (4, 2, 1); end-of-arc Terran-bias blind audit

The chemistry arc from the roadmap's scoped build order (C1 -> A -> B -> C2, residues F1/F4) is landed on branch `claude/nutrient-cycle`, each slice byte-neutral opt-in and falsifier-backed, followed by the three folded autonomous Cluster-I arcs (Arc 4 conservation, Arc 2 whole-body-bite, Arc 1 field-handle). The nutrient cycle's chemistry is now WORLD-DECLARED rather than Earth-hardcoded on the producer WRITE and decomposition sides (the eater side was already general).

- **C1 (T2 source-vector lift, `d048ee8`).** A producer's evolved abiotic draw is a per-cell LIST of source ids, and `extract_producers` caps productivity to the Liebig MINIMUM over the set (light AND water AND a soil nutrient, whichever is scarcest, no authored priority). `producer_sources()` emits every Abiotic edge. Byte-neutral (source not folded, one edge today; run_world default and full both matched baseline exactly). Falsifier `productivity_is_the_liebig_minimum_over_the_evolved_source_set_not_the_first`.
- **A (T5 decomposition by axes, `d98fbb8`).** The fixed ash/organic split is a data-defined `ConstituentRegistry {fraction_axis, deposit_class}` + residual, mass-EXACT by a remainder construction, the SAME split serving the material leg (a substance's own vector) and the tissue leg (a decayed body's own composition; `TissueField::decay` now returns per-parcel composition), symmetric. The ash GATHER-GATE retired: decomposability keys on the substance declaring a `bio.decomposition_barrier`, not the presence of a mineral-ash axis (verified byte-neutral: carrion and spent_hull, the only barrier substances, both declare ash too). Byte-neutral (Option None -> `terran_default` reproduces the old 2-class split; no body carries the ash axis). Falsifiers `constituent_split_is_mass_exact_and_generalises_the_ash_pair_to_k_axes` and `the_decomposition_split_is_data_defined_and_gated_on_the_barrier_not_the_ash_axis`.
- **B (T3 food as composition, `baac36c`), the HIGHEST-RISK slice (the universal food representation + ingest hot path).** A producer's standing food carries its OWN composition (a nutrient simplex) stored as a single logistic biomass VOLUME with the composition riding as fixed densities. Grazing draws the VOLUME (all axes shrink together) via `regrow_supply`'s Liebig-minimum read-back (`env::food_volume` = min over axes of supply/density), so the per-axis ingest hot path is UNCHANGED and the trap of N independent stocks is avoided. `genesis::producer_compositions()` mirrors `consumer_bodies`; `EnvironFields::set_producer_food` normalises to a simplex excluding the environmental water/salinity axes (F4). Byte-neutral (opt-in per-cell Option, None -> the single energy-density food; default AND full both matched baseline exactly). **The demo wiring is OWNER-GATED and left UNARMED:** seeding real plant compositions collapses the full run (pop ~25 -> ~1) because a structurally-dominated plant carries little digestible energy per biomass, until the owner sets the food-VALUE axis-conversion reserved calibration, which no autonomous value may fabricate; the arming call is documented at the `worldbuild.rs` seeding site. Falsifier `standing_food_carries_the_producers_own_composition_and_grazing_draws_the_whole_volume`.
- **C2/F1/F4 (`dc36c73`).** F1 (the ash gather-gate) retired by A; only a stale doc comment remained, corrected. F4 (water re-import through the food vector) handled by B's exclusion of the environmental axes. C2 (background source-vector for unseeded cells) deferred as optional (the background already uses the general Liebig `biomass_from`).
- **Arc 4 (conservation ledger, `01687c3`).** The closed material+soil+tissue decay ledger registered (`Embodiment::decay_ledger_mass`, `MaterialField::total_mass`, `TissueField::total_volume`), the producer BIOMASS layer deliberately OPEN (a source/sink, not in the ledger). Falsifier `the_matter_cycle_conserves_material_plus_soil_plus_tissue_across_both_legs` proves invariance across both legs over many ticks.
- **Arc 2 (whole-body-bite predation, `035279e`).** `TissueField::bite` removes one body VOLUME once and credits EVERY assimilated axis, fixing the multi-axis over-depletion leak (the per-axis geophage draw removed a volume per axis). The geophage's per-axis loop now eats only loose cell matter and inventory; a single whole-body bite pass after it eats the located body, sizing the bite so no reserve overflows (Liebig min over the digestible body axes of the room-filling volume). Byte-neutral where no corpse lies (the bite pass is a no-op on an empty tissue field); full re-pins with an IDENTICAL population trajectory (33,34,15,7,1,0 at seed 0x5EED both before and after, so the seed's decline is pre-existing, not the fix). Falsifier `a_whole_body_bite_removes_one_volume_and_credits_every_axis_where_per_axis_takes_over_deplete`.
- **Arc 1 (field-handle, `5d95b9a`).** `AbioticField` decoupled into an explicit field identity (Light/Water/Soil) plus a data `AbioticBinding::depletes` flag (FINDING-1), so the flux-versus-stock choice is the world's data and an alien source becomes a data row (enables Arc 5). Byte-neutral (Light+false reproduces Flux; Water/Soil+true reproduce the stocks).

State: sim 710 lib tests + 36 test binaries green, clippy + fmt clean, run_world default `7ac663ee` byte-unchanged, --scenario full re-pinned `fd0d7b2e` (the armed scenario re-pins as the mechanism changes). The consensus roadmap carries the arc-built record. IN FLIGHT at hand-off: a repo-walled Terran-bias blind audit (six lens auditors, Terran-bias + emergence + conservation + determinism + byte-neutrality + overflow, each finding verified against source). Its confirmed findings and any hardenings are recorded in the AUDIT OUTCOME note below.

REMAINING Cluster I (per the arc-closure plan): Arc 5 (source-model T1 derive Region.abiotic from the declared floor axes + T4 niche axes + T6 decompose kernel axes, AUTONOMOUS to build but OWNER-GATED to finalize, depends on Arc 1); Arc 6 (biosphere-into-run 4b, grow each species' body from its genome via the morphogen substrate); Arc 7 (creatures-have-simpler-minds, every evolved biosphere organism a cheap living agent). OWNER-GATED batch to finalize the chemistry arc: the T2/T3/T5 axis-conversion reserved values set + sign-off, then Part 62 consolidation; and the CLUSTER-I MERGE CHECKPOINT (owner runs final sims, merges the PR, directs the next branch).

> AUDIT OUTCOME (`d53b739`). The repo-walled six-lens blind audit (17 agents: Terran-bias + emergence + conservation + determinism + byte-neutrality + overflow, each finding verified against source) confirmed nine facts; FOUR were real latent defects, hardened, all byte-neutral (the current scenarios never triggered them, so default `7ac663ee` and full `fd0d7b2e` are unchanged): (1) CRITICAL, the whole-body bite double-credited two reserves backed by the SAME substance (mass created in the eater); fixed with a per-substance `consumed` tracker, falsifier added. (2) CRITICAL, `food_volume`'s Liebig fallback was ZERO where a non-limiting overflowing axis needs MAX (one axis could zero a whole cell's food); fixed. (3) MAJOR, `decomposed` was unclamped, so a world-authored `bio.decomposition_rate` above one created matter; clamped to the volume. (4) the bite sized to the WHOLE body when a full or non-digesting eater had no qualifying axis (destroying a carcass it could not use) and mis-handled a density overflow; fixed to bite the tightest room-filling volume, nothing if none qualifies. Confirmed-but-not-defects (documented honest limits): the `AbioticField` closed Light/Water/Soil enum (a real Terran residual, the field SET is Earth's growth triad, resolved by Arc 5's data-defined field registry), the `terran_default` Earth-named split (the overridable unconfigured default), the split's fraction axis unvalidated (code stays mass-exact). One doc corrected (`depletes` inert on Light).

> DERIVABILITY FINDING (owner asked whether the food-VALUE and the T2/T3/T5 reserved values are real gates or derivable). The metabolism is HALF-GROUNDED: the DRAIN side is physical (`physiology::derive_base_drain` composes Kleiber basal + thermoregulatory heat loss, and the reserve's stored energy is `capacity * body_mass_kg * bio.energy_density`), but the INTAKE side (`Embodiment::geophage`, the locomotion INGEST) uses `laws::satisfaction * capacity` and NEVER reads the food's own `bio.energy_density`. So the food-VALUE gate is NOT a real authored value: eating should replenish the reserve by the food's PHYSICAL energy content, `food_mass (= food_volume * mat.density) * food bio.energy_density * assimilation`, exactly the axes the drain already uses and that plants already declare, dissolving `biomass_per_stock` and the food-value fudge (this is the deferred R-PHYS-BIO edibility measure the physiology arc flagged). What REMAINS the owner's to author are physics/biology CONSTANTS (the Kleiber coefficient `kleiber_a`, the ecological/assimilation trophic efficiency `ingest_efficiency`, the rock-weathering and decomposition kinetics) plus R-UNITS-PIN (the absolute kJ/g-to-joule reserve reconciliation, already a flagged owner calibration). NEXT WORK recommended: ground the intake in `bio.energy_density * mass` (the food-value derivation), which dissolves the B owner-gate, before or alongside the Cluster-I arcs 5/6/7.

## 2026-07-07 (deep night, cont. 6, ULTRACODE ON): BIOSPHERE DIRECTIVE 2 first slice landed (organisms as usable material stuff), directives 1/2/3 now all complete; CPU/GPU interleave design workflow in flight

Biosphere directive 2 (organisms as usable MATERIAL STUFF) landed its first slice (`5038ddc`), from the scored `biosphere-world-material-bridge` design workflow (17 agents, three-driver panel: mint-a-substance vs composition-as-mix vs tissue-as-field). The winner was TISSUE-AS-FIELD, chosen because it needs ZERO growth of the physics substrate: it never calls `reg.substance(id)` for tissue, so no `add_substance` capability (the mint route would have needed that owner-sign-off gate). The panel also caught, verified against source, that the mint route's "eating just works" claim is FALSE (edibility reads a per-race `assimilation`/`requirement` table keyed by literal substance id, never the substance vector), which would have forced an authored per-species-id entry, the closed lookup Principle 8/9 forbids.

Built: (1) `material.rs` `TissueParcel { volume, composition }` and `TissueField`, a sibling of `SoilNutrientField` keyed by CONTENT identity (the sorted composition itself, collision-free, not a hash) with the empty-default-folds-nothing discipline. (2) `physiology.rs` `whole_body_composition_vector`, generalizing `body_density`/`whole_body_energy_density` from one axis to the UNION of every axis any part declares, a development-weighted mean over organs (via `organ_composition`) plus covering and weapons (via `KindDef::material`, NOT `organ_composition`, which would alias their kind id onto an organ, the seam a judge flagged); its `mat.density` equals `body_density` on an organs-only body (the special case it subsumes). (3) `runner.rs` `Embodiment.tissue` (folded into `state_hash` only when non-empty), `set_tissue`, the default-off `set_corpse_matter` flag, and a death hook in `reconcile_lifecycle` that derives the dying being's own composition from its INDIVIDUAL `Walker.body` (preserving per-individual variation) and deposits it where it fell, read before `retire_body` removes the walker; the one consumer touch, `extract_underfoot`'s fracture gate `.max(tissue.fracture_hardness(coord))`, so a corpse is worked by the SAME contest and axis as rock.

Wired demo (`determinism_harness`, directive 1): a culled being leaves its own flesh as located matter (fracture hardness DERIVED from the body, not authored), off deposits nothing (byte-neutral opt-in), the deposit is deterministic. Plus unit tests for the composition-vector generalization and the tissue field's content accumulation and order-free hash. The FLIP NOTE (directive 1) is documented in `docs/working/BIOSPHERE_WIRING_NOTES.md`: both seams are one `Runner` setter (`set_decomposer` / `set_corpse_matter`), off on canonical, and they COMPOSE (a directive-2 corpse is exactly the organic matter directive 3 decomposes; a generated decomposer species is both the Life kernel's biomass source and an organism made of material stuff).

So the three biosphere directives are all landed: 1 (opt-in + wired demo + flip note), 2 (organisms as material stuff, first slice), 3 (decomposition-as-emergence). Reserved as data awaiting owner sign-off (surfaced, not fabricated): the tissue density/ash/barrier on each anatomy kind, and the corpse volume derivation (body-size trait now, mass-over-density once tissue density lands). Follow-on slices (flagged, not forced): eating tissue (route through the composition vector, not the substance-id table), cutting and the matter cycle onto the tissue field, and filling the field from the generated biosphere rather than a death alone (the rest of task 21). State: sim 908 (up from 904), clippy clean, `run_world` `a465919e`.

IN FLIGHT: the `gpu-cpu-interleave-design` workflow (how GPU and CPU workloads interleave within/across the deterministic tick, adversarially verified determinism-safe) is running (verify/synthesis phase). When it returns, fold its synthesis into `docs/working/GPU_BLUEPRINT.md` as the CPU/GPU interleaving section. The owner asked for that plan and for confirmation the Rayon determinism holds (verified: bit-identical hash at thread counts 1,2,3,4,6,8,16,32; no thread-identity reads; the guarantee extends across the async GPU boundary because the logical consume-order is fixed).

## 2026-07-07 (deep night, cont. 5, ULTRACODE ON): the decomposition BLIND AUDIT returned and its findings were HARDENED; heterogeneous-core question answered

The repo-walled blind audit of the decomposition-as-emergence mechanism (pilot plus six adversarial lens auditors: byte-neutrality, determinism, emergence, mass-conservation, overflow, reserved-discipline) completed. Byte-neutrality and determinism verified HOLD (the determinism auditor proved the combine is order-independent because every per-row contribution is clamped to `[0,1]` before combining). Mass-conservation came back UNCERTAIN only because the packet truncated the unchanged `m_before`/`m_after` math the ConservationRegistry test already guards, verified holding against source. Three real seams were caught and HARDENED (`b705415`):

1. EMERGENCE (the critical one, caught on two independent angles by the emergence lens and the pilot). The additive-clamped combine ("activities add") let a Conditions row swallow the Life kernel's zero-at-sterility signal when both were armed: a sterile warm-wet-oxic cell decayed at the full rate through the Conditions term (and `dev_fixture` ships both rows), contradicting the module's own sterile guarantee and relocating the banned universal into "all warm-wet-oxic matter rots." Fixed by making the cross-row combine DATA (Principle 11, the harden-into-a-registry discipline): a new `CombineMode { All, Any }`. `All` (the default) takes the MINIMUM so a Life row gates a favorable Conditions row and a sterile cell preserves (decomposer life gated by conditions); `Any` takes the MAXIMUM (biotic OR abiotic decay, the world's explicit choice). The combine can no longer author the OR regime. Min/max over `[0,1]` contributions stay order-independent, so determinism holds.
2. OVERFLOW (medium). The warmth term `temperature - barrier` used a plain Fixed subtraction that panics on i64 overflow for extreme pure-function inputs under the release overflow checks. Replaced with a saturating bit-level subtraction, the exact idiom `organic_salience` uses for its negation.
3. RESERVED-DISCIPLINE (medium). Documented the moisture/oxygen `ONE` fallback as the "unmodelled axis is non-limiting" convention (the open-air convention the combustion beat already uses for its respirable read), a structural boundary of the saturating response, not a fabricated tuned value.

The falsifier gained the decisive both-armed case (case 6): under `All` a sterile warm-wet cell preserves, under `Any` it decays. State: sim 905, clippy clean, `run_world` `a465919e`. This is the "audit the input" discipline working: the panel's critical finding became a data-defined combine mode that strengthens the emergence guarantee.

The owner also asked whether the Rayon arc can classify and use Intel P/E cores and AMD X3D (V-Cache) cores, or whether a box with them is needed. Answered and recorded in `docs/working/GPU_BLUEPRINT.md`: Rayon is topology-agnostic and does not need to classify cores for correctness, because every parallel loop keys its RNG on a `DrawKey` (item id, never thread) and writes fixed positions, so core placement moves wall-clock but never the hash (the `RAYON_NUM_THREADS` gate proves this on any topology). Work-stealing absorbs the speed asymmetry dynamically. Classifying cores (a two-pool split pinning the cache-bound field/matter steps to V-Cache or P cores via `hwlocality` plus `core_affinity`) is an optional, determinism-safe PERFORMANCE tuning for later, needing such a box only to benchmark, not to build.

IN FLIGHT: the directive-2 design workflow (`biosphere-world-material-bridge`, organisms as usable material stuff, wiring `biosphere.rs` `Species` into the world material field, derived not authored) is running (map and design phases done, judge and synthesis pending). When it returns, verify the synthesis against source (especially the physics-registry substance-minting gate it will surface), then build the first byte-neutral slice, then directive 1 (opt-in + wired demo + flip note).

---

## 2026-07-07 (deep night, cont. 4, ULTRACODE ON): ARC 4 RAYON completed, and BIOSPHERE DIRECTIVE 3 landed (decomposition-as-emergence), both byte-neutral

Two arcs advanced. First, arc 4's deterministic Rayon parallelization finished: the six per-being pre-compute maps in `Runner::step_embodiment` (field_dirs, field_signed, appetitive, attraction, discovery_choices, drains) now iterate with `par_iter`, joining the five hot loops parallelized last session (Field::step, GenePool::drift/found, evolve_with, phase_body_exchange, WorldGenesis::step_once). Each map produces its entries from pure reads of immutable shared state, keyed by the being's `StableId`, collected into an order-free `BTreeMap`. The one map that draws randomness, `discovery_choices`, was verified safe first: `sample_candidate` keys its RNG purely on `DrawKey::entity(w.id, tick, HYPOTHESIZE).rng(seed)`, a counter-keyed stream independent of iteration order that mutates no shared state, so the draw replays bit-for-bit. Verified bit-identical: `run_world` `a465919e` at `RAYON_NUM_THREADS` 1 and 8. Commit `14397b7`.

Second, biosphere directive 3, DECOMPOSITION-AS-EMERGENCE (Principle 8), the largest of the biosphere seams, is built and falsifier-backed. The design came from the completed `biosphere-decomposition-emergence` workflow (17 agents, three-driver scored panel: conditions-proxy vs decomposer-life vs hybrid). The panel's binding constraint was emergence: every panelist across all three designs converged on the same disqualifier for the conditions-only path, that it relocates the banned universal ("all warm matter rots" becomes "all warm-wet-oxic matter rots") and cannot express the litmus case (a sterile-but-favorable world must NOT decay). The winner was the hybrid `DecomposerDriverRegistry` (a data-defined extensible substrate, the project's own hardening discipline), with the emergence crown grafted from the life design and the conditions terms grafted from the conditions design.

Built (`c72b26d`, `0012d51`): a new `crates/sim/src/decompose.rs` substrate. `DecomposerKernelId { Conditions, Life }` is a fixed kernel set (the accepted `TransformKernelId`/`CapabilityKernel` shape: mechanism fixed Rust, membership and params data, Principle 11). The Conditions kernel is the abiotic microbial proxy (a Liebig minimum over moisture, oxygen, and warmth above the substance's own `bio.decomposition_barrier`), so a dry, airless, or barely-thawed cell preserves its matter. The Life kernel is the emergent branch: a saturating response on the per-cell standing decomposer biomass (`DecomposerStockField`, structured like `SoilNutrientField`), zero at sterility, so a warm, wet, airy but STERILE cell does not rot (the case the abiotic proxy cannot express). A world arms either kernel or both, so neither is an engine universal. The seam is one multiplicative factor: `step_matter_cycle`'s `decomposed = volume.checked_mul(rate)` gains `.and_then(|d| d.checked_mul(factor))` where `factor = self.decomposer.map(activity_at).unwrap_or(ONE)`, and `trace::organic_salience` gains a pure `decomposer_activity` argument on the same rate. The physics barrier gate and the exact `m_before`/`m_after`/`mass_lost` conservation math are UNTOUCHED, so falsifiability-by-physics survives and mass conserves bit-for-bit. Opt-in on two separate `Runner` fields (`decomposer`, `decomposer_stock`), both `None` by default and folded only when non-empty, so no existing struct literal breaks and `run_world` stays `a465919e`.

Two INPUT-AUDIT catches on the workflow's synthesis (the "audit the input" discipline): (1) the synthesis named the Life reserved value `biomass_half_saturation`, but its own basis and formula (activity reaches ONE at full colonisation) describe a linear-saturation reference, which a Michaelis-Menten half-saturation constant cannot deliver (it only reaches one half there); renamed `biomass_reference` to match the mechanism. (2) The synthesis wanted fail-loud `[[reserved]]` sentinels, but the sibling matter-cycle and trace params use labelled dev-fixtures plus a documenting comment block; followed the sibling convention (a comment block in `calibration/reserved.toml` surfacing all four values with basis), and wired the warmth term now so no reserved value is a dead sentinel.

Falsifier: `decomposition_is_driven_by_life_and_conditions_not_by_an_engine_law` (the decisive sterile-vs-colonised case, the frozen physics gate, the conditions warmth wiring, the unarmed control, and mass conservation) plus six `decompose` unit tests and the trace decomposer-activity gate. State: sim 904 (up from 896), clippy clean, `run_world` `a465919e` unchanged. Reserved (surfaced with basis, none set): `decompose.conditions.{moisture_saturation, oxygen_reference, warmth_span}`, `decompose.life.biomass_reference`.

IN FLIGHT: a repo-walled blind audit of the decomposition mechanism is running (pilot + six adversarial lens auditors: byte-neutrality, determinism, emergence/steering, mass-conservation, overflow/saturation, reserved-discipline). When it returns, verify every finding against source and apply real hardenings.

NEXT (the remaining biosphere directives, which together are task 21): directive 2, organisms as usable MATERIAL STUFF (a generated species composed of forageable/manipulable/eatable/cookable physics substances, which is also the deferred SOURCE of the Life kernel's decomposer biomass stock); directive 1, biosphere opt-in plus a REAL wired demo with an easily-accessible flip note. These wire the generated ecology (`crates/sim/src/biosphere.rs` `Species`) into the running world, which has zero biosphere references today.

## 2026-07-07 (deep night, cont. 3, ULTRACODE ON): the TOOL ACTION SET completed (CRUSH, STRIKE + GATE 2), and TWO design WORKFLOWS launched for the biosphere and GPU arcs

The owner turned ULTRACODE on ("power through it all") and I am now orchestrating multi-agent workflows for the design-heavy arcs while implementing in the main loop. The made-world tool action set is now RICH and complete for the buildable-derivable scope, every action byte-neutral (`run_world` `a465919e`), falsifier-backed, derived from physics:
- CRUSH (`465cbc7`): the compressive-failure action, `CapabilityKernel::Crush` + `crush_underfoot` on `mat.compressive_strength`, the same tool crushes a compression-weak and cuts a shear-weak constituent.
- GATE 1 (tool body geometry) + BUCKLING: a tool carries a `length` (cross-section = `volume/length`), and `break_check` buckles a slender tool under its axial working load (`laws::euler_buckle`).
- STRIKE / POUND (percussion, the MASS payoff): `strike_underfoot` swings the tool and its kinetic energy (`laws::kinetic_energy`, `1/2 m v^2` over the tool's own mass) fractures matter whose Griffith energy the blow exceeds (`fracture_onset`'s energy limb; the delivered energy scaled kJ->J). The AFFORDANCE is `CapabilityKernel::Impact` (`ID_IMPACT`) reading the tool mass exposed to the capability closure as `mech.mass` (GATE 2 DONE); the `POUND` affordance (id 12, named to avoid the body-weapon `STRIKE` id 2) is gated on it. A HEAVY tool shatters rock a light one of the same shape cannot. Falsifiers: enact, compose kernel, affordance-grant.
So the tool substrate now holds: extract, cut (shear), crush (compression), strike (percussion), plus the failures wear (Archard), breakage (brittle fracture), and buckling (Euler); and the capability kernels Pierce, Shear, Crush, Impact (plus Locomote, Refract). State: sim 896, physics 154, compose 27, clippy clean.

REMAINING tool actions are substrate-gated (marked in the plan's gate-map): LEVER/pry (needs a fulcrum/load-arm model), ABRADE, BORE (GATE 4 hertzian), the DYNAMIC toughness limb of breakage, and the deep sections H (composite/hafted) and I (identity/thermal). These are marked, not forced.

IN FLIGHT (two background WORKFLOWS, ultracode): (1) `biosphere-decomposition-emergence` maps the authored decay substrate and runs a scored design panel over three drivers (conditions-proxy / decomposer-life / hybrid) to design decomposition-as-emergence (directive 3); (2) `gpu-blueprint-and-rayon-scan` maps the sim hot loops and designs the GPU-integration blueprint plus an adversarially-verified Rayon parallelization plan (arc 4). When they return I implement the syntheses (with adversarial verification), then the remaining biosphere directives (2 organisms-as-material-stuff, 1 wired demo + flip note). `SEQ_FIELD_BITS` (CUT 10, CRUSH 11, POUND 12, runway to 15) and the `plan_chain` simple-path bound still come due.

## 2026-07-07 (deep night, cont.): ARC 3 BUILDABLE-DERIVABLE SCOPE COMPLETE: R-CUT-SHEAR + three-panel blind AUDIT + CRUSH + the substrate-expansion GATE-MAP. Owner: "continue the whole plan, audits after each arc, mark where the substrate needs expansion, and ideally get the physics substrate in too."

The owner sharpened the mandate: continue through the whole plan, audit after each ARC (not each section), and when a tool action cannot be DERIVED from the current physics substrate, MARK where the substrate must grow rather than stalling (and expand the substrate where possible). Under that mandate the buildable-without-substrate-expansion scope of arc 3 is now complete and audited. Everything below is byte-neutral (`run_world` `a465919e` unchanged), falsifier-backed, and derived from physics.

- R-CUT-SHEAR (`e9fa4d4`): the cut ENACT now severs by SHEAR, retiring the normal-stress `fracture_strength` proxy. `cut_underfoot` drives a shear stress over the edge's contact area (`laws::shear`), self-limited at the tool's own `mat.shear_strength` (von Mises from yield where silent), and severs each constituent whose own shear strength the deliverable shear beats. The cut/wear/breakage enact floors gained `mat.shear_strength`; the cut falsifier isolates the change with a `fibre` weak in fracture but tough in shear that the shear cut leaves. So the cut is now shear end to end: it AFFORDS via the SHEAR kernel (F) and ENACTS via the shear contest.
- THREE-PANEL BLIND AUDIT of D/E/F/R-CUT-SHEAR (`a5fc10e`): three repo-walled auditors (determinism/overflow, physics correctness, fresh adversarial) over a self-contained packet (`scratchpad/arc3_audit_packet.md`). All three independently VERIFIED the shear self-limit algebra correct and found no fabricated constants. Every finding was checked against source before acting (one packet premise about `sat_sub` was itself wrong and corrected before the panels ran, the "audit the input" catch). Verified: the physics laws (`contact_pressure`, `shear`, `fracture_onset`) are all checked_div/checked_mul and SATURATE to their cap (never wrap); `SubstanceMix` is a BTreeMap (canonical walk); `run_world` is byte-identical and the cut's opt-in IS the CUT affordance (afford and enact are shear-consistent), so the "silent overflow" and "no opt-in / byte-identity broken" CRITICALs were overstated. HARDENINGS applied, byte-neutral: `STRESS_GUARD` (1e9 MPa, a named overflow guard clear of the Q32.32 edge) replaces the looser `Fixed::MAX` cap in `cut_underfoot`/`break_check`; `break_check`'s zero delivered energy reframed as the QUASI-STATIC working-stroke limit (a press/draw delivers no kinetic fracture energy, so the Griffith energy limb is correctly inert) rather than an authored disabling value; the tool-absence and target-absence conventions documented (tool with no strength = not judged, matching wear/break; target with no strength = yields, matching the extract Principle-8 convention); `parse_scale_factor` handles a negative-exponent storage scale.
- CRUSH, Section G (`465cbc7`): the second non-piercing action, compressive failure. `CapabilityKernel::Crush` (`ID_CRUSH`, compose) + `crush_underfoot` gating per constituent on `mat.compressive_strength`, afforded via `dev_crusher` (`CRUSH` id 11), wired into the dispatch with breakage-before-wear. Reserved: `reference_compressive_resistance`. Falsifier `a_crush_fails_compression_where_a_cut_parts_shear_the_same_tool_diverging_by_the_targets_axis`: the SAME tool crushes a compression-weak chalk and cuts a shear-weak fibre, the opposite outcome from one geometry and force, diverging only on the target's own axes. So the non-piercing action space now holds two kernels (shear + crush) and two enacts.
- The SUBSTRATE-EXPANSION GATE-MAP (`fc004d1`, in `docs/working/TOOL_SUBSTRATE_PLAN.md`): the six places the tool work reaches the edge of the current physics substrate and needs it to grow, ordered by leverage: GATE 1 tool BODY GEOMETRY (`WieldedTool` carries no length/cross-section/section-modulus; deriving them at craft unblocks E's toughness limb, bend/buckle failures, LEVER, and STRIKE's energy limb, the completion of root R2); GATE 2 tool MASS in the capability closure (for STRIKE percussion); GATE 3 matter FORM/STATE (a cell's `SubstanceMix` is bulk, so transformative/percussive/abrasive actions cannot represent their outcome or a struck/removal geometry without a matter form-state axis; gates STRIKE target-mass, ABRADE amount, R-CUT-DEPTH depth-sizing); GATE 4 hertzian contact for BORE; GATE 5 tool object identity + thermal wiring (Section I, the thermal laws exist); GATE 6 composite/hafted tools (Section H).

STOPPED after CRUSH + the gate-map, all committed and pushed. State: sim 894, physics 154, compose 26 green, clippy clean, byte-neutral `a465919e`. NEXT, per the owner's mandate ("get the physics substrate in as well if possible"): GATE 1 (tool body geometry at craft) is the highest-leverage substrate expansion, unblocking E's toughness limb and STRIKE/LEVER; then STRIKE (percussion via `kinetic_energy` over the tool mass, GATE 2) and LEVER (`laws::lever` over the tool's arm); then H (composite/hafted) and I (identity/thermal). Then the BIOSPHERE arc (task 21, `docs/working/BIOSPHERE_WIRING_NOTES.md`), whose directive 3 (decomposition as EMERGENCE) is the cleanest entry: the decay is already a data-defined transform substrate gated on a thermal-activation barrier (`crates/sim/src/trace.rs`), so the remaining work is making the decay RATE depend on decomposer LIFE or world conditions (moisture/oxygen/microbial proxy) rather than a universal authored rate, so a sterile/frozen/dry world does not decay. Then arc 4 (GPU blueprint + Rayon). Standing bounds still coming due: `SEQ_FIELD_BITS` (16-value belief-subject packing; CUT is 10, CRUSH is 11, runway to 15) and the `plan_chain` simple-path enumeration.

## 2026-07-07 (deep night): ARC 3 SECTIONS D (WEAR), E (BREAKAGE), F (SHEAR KERNEL, root R1) landed, each derived-from-physics, opt-in, byte-neutral, falsifier-backed

Continued arc 3 through the tool-life-cycle physics. Three sections landed, each byte-neutral (`run_world` `a465919e` unchanged), fully derived from the physics substrate (the owner's absolute rule held as the standing audit seat), and falsifier-backed. The tool is now MATTER that wears, breaks, and (as an affordance) shears.

- SECTION D, tool WEAR (`b16608a`): `Embodiment::wear_tool` wears the WIELDED tool by one use via the Archard law (`laws::wear`), the coefficient the tool material's own `mat.wear_coefficient`, the being's force, the stroke distance, the tool's own hardness. Retained volume drops; a tool worn below the craft-minimum is a spent nub and is unwielded (the make-use-wear-remake lifecycle). Wired into CUT and EXTRACT gated on positive work; no-op for a bare-handed extract and when unarmed. The Archard coefficient's STORAGE SCALE is DERIVED, not fabricated: new `QuantityAxis::storage_scale` (crates/physics/src/lib.rs) parses the canonical `mat.wear_coefficient` `x1e6` convention, and `wear_tool` divides it back out (a plain unit label reports scale one). Reserved-with-basis: `WearParams` (stroke distance, worn-volume ceiling), sibling to Craft/Extraction params, dev fixture stand-in. Falsifier `a_worked_tool_wears_down_and_spends_out_over_repeated_use_and_an_unarmed_tool_is_immortal`. Physics test for `storage_scale`.
- SECTION E, tool BREAKAGE (`78bbd9a`): `Embodiment::break_check` fractures the WIELDED tool when the reaction stress of its own working force over its edge (`laws::contact_pressure`) exceeds the tool material's own `mat.fracture_strength` (the brittle-fracture criterion `laws::fracture_onset`), unwielding it. The SUDDEN failure complementing wear's gradual one, and the hardness-versus-fracture-strength tradeoff biting: a hard edge that imposes a high cutting stress must survive it. Wired into CUT and EXTRACT, checked BEFORE wear (sudden before gradual). Opt-in via `set_breakage(bool)`; NO reserved value (fully derived). The energy/toughness limb of `fracture_onset` rides inert (delivered fracture energy zero) because it needs the tool's own crack cross-section (a tool-geometry extension); documented. Falsifier `a_brittle_tool_snaps_under_its_own_working_stress_where_a_tough_one_survives_and_an_unarmed_tool_never_breaks`.
- SECTION F, the SHEAR KERNEL, root R1 OPENED (`80fe097`): the `CapabilityKernel` enum is no longer closed at Pierce. `CapabilityKernel::Shear` (crates/compose/src/capability.rs) reads `mech.contact_area` + `mat.shear_strength`/`mat.yield_strength`: an edge drives the reference force over its contact area as a shear stress (`laws::shear`), self-limited at the part's own shear strength (von Mises from yield where the axis is silent), and if that effective shear clears the reserved reference resistance it severs, graded above the threshold. Registered as `ID_SHEAR` in the dev seed, so an `AffordanceDef` can now require a non-piercing law. Its CONSUMER: `homeostasis::dev_cutter` now gates CUT on `ID_SHEAR`, retiring the earlier PIERCE (normal-penetration) proxy (a cut is a shear-parting action). `CapabilityRefs` gains `reference_shear_resistance` (reserved-with-basis, dev fixture 3 MPa). Compose falsifier `a_keen_strong_edge_reads_a_shear_capability_a_blunt_or_weak_or_ductileless_one_does_not`. The SEVER THRESHOLD was found by a test: without it a tiny-area weak body read a spurious positive shear (the morphogen inert-body viability test caught it), so the kernel now requires clearing the reference, which is also the physically correct sever gate.

STOPPED after Section F, all committed and pushed. Full state: sim 893, physics 154, compose 25 green, clippy clean, byte-neutral `a465919e`. NEXT, per `TOOL_SUBSTRATE_PLAN.md`: the cut ENACT sever gate refinement R-CUT-SHEAR (fold the sever contest onto the shear physics: each constituent's `mat.shear_strength` and the tool's own, retiring the `fracture_strength` proxy) is the next natural fold, but it RIPPLES the cut/wear/breakage enact falsifiers (their tools and target floors must declare shear strength), so it needs a deliberate floor rewrite; Section G (new derived actions: strike/percussion via `impact`/`kinetic_energy` over the tool's mass, lever/pry via `lever`, crush via compressive strength, abrade, bore, each with its own kernel added as its consumer lands, the "capability lands when read" discipline); R-CUT-DEPTH (depth-sized cut via `cut_penetrate` + `mat.specific_cut_energy`); H (composite/hafted tools, the tool carrying a body cross-section and length, which also unblocks E's toughness limb and the bend/buckle failures); I (identity/provenance/thermal, quench raising hardness). Then the arc-END deep blind audit (physics-only seat) over the whole reworked tool substrate. Then the BIOSPHERE wiring (task 21) and arc 4 (GPU + Rayon). Standing bounds still coming due: `SEQ_FIELD_BITS` (16-value belief-subject packing; CUT is id 10) and the `plan_chain` simple-path enumeration.

## 2026-07-07 (night): ARC 3 TOOL-USE AUTHORING PURGED and DERIVED FROM PHYSICS (Sections A, B, C + checkpoint audit); the owner set the absolute rule "the physics substrate is the ONLY authored place, everything else derived, no exceptions"

The owner sharpened arc 3 to its hard core: the physics substrate (material axes + laws) is the ONLY place authored data may live; everything a tool does, produces, or becomes MUST be derived from it, no exceptions. This is now a STANDING AUDIT SEAT on every panel. A SIX-AGENT completely-blind sweep (three authoring lenses fed only the tool surface, three gap lenses, each told to loop until saturated) produced the complete list, now in `docs/working/TOOL_SUBSTRATE_PLAN.md`: two authoring violations (the cut's output and the crafted edge) and two structural roots (`WieldedTool` too thin to carry mass/wear/geometry/identity; the `CapabilityKernel` enum closed at Pierce, so no non-piercing action is expressible), decomposed into derive-from-physics Sections A through I.

LANDED this session, each byte-neutral (run_world `a465919e` and crucible unchanged), falsifier-backed, and checkpoint-audited:
- SLICE 1a, afford-from-tool (`dbf8f5d`): a wielded tool's capability enters the SAME affordance derivation a body part's does (`affordance_percept::tool_capability`, `homeostasis::{CUT id 10, dev_cutter, tool_affordances}`, `runner::being_afforded`, opt-in `set_tool_affordances`), so a keen edge grants a CUT the barehanded forager lacks. Unit-proven, self-audited.
- SECTION A, the derived cut (`a9eb09f`): PURGED the authored `cut_yields` map (it authored WHICH targets are cut-openable, HOW MUCH is freed, WHICH substance appears). `cut_underfoot` now gates PER CONSTITUENT of the cell's own `SubstanceMix` (severs each substance whose OWN `mat.fracture_strength` the edge beats), frees the strength-bounded carry (the grasp's bound), no table, no fraction, no deposit. Distinct from EXTRACT's aggregate gate: a keen edge frees the soft flesh from a tough composite EXTRACT cannot (falsifier `a_cut_frees_the_soft_constituent_of_a_composite_a_bare_extract_cannot_and_authors_nothing`).
- SECTION B, the derived craft edge (`eb6012c`): PURGED the authored `edge_area` constant. New law `civsim_physics::laws::edge_area_at` (the inverse of `contact_pressure`, staged to avoid fixed-point overflow) derives the finest edge from the worked stone's own `mat.fracture_strength`, so a hard stone makes a sharp tool and a soft one a blunt tool; the fittest stone is chosen by the derived cutting power `min(fracture, hardness)` (falsifier `a_crafted_edge_is_derived_from_the_worked_stone...`). Craft now needs the material registry and physiology (a being knaps with its muscle).
- SECTION C, the tool as matter (`48a2bc4`): `WieldedTool` retains its `volume`, so `mass = density * volume` is recoverable (`WieldedTool::mass`, the defect the gap agents flagged: the struct claimed mass was derived but discarded the volume). The enabler for wear (D), breakage (E), impact (G).
- CHECKPOINT AUDIT FIXES (`9f34cab`): a two-agent blind panel (physics-only seat + correctness; determinism + crash) confirmed the two CRITICAL authoring defects truly fixed and determinism/byte-neutrality intact, and drove three fixes: `mass` uses `checked_mul` (was an unchecked mul, a debug panic / release wrap and cross-build hazard); craft fitness ranks by `min(fracture, hardness)` not an `argmax` over Pierce (which mis-ranked a cutter and authored "this is a piercer", and is precision-safe against the tiny-area cliff); the cut's pressure cap is now a `Fixed::MAX` overflow guard, not a `pressure_max` borrowed from `ExtractionParams`, decoupling cut from extraction.

THREE PHYSICS REFINEMENTS the checkpoint surfaced, real and NOT yet fixed (they need the deeper substrate, recorded in the plan): R-EDGE-INTRINSIC (the edge is derived from the CRAFTER's force, so at use the force cancels and cutting is force-INSENSITIVE and maker-dependent; the correct edge is an INTRINSIC material length scale, a new axis, so `force/area` stays a real function of the wielder's force); R-CUT-DEPTH (the cut is carry-bounded, not depth-sized; needs `cut_penetrate` + a `mat.specific_cut_energy` axis); R-CUT-SHEAR (the sever gate is a normal-stress threshold against `fracture_strength`, but cutting is shear; needs a shear capability kernel, root R1). These are honest limits: A/B/C purged the AUTHORING and stood up the tool-as-matter foundation, and the derivations are sound as far as they go, but the edge is force-derived rather than intrinsic, the cut amount is carry-bounded rather than depth-sized, and the sever gate is normal rather than shear.

STOPPED after the checkpoint audit + fixes, all committed and pushed. Full state: 891 sim + 84 physics tests green, fmt/clippy clean, byte-neutral. NEXT, per `TOOL_SUBSTRATE_PLAN.md` (dependency order): Section F (the CapabilityKernel expansion, root R1: add shear/impact/lever/wear/bend/friction kernels so non-piercing affordances are expressible) unblocks the sharpest refinements; Section D (wear, the `wear` law over the tool's volume, "the sharpest single gap"); E (breakage); G (new derived actions: sever via shear, strike via impact over mass, lever, crush, abrade, bore); the three refinements R-EDGE-INTRINSIC / R-CUT-DEPTH / R-CUT-SHEAR fold into B / D-G / F; H (composite/hafted tools); I (identity/thermal, quench raising hardness). Then the BIOSPHERE wiring (task 21, `docs/working/BIOSPHERE_WIRING_NOTES.md`): organisms made of usable material STUFF (people mostly done), opt-in plus a real wired demo with a flip note, and DECOMPOSITION AS EMERGENCE (no authored "matter decays always" law, decay driven by decomposer life or world-specific/evolved conditions). Then arc 4 (GPU blueprint + Rayon). Standing bounds coming due as arc 3 densifies: `SEQ_FIELD_BITS` (16-value belief-subject packing) and the `plan_chain` simple-path enumeration.

## 2026-07-07 (later still): ARC 3 (THE MADE WORLD) OPENED; slice 1a (AFFORD-FROM-TOOL) landed; owner set the biosphere-wiring directives; slice 1b (the CUT enact) designed and ready

Opened ARC 3, the made world (matter that affords a USE). Grounded the tool machinery (the wielded-tool substrate, the affordance-percept set, the fracture/extraction contest, the geophage byproduct-deposit hook, the run-path afford and enact sites); the ground map is in the session transcript and drove the slice plan.

SLICE 1a LANDED (committed `dbf8f5d`, pushed): AFFORD-FROM-TOOL, the honest core of "matter affords a use." A wielded tool's capability now enters the SAME affordance derivation a body part's does, so a keen edge grants a CUT the barehanded forager lacks. This fills the flagged gap the grounding surfaced (affordance was purely body-derived; the tool only tweaked outcomes). Mechanism: `affordance_percept::tool_capability` (the tool's capability on any law, derived from its contact area plus substance hardness on the same `derive_capabilities` dispatch `body_capability` uses, a keen edge reads PIERCE, no `IsAxe` tag); `homeostasis`: `CUT` (id 10, capability-gated on PIERCE which a forager body does not read), `dev_cutter` fixture, `AffordanceRegistry::tool_affordances` (a closure-gated union; an unconditional affordance is never tool-granted); `runner`: `Embodiment::being_afforded` unions the tool's granted affordances into the body's, opt-in `set_tool_affordances` (off by default), inert for a toolless being, both afford consumers routed through it. Falsifiers: a keen edge reads a positive PIERCE a body lacks (`affordance_percept`), a tool that reads the required capability grants CUT and never an unconditional one (`homeostasis`). Byte-neutral (`run_world` `a465919e` and crucible unchanged, 694 lib + 888 integration green, fmt/clippy clean). No fabricated value (CUT's `min_capability` is ZERO like STRIKE's). Focused self-audit (this is the afford path, not the flagged deep planner/appetitive spot): byte-neutrality, determinism (registry-order Vec union, pure `tool_capability`), emergence line (tool grants CUT by its own physics, membership is data), all clean; both afford consumers use `being_afforded`, no conflicting afford site.

FINDING flagged for slice 1b: the Pierce/Sharpness kernel credits a tiny-area edge with MAX pierce whatever its material hardness (zero hardness = zero resistance = max penetration), so "sharpness" at the AFFORD gate is edge GEOMETRY, not hardness. The material-hardness distinction (a keen granite edge cuts where a soft point of the same geometry deforms) is therefore the cut's EFFECTIVENESS, gated in the ENACT, not the afford. Good news: the extraction contest already has exactly this gate (`extract_underfoot`: `effective = contact_pressure(force, tool.area, pressure_max).min(tool_hardness)` vs the target's fracture resistance, so a SOFT tool blunts to its own low hardness and cannot exceed a hard target's resistance). Slice 1b reuses it.

SLICE 1b DESIGNED, READY TO BUILD (the CUT enact, what a sharp tool DOES): add `CUT` to the two `matter_primitive` guards (runner.rs ~3939, ~3991) and a `CUT => { emb.cut_underfoot(id); }` arm to the enact dispatch (~4032). `Embodiment::cut_underfoot(walker_id)` mirrors `extract_underfoot`'s contest but (a) REQUIRES a wielded edge (a cut needs a tool; a bare being does not afford CUT), and (b) on success RELEASES an inner substance rather than taking the target: for each present target substance in an opt-in `cut_yields: BTreeMap<String,(String,Fixed)>` (target to (inner substance, fraction), the mirror of `byproducts`), if the tool's `effective` edge pressure exceeds that target's own cut-resistance (its `mat.fracture_strength`), take a fraction of the target and DEPOSIT the released inner substance in the cell. Opt-in via `set_cut` (`cut_yields` empty by default, so no cut releases anything and every world is byte-identical; CUT is only afforded in a `dev_cutter` world with tool affordances armed). Reuse `self.extraction`'s `pressure_max` for the contest cap (a cut world arms the extraction contest params, the shared contact-pressure physics), or a minimal `CutParams` if keeping CUT independent of the extraction fixture is preferred (owner-neutral call; reuse is simpler). FALSIFIER (end-to-end through the runner, the natural home now that CUT does something): a being wielding a keen HARD tool cuts a rind-like target and its inner food appears in the cell; a being wielding a SOFT tool (blunts below the target's resistance) or no tool releases nothing; byte-neutral opt-in. Then a DEEP blind audit (this is run-path-consequential and adjacent to the reward/eligibility credit the owner flagged, so it earns the full three-skeptic panel). Then slice 1c: form the relation `(cut-action, YIELDS, released-food)` or `(strike, YIELDS, sharp-thing)` on the run path (the FIRST run-path `consider_relation` call, in `couple_conversation` beside the nutrition credit, respecting enact-then-credit-then-world.tick order) so arc 2's multi-hop planner chains tool-making to tool-use, the tool-reasoning payoff.

OWNER DIRECTIVES for the BIOSPHERE arc (task 21, after arc 3), captured in `docs/working/BIOSPHERE_WIRING_NOTES.md`: (1) build opt-in plus a REAL wired demo (a worldgen path composing the pre-dawn biosphere epoch, dawn, play), leaving the canonical hashes intact, with an EASILY-ACCESSIBLE note on how to flip it into the main world (the deliberate re-baseline is the owner's, "the easy part"); (2) the HEART of it: creatures and plants (and people, mostly done) made of usable MATERIAL STUFF, so a generated species is composition a being can cut, carry, cook, eat, not an opaque token, coupling the biosphere to the material substrate and the tool-use arc; (3) DECOMPOSITION AS EMERGENCE: replace the authored "matter decays always" law (the matter cycle's `bio.decomposition_barrier` and reserved decay rate on carrion and the spent_hull trace) with decay DRIVEN by decomposer LIFE (microbes, fungi, scavengers as generated species) or world-specific/evolved CONDITIONS (chemical or physical), so a sterile or frozen or anoxic world does NOT decay matter and a living warm wet one does, the trace's falsifiability-by-physics preserved but its driver made emergent (Principle 8). Also: the owner approved keep-adding affordance primitives up to the `SEQ_FIELD_BITS` 16-value ceiling, then flag (CUT is id 10, runway to 15).

STOPPED after slice 1a (committed, pushed, green). NEXT: build slice 1b (the CUT enact, designed above), deep-audit it, then slice 1c (run-path relation formation, the tool-reasoning payoff), closing arc-3's tool-use foundation; then composition, then artifacts; then the biosphere wiring (task 21) with the three directives above; then arc 4 (GPU blueprint plus Rayon). The `plan_chain` simple-path bound (arc 2) and `SEQ_FIELD_BITS` both come due as arc 3 densifies.

## 2026-07-07 (later still): ARC 2 (RELATIONAL-BELIEF SUBSTRATE) LANDED and deep-audited; the flagged planner spot was hardened on an audit finding, one bound flagged; moving to arc 3

Built ARC 2, the relational-belief substrate: a belief whose HEAD is another belief's SUBJECT, an edge `(head, relation, tail)` the being holds with a confidence, so "A yields X" and "A causes B" become first-class, turning the single-hop planner into MULTI-HOP planning, the seed of tool-reasoning. Byte-neutral opt-in (a mind forms a relation only by USE; empty by default).

THE SUBSTRATE (`crates/sim/src/agent.rs`): `Mind` gains `relations: BTreeMap<(StableId, AttrKindId, StableId), InferenceFrame>`, edges over the `{RELATES, UNRELATED}` hypotheses inferred by the SAME evidence engine as a first-order belief (a relation is LEARNED from correlated evidence, never handed). Methods `consider_relation`, `relation`, `has_relations`, `relations_into` (backward enumeration by tail). Folded into `Mind::state_hash` under `belief_params` after the wondering loop; an EMPTY relations map folds nothing, so a mind that holds none hashes exactly as before. Vocabulary (`crates/sim/src/learn.rs`): `RELATES=1`/`UNRELATED=0`, and `YIELDS` (`AttrKindId(u32::MAX-4)`, disjoint from HARM/REWARD attrs), the one built-in causal relation kind.

THE MULTI-HOP PLANNER (`crates/sim/src/planning.rs`): `plan_chain` (a `Plan` is a step chain, GOAL-LAST, `action_step()` = the deepest antecedent to do now) seeds from the one-hop frontier (`plan_toward`, kept as the primitive, its tests intact) then traverses `relations_into` BACKWARD from each goal subject, prepending the antecedent that brings it about, up to a reserved `hop_cap` (STEPS, a `k`-step plan chains `k-1` edges). Ranked weakest-link confidence desc, shorter-first, support desc, canonical (a strict total order). A being with NO relation returns the frontier as length-one chains in the same order, so it degenerates to the one-hop lookup exactly. WIRED on the run path (`crates/sim/src/runner.rs`): the discovery-pass deliberation now calls `plan_chain` instead of `plan_toward`; byte-identical for a relationless being (the only kind today, so `run_world` is unchanged).

THE SEAM CAUGHT AND HARDENED (deep blind audit, the owner-flagged planner spot). The fully-blind panel (three repo-walled skeptics on a tests-and-docs-stripped packet, each told to refute) confirmed byte-neutrality and determinism (no off-by-one, strict total order, no ties, safe against the two crash targets), and TWO independent auditors caught the same real seam: the planner traversed ANY committed-RELATES edge as a causal means-ends edge regardless of relation KIND, an authored universal that turns wrong the moment a non-causal relation kind (a similarity, say) is added. HARDENED per Principle 9 and the "harden the closed list authored one level down" discipline: `plan_chain` now takes a DATA-defined `reachable` set of causal relation kinds (`learn::builtin_reachable_relations`, today `{YIELDS}`); a kind absent from the set is INERT to the planner (default-off). A being may still hold and hash such a relation; the planner just never plans through it until the kind is declared causal in the data. Falsifier `a_non_causal_relation_kind_is_inert_to_the_planner_by_default` proves it (a committed non-causal edge is never surfaced; the same edge under YIELDS is). The reserved value `planning.hop_cap` is surfaced-with-basis (manifest `planning.hop_cap`, dev fixture 4), never fabricated.

FALSIFIERS (all green): the tool-reasoning payoff `a_being_chains_two_beliefs_to_reach_a_goal_a_single_hop_cannot` (a being believes "cutting pays off" and "striking yields cutting"; one hop grounds nothing present, multi-hop grounds STRIKING, the antecedent), `removing_the_relation_removes_the_multi_hop_capability` (the control: no relation, no chain), `with_no_relation_plan_chain_is_byte_identical_to_the_one_hop_planner` (unit byte-neutrality), the hop-cap bound, the direct-beats-chain ranking, the two determinism replays, plus the non-causal-kind falsifier and three `Mind`-level relation tests (form/read, empty-hashes-identically, order-independent fold).

FLAGGED BOUND (arc 2, sibling to `SEQ_FIELD_BITS`): the backward search enumerates every distinct simple path to a goal node with no cross-chain dedup, so a DENSE relation graph costs ~`b^(hop_cap-1)` chains per goal node. It TERMINATES and stays deterministic and is INERT today (no relation is formed on the run path yet, so the search never runs), but arc 3's relation formation would make a dense graph expensive. Named remediation, to take before arc 3 forms dense graphs: a best-first (widest-path) traversal with a per-node best-confidence memo, bounded by edges not paths (the consumer only reads each plan's `action_step`). Also noted: `DiscoveryCalib` still has no `from_manifest`, so `plan_hop_cap`/`plan_depth_cap` live in the dev fixture until that wiring reads them fail-loud (shared with the whole calib, a follow-on).

BYTE-NEUTRAL: default `run_world` `a465919e9825007c1ff876c03e4877ea` unchanged (clean rebuild), crucible `world_build` (17) + `world_determinism` (3) green, 692 lib + 886 integration tests green, fmt and clippy (`--all-targets -D warnings`) clean. No fabricated value.

STOPPED after the arc-2 hardening + audit. NEXT per the owner's arc order: ARC 3 (the made world, "the big one"): matter that affords a USE, not just food; tool-use, then material composition, then discarded/shaped artifacts; start with the simplest usable tool (a sharp thing that cuts). Arc 3 is where relations get FORMED on the run path (a being observes striking produce a flake, forms `(strike, YIELDS, sharp-thing)`), which is the seam `consider_relation` leaves clean. Take BEFORE arc 3 grows dense: the `plan_chain` simple-path bound (above) and the `SEQ_FIELD_BITS` 16-value belief-subject bound (piece 3). Then arc 4 (GPU blueprint + Rayon). The richer-observability thread (per-material, per-technique, and now per-RELATION readers) is parallel.

## 2026-07-07 (later still): ARC 1 (SOCIAL-LEARNING) CLOSED, the payoff MIXED-MATERIAL world landed; moving to arc 2

Closed arc 1 with the PAYOFF world, the first world where a being interacts with more than one material. Added a second FOOD substance to the ground floor (a soft, energy-dense `tuber`, cited: starchy-tuber energy ~15 to 17 kJ/g, potato-flesh density ~1080, raw-tuber fracture stress ~1 to 2 MPa, far softer than the oilseed shell), byte-neutral (deposited nowhere by default, `run_world` `a465919e` and the physics floor tests unchanged). The demonstration `the_mixed_material_payoff_world_composes_a_variety_of_food_techniques` (runner.rs): a being forages a MIXED cell of a hard oilseed, a soft tuber (a second food reserve `STARCH` backs it), and inert granite, and discovers a VARIETY of food techniques, learning that BOTH foods nourish it (each keyed on the composition of what it ATE) and NOTHING about the granite it never ate. All three arc-1 pieces are armed together on one runner (nutrition + observe-and-imitate + granular beliefs) over the keystone's material substrate, so the composition runs coherently; the opt-in falsifier holds (learning off, no food belief). Byte-neutral: crucible green, `run_world` `a465919e` unchanged, 876 lib+integration green, fmt/clippy clean.

ARC 1 IS COMPLETE: piece 1 nutrition learning, piece 2 observe-and-imitate, piece 3 material granularity, and the payoff mixed-material world, each byte-neutral opt-in, falsifier-backed, and blind-audited. The three transmission channels the payoff composes are each proven: gossip and the physical trace by the merged keystone, observation by piece 2. STOPPED after the payoff. NEXT per the owner's arc order: arc 2 (the RELATIONAL-BELIEF SUBSTRATE, a belief whose HEAD can be another belief's SUBJECT, "A causes B" / "A yields X", turning the single-hop planner into multi-hop planning and cross-domain insight, the seed of tool-reasoning); then arc 3 (the made world, matter that affords a USE: tool-use, then composition, then artifacts); then arc 4 (the GPU-integration blueprint AND implementing Rayon parallelization, tested headlessly). The richer-observability thread (per-material and per-technique readers) is parallel. The `SEQ_FIELD_BITS` 16-value belief-subject bound flagged at piece 3 is the one thing to take before arc 3 grows the affordance alphabet past 16.

---

## 2026-07-07 (later still): social-learning arc piece 3 (MATERIAL GRANULARITY) LANDED and deep-audited; the flagged appetitive/planner spot passed the masking crux, one seam hardened, one pre-existing bound flagged

Built Arc 1's THIRD piece, material granularity, the DEEP and PERMANENT form (the owner ruled channel+value keying and "whichever is deeper and won't have to be replaced later," so the target-blind deliberative reads were made value-aware rather than scoped). Byte-neutral opt-in.

MECHANISM: a discovered action's reward belief keys on the TARGET it acted on rather than the primitive alone, so "strike a hard thing" and "strike a soft thing" diverge as distinct learned actions. One shared helper `learn::step_belief_subject(step, granular)` is the SINGLE point that decides the grain (primitive-only by default, byte-identical; the full step of primitive, target affordance channel, and quantized target value when armed), and EVERY write and read routes through it, so write and reads never disagree on the grain. Opt-in `Embodiment::granular_beliefs` (default false); reserved `DiscoveryCalib::target_value_granularity` (basis: the sensorium per-class JND, the same basis harm/reward feature_granularity carry). A transient `Walker::decided_step` (the full step the being acted on, carried by the enacted discovery proposal or deliberated plan, a zeroed target for a base-controller action) is what the credit RECORD keys on; `candidate_bindings` carries the quantized value in param_bucket when granular.

THE FLAGGED DEEP SPOT (appetitive_salience + the planner match), audited deeply as the owner asked: these were TARGET-BLIND, at odds with a value-keyed belief, and were made value-aware permanently (`appetitive_salience` is now CANDIDATE-AWARE: an affordance lights when the being believes a PRESENT candidate of it pays off, keyed at the target grain; the deliberated match reconstructs the candidate's subject at the same grain; both byte-identical when granular is off). It got a DEDICATED deep blind panel (an Opus auditor on the masking crux plus two Sonnet lenses, byte-neutral and appetitive/planner). ALL THREE converged: the R1 MASKING CRUX PASSES (every write and read routes through the one pure `step_belief_subject` at the same `emb.granular_beliefs` flag, verified uniform at all sites, and `decided_step` IS the acted candidate, so write and read subjects coincide by construction). Two findings verified against source and acted on: (1) ZERO-TRIPLE ALIASING (the base-controller "no target" sentinel `(primitive,0,0)` was not disjoint from a real granular candidate at channel 0 / value-bucket 0) HARDENED by making granular candidate channels 1-based, reserving `target_bucket==0` for the sentinel (byte-neutral, granular-only); (2) the 4-bit belief-subject field CLAMP (`SEQ_FIELD_BITS`) caps the primitive alphabet, channel count, and value buckets at 16 each, a PRE-EXISTING over-merge (not a mask), currently LATENT (10 affordances, ids 0..9) but one arc 3 will pressure, FLAGGED prominently at `SEQ_FIELD_BITS` with the not-byte-neutral refinement (widen the field or hash the subject) surfaced for the owner rather than changed. Two packet "missing facts" (uniform granular threading; plan_toward's subject origin) were both verified fine against source.

FALSIFIER, two tests: `granular_beliefs_split_a_hard_target_from_a_soft_one_and_the_default_keeps_them_one` (discovery.rs, deterministic: the same primitive against a hard vs soft target of one channel mints distinct subjects when granular, one subject by default); `granular_beliefs_key_the_discovered_technique_on_the_target_it_acted_on_end_to_end` (runner.rs, run path: a granular being's committed geophage belief lives on the target-keyed subject and NOT on the primitive-only subject the default keys, so the whole loop moved grain together with no belief masked; default byte-identical to the viability loop).

BYTE-NEUTRAL: default run_world a465919e unchanged (clean rebuild), crucible green, 875 lib+integration green, fmt/clippy clean. No fabricated value (the JND reserved-with-basis). Mechanism sites: `crates/sim/src/{learn,discovery,forward_model,runner,locomotion}.rs`.

STOPPED: piece 3 built, falsified, deep-audited, hardened; committing. NEXT: close ARC 1 with the PAYOFF world (a mixed-material world with several foods and some inert matter, beings discovering a VARIETY of techniques and moving them three ways: gossip, observation, trace), composing nutrition + observe-and-imitate + granularity + the keystone trace. Then arc 2 (relational-belief substrate: a belief whose head is another belief's subject, multi-hop planning), arc 3 (the made world: matter that affords a USE, tool-use then composition then artifacts), arc 4 (GPU-integration blueprint AND implement Rayon parallelization, tested headlessly on this box). The richer-observability thread (per-material and per-technique readers) is parallel. R-OVERLAP-NE stays flagged.

---

## 2026-07-07 (later still): social-learning arc piece 2 (OBSERVE-AND-IMITATE) LANDED and fully-blind audited on `claude/agent-handoff-audit-ikmsdy`

Built Arc 1's SECOND piece, observe-and-imitate, the LIVING sibling of the physical trace (the trace carries a dead maker's mark to a later being; this carries a live neighbour's present action). Byte-neutral opt-in, founder-zero, falsifier-backed.

MECHANISM (landed): a being perceives the valence-free ActionTrace a co-located being leaves by eating and is BIASED toward trying that action, without forming a belief (copy-and-verify). A new founder-zero unit `Channel::SocialLearning` (mirror `Channel::Exploration`/`Deliberation`, genome.rs) expresses `Walker::social_learning` at the birth paths (runner.rs, worldbuild.rs); a new transient folded-when-nonempty `Walker::observed_actions` (a `BTreeSet<u16>` of the primitives co-located OTHER beings enacted-and-ate last tick) is rebuilt each armed tick in `couple_conversation` (the emit-and-gather, gated on the opt-in `Embodiment::observe_and_imitate` AND the being's own `social_learning > 0`, so only a being that can imitate carries the prior); and `discovery::candidate_weight`/`sample_candidate` LIFT an unbelieved candidate whose primitive is in `observed_actions` by the being's `social_learning` weight (capped at one), so the discovery draw tips toward a demonstrated technique. The one-tick lag is inherent (eating happens after the proposal in a tick, so tick T's eating biases tick T+1's proposal), so `observed_actions` is a folded one-tick memory.

EMERGENCE LINE HELD: the bias reads only the opaque primitive id a neighbour enacted plus the being's own founder-zero weight, never a label, valence, or kind. It biases ONLY the proposal (which action the being tries); the being's own felt reward stays the sole gate to a committed belief (copy-and-verify), so a copied action a being is not rewarded for forms no belief. Founder-zero at BOTH levels: a being with zero social-learning applies no bias AND folds no prior (imitation emerges by selection).

FALSIFIER, two tests. `an_observed_action_is_proposed_by_the_social_prior_and_a_founder_ignores_it` (discovery.rs, deterministic): with a zero floor, an observed action carries weight only through the social prior, so a being with a positive weight proposes it every tick (copies), a founder (zero weight) ignores it, and an unobserved action gets no lift. `a_being_perceives_a_co_located_eater_into_a_transient_prior_and_forms_no_belief_from_watching` (runner.rs, run path): an observer co-located with an eater perceives the eater's geophage into its prior yet forms NO reward belief from watching (copy-and-verify); with observe-and-imitate off it perceives nothing (opt-in falsifier); and the prior folds bit-identically in the pinned and scheduled tick orders (worker-invariant).

BYTE-NEUTRAL: default `run_world` `a465919e9825007c1ff876c03e4877ea` unchanged (verified on a clean rebuild; an early stale-incremental-build reading misled at first, a lesson to force rebuilds for hash checks), crucible `world_build` (17) + `world_determinism` green, 873 lib+integration tests green, fmt/clippy clean. No new reserved values (reuses the discovery calib's exploration floor as the boost base).

FULLY-BLIND AUDIT: a repo-walled packet (substrate contract + the boost/gather/fold code + spec, no tests/docs) audited by two independent Sonnet panelists (a determinism lens and an emergence lens). Both converged on ONE real finding: the gather pass populated and folded `observed_actions` for a founder too, so founder-zero held behaviourally but not at the hash level. Verified against source and FIXED (gate population on `social_learning > 0`), a behaviour-neutral improvement that shrinks the fold surface and makes founder-zero hold at the hash level. The panel's other calls: R5 copy-and-verify PASS, boost totality PASS, no-handed-knowledge PASS, behavioural founder-zero PASS. Two NEED-MORE-INFO items resolved against source: the prior is a deterministic function of the tick (counter-keyed RNG, sorted containers, the same guarantee the folded `eligibility_trace` relies on), now proven by the scheduler-invariance assertion; and `observe_and_imitate` is a set-once build-time flag (the codebase convention for opt-in flags), so a stale-on-flip prior would be used consistently by both the fold and the draw (deterministic, not a replay hazard).

STOPPED: piece 2 built, tested, audited, committed (`4ef7e54`) and pushed. NEXT (Arc 1 continues, gated): piece 3 material granularity, then the payoff mixed-material world composing gossip, observation, and trace. R-OVERLAP-NE stays flagged.

PIECE 3 GROUNDING (for the next session, so it is a crisp gate hand-off). Material granularity keys the reward belief on the target's quantized perceived VALUE at the just-noticeable difference, so "strike a hard thing" and "strike a soft thing" diverge as distinct learned actions instead of one flat "geophage pays off." UNLIKE pieces 1 and 2 (pure opt-in additions), this changes the KEYING of the discovery loop's sequence beliefs, so it is NOT automatically byte-neutral for a discovery-armed run and it has a SILENT-FAILURE mode: a keying mismatch masks the belief (exactly the defect the earlier belief-key consistency fix caught, HANDOFFS 2026-07-06). The 14 sites that today key a sequence belief on `target_bucket: 0` and must all move together: `discovery.rs:candidate_weight`, `learn.rs:appetitive_salience` (~649) and the credit/subject sites (~952, 1048, 1053), `forward_model.rs:predicted_reward` (~120), `planning.rs:plan_toward` (~164), and the runner's eligibility-trace RECORD (~2835), the surprise read (~3036), and the discovery deliberation match (~3596), plus the test fixtures. RECOMMENDED SHAPE (opt-in, preserves byte-neutrality): an opt-in flag (a new `Embodiment` bool, e.g. `value_keyed_beliefs`, default off) that switches the belief key from `target_bucket: 0` to `target_bucket = feature_bucket(perceived_value, jnd)`; the perceived VALUE is the affordance-percept scalar the candidate carries (the FracturePotential the being acted on), so a candidate already carries its target value and the RECORD keys on the enacted candidate's value bucket while `candidate_weight` keys on the proposed candidate's value bucket (they match because the being enacts the candidate it proposed). DESIGN FORK for the gate: whether the belief keys on the VALUE only, the affordance CHANNEL (type) only (today's `candidate_bindings` carries the channel index), or CHANNEL-plus-VALUE (type and how-hard together); the screed's "hard thing vs soft thing" points to the quantized value of the acted-on channel. THE COORDINATION CRUX (the reason this is a gate, not a mechanical find-replace): the eligibility-trace RECORD (runner.rs ~2835) keys off `w.decided_affordance` (a primitive, no target value stored at RECORD time, so a value key needs the enacted target value carried on the walker, an `ate`-style transient), and `appetitive_salience` (learn.rs ~649) and the planner (planning.rs ~164) are TARGET-BLIND (they read a belief per affordance, with no specific target value), fundamentally at odds with a value-keyed belief. So value-keying the credit WITHOUT reconciling these two target-blind reads reintroduces the exact masking bug the 2026-07-06 belief-key consistency fix caught (a belief committed on one key is never read on another). The gate must rule how the deliberative reads reconcile: make them value-aware (they look up the current perceived target's value), keep them target-blind on a parallel primitive-only key alongside a value-keyed discovery key, or scope value-keying to the discovery loop only. This is why the code names it "the deferred generalisation." RESERVED: the target-value JND (basis: the sensorium per-class just-noticeable difference, the same basis `harm.feature_granularity` and `reward.feature_granularity` already carry, so a reuse candidate). Then the payoff world (a mixed-material world with several foods and inert matter) composes nutrition + observe-and-imitate + the trace, and a variety of techniques diverge and move three ways (gossip, observation, trace).

---

## 2026-07-07 (later): PR #97 MERGED to main; SOCIAL-LEARNING ARC opened; piece 1 (NUTRITION learning) LANDED and fully-blind audited on `claude/agent-handoff-audit-ikmsdy`

PR #97 (the lifetime/demography keystone) is merged to `main` (`e18dc78`). Picked up the owner's Arc-1/2/3 re-sequencing (the social-learning arc is next, highest value, fully unblocked; then the relational-belief substrate, then the made world). Built Arc 1's FIRST piece, NUTRITION learning, byte-neutral opt-in and falsifier-backed, composing the merged keystone's material-percept and reward pieces.

MECHANISM (landed): a being re-earns "this food nourishes me" from the perceived composition of what it ATE, the eaten-side sibling of the keystone's ground-side (trace) credit. A new transient `Walker::ate` (`SubstanceMix`, the per-substance volume `Embodiment::geophage` ingested this tick; cleared at the top of the embodiment step in `step_with_field_dirs`, populated by the bite, NEVER folded into `state_hash`) carries what was eaten to `couple_conversation`, where a new NUTRITION credit (gated on the opt-in `Embodiment::nutrition_learning`, off by default, plus the reward learner armed) perceives it through the material-percept registry and feeds `reward_observations` under `REWARD_ATTR` / `MATERIAL_FEATURE_CHANNEL_BASE` on a disjoint ordinal band (`NUTRITION_REWARD_LEARN_ORDINAL_BASE = 4_000_000`). It shares the material feature subject with the ground-side credit, so the two evidence streams commit one "this signature rewards" belief the existing attraction gradient already reads (the behaviour half rides for free). The existing ground-side credit gained an explicit `place_reward_learning` gate (default TRUE, a switch on an existing mechanism so the keystone behaviour is unchanged) so a world can run the eaten-side credit in isolation.

EMERGENCE LINE HELD: the credit reads only the opaque per-substance volume of what was eaten and the felt-reward sign (`is_reward_tick`), no handed "this is food" label, kind, or race (Principles 8, 9). The belief forms only from felt reward (the copy-and-verify gate); a being that eats inert matter (no reserve rise) or never eats forms none. Founder-zero compatible (the behaviour rides an evolved attraction weight lifted off zero by selection). The eaten-keying is the genuine delta over the ground-keyed trace: in a mixed-material world a being on a tile of food-plus-inert-matter eating only the food must not learn the inert matter nourishes.

FALSIFIER (`a_being_learns_which_food_nourishes_from_what_it_ate_not_the_ground_it_stood_on`, runner.rs): proven three ways with the place credit OFF so the eaten-side credit is isolated. (1) a primed nutrition learner eats a bounded oilseed ration while standing on inert granite it never eats, and re-earns a REWARDS belief about the OILSEED IT ATE and NONE about the GRANITE IT STOOD ON (the eaten-keying); (2) with nutrition off the being eats but forms no belief (the byte-neutral opt-in falsifier); (3) a founder-zero being never enacts geophage, never eats, forms none (the emergence falsifier).

BYTE-NEUTRAL: crucible `world_build` (17) + `world_determinism` green; default `run_world` `a465919e9825007c1ff876c03e4877ea` unchanged; 677 lib (+1 for the nutrition test) + all integration tests green; fmt and clippy clean. No new reserved values (reuses the reward calib, whose values are already reserved-with-basis).

FULLY-BLIND AUDIT (the owner's "cheapest good agent full blind audit methodology"): a repo-walled packet (substrate contract + the credit code stripped of self-assessing comments + the declared spec, no tests or docs) was piloted on one Sonnet auditor for sufficiency, its gaps folded in, then a small independent panel on Sonnet with varied framing. Every finding was verified against the real source (prime directive 1). Result: the slice introduces NO defect; the panel converged. Three raised items, all resolved against source. (a) An unchecked `u16` add `channel_base + channel as u16` in `reward_observations`, a PRE-EXISTING by-design channel-space partition (biology 0..32767, material 32768..65535; the material floor declares about eight substances) shared with the merged trace credit, astronomically out of range and not introduced by this slice; surfaced for the owner rather than fixed here (it touches keystone code and needs a channel-width call). (b) The shared belief subject double-counts evidence when a substance is both eaten and stood on, which is INTENDED (one "this signature rewards" belief) and bounded by the evidence log-odds clamp (`agent.rs` `clamped_total`), and is off in the demonstration. (c) A stale-`ate` concern that CANNOT occur, since `reconcile_lifecycle` retires dead walkers every tick after `couple_conversation` and there is no revival, so `ate` is never multi-tick stale and a dying walker's falling reserve yields NEUTRAL not a spurious REWARDS.

STOPPED: piece 1 built, tested, audited, committed and pushed to `claude/agent-handoff-audit-ikmsdy`. NEXT (Arc 1 continues, gated by the owner as the prior slices were): piece 2 observe-and-imitate (a valence-free `ActionTrace` placed-event when a being eats, riding the co-located placed-event pipeline, perceived by co-located observers into a TRANSIENT PRIOR that biases the discovery loop's `candidate_weight`, scaled by a new founder-zero `Channel::SocialLearning` mirroring `Channel::Exploration`; `is_reward_tick` stays the sole gate to a committed belief, copy-and-verify); piece 3 material granularity (key the reward belief on the target's quantized perceived value at the just-noticeable difference so techniques diverge by target); then the payoff mixed-material world composing gossip, observation, and trace. R-OVERLAP-NE stays flagged (census untouched).
