## 2026-07-06: IDEATION arc COMPLETE (all four pieces), slice 4b the deliberation gate, on branch claude/ideation-discovery, PR #94

THE IDEATION / EXPERIENTIAL-DISCOVERY ARC IS COMPLETE in its buildable scope. Slice 4b landed (commit b015941), closing piece 4 and the arc: a being now LEARNS which of its own actions pay off (piece 1), DISCOVERS and tries novel ones (piece 2), EXPLORES more where its predictions fail (piece 3), and PURSUES its believed-best action toward a goal from memory (piece 4). All four pieces are gate-signed-off through 4a; 4b is freshly pushed for review. The built-but-dormant materials affordances now get USED by beings acting on their own felt experience, the arc's purpose. The one remaining item is the FLAGGED relational-belief substrate (the multi-hop dependency, the owner's next-arc sequencing call), not part of this arc.

SLICE 4b (WIRE, opt-in, hash-changing when armed, commit b015941): the deliberation enactment gate. `Walker::deliberation` (heritable, founder-zero, folds when positive), the deliberation analogue of `exploration`: exploration tries the untried, deliberation exploits the best-believed. The discovery pass now also computes each being's DELIBERATED action: `plan_toward` (slice 4a) over the reward goal, intersected with the being's current binding-graph candidates, so the recalled belief is GROUNDED (actable only where the senses afford and perceive it, matched on the primitive subject the credit records and the plan ranks). The enact block gains a `DELIBERATE`-phase gate (0x1E, disjoint from ENACT) BEFORE the exploration gate: a being with a positive deliberation weight draws and, on a grounded believed-best matter primitive, enacts it and takes PRECEDENCE over exploration (exploit the plan when there is one, explore otherwise, via an `acted` flag gating the exploration draw). FOUNDER-ZERO: a founder never deliberates, so goal-directed pursuit EMERGES by selection, never a coded "when idle, plan" (the gate's explicit direction). `DiscoveryCalib.plan_depth_cap` wires the reserved `planning.depth_cap`; `World::mind_mut` added (a mutable mind accessor for seeding beliefs in scenarios and tests). NO new reserved scalar (the deliberation base is founder-zero structural, the evolve follow-on reuses `genome.additive_mutation_step` and a `genome.loci_per_channel` data row, same grounding as 2c-2, noted in reserved.toml). Opt-in and byte-identical when unused (crucible unchanged, world_build 16 and world_determinism 3 replay-identical; 662 sim lib tests green; fmt --all --check and clippy -D warnings clean). Proven by `a_being_deliberates_onto_its_believed_best_grounded_action_and_a_founder_never_does` (a primed being enacts its believed-best grounded grasp; a founder never deliberates; the same believed-good being on a void cell enacts nothing, the grounding gate).

DESIGN CHOICES surfaced for the gate (4b's integration, made defensibly and flagged): (1) the deliberated action is the highest-plan-confidence candidate PRESENT in the current binding graph (the grounding gate reuses the binding graph rather than a separate percept-bind test, so a recalled belief with no matching percept is inert without new machinery). (2) Deliberation takes PRECEDENCE over exploration (a being that acts on its plan does not also explore this tick); the two heritable drives are independent draws (disjoint phases) so selection tunes each, and a being exploits its best grounded belief when it has one and explores otherwise. (3) The plan-to-action match keys on the PRIMITIVE subject (the channel-blind subject the credit records), so 4b inherits and is consistent with the channel-blind-credit seam surfaced at 3b (aligning the credit/sampler/plan on one subject is the flagged piece-2 hardening).

NEXT (the arc's buildable scope is done; these are the flagged threads, the owner sequences): (1) the RELATIONAL-BELIEF SUBSTRATE (the multi-hop dependency that turns the single-hop planner into insight and cross-domain innovation, a substrate/next-arc). (2) The CHANNEL-BLIND-CREDIT seam (a piece-2 hardening: align the reward credit, the discovery sampler, and the planner on one sequence subject). (3) The Channel::Exploration and Channel::Deliberation EVOLVE follow-ons (express the two heritable weights from genome at the birth path, so they evolve rather than being primed) and the deferred force-param perturbation (piece 3). All non-blocking; the arc's forward mechanism is complete. Keep building ahead on the owner's/gate's next direction; the gate reviews PR #94 and pings each landing.

---

## 2026-07-06: IDEATION arc PIECE 4 BEGUN (belief-graph planning), slice 4a the planner READ, on branch claude/ideation-discovery, PR #94

The gate confirmed the piece-4 scoping (praised the audit-the-input catch on its own "build piece 4" direction): BUILD slice 4a as the honest single-hop confidence-ranked goal planner, the DELIBERATIVE complement to piece 1's reactive salience; FLAG (do not build) the relational belief type as its own substrate, the genuine multi-hop dependency, a next-arc candidate the owner sequences (relational-belief substrate versus biosphere-into-run). Slice 4a pushed (commit ac08f10). The ideation arc's READ substrate is now complete across all four pieces; pieces 1, 2, 3 are fully wired; only slice 4b (the deliberation-to-action WIRE) remains buildable, and the relational substrate is the flagged next-substrate.

SLICE 4a (READ, hash-neutral, off the run path, commit ac08f10): the belief-graph planner, a new module `crates/sim/src/planning.rs`. `plan_toward(mind, goal_attr, goal_value, params, depth_cap) -> Vec<PlanStep>` returns the being's committed beliefs matching the goal predicate `(goal_attr, goal_value)`, ranked by the frame's commit margin (confidence, the same decisiveness `InferenceFrame::commit` gates on, exposed via `clamped_total`) and support (evidence count), truncated to the reserved depth cap, a deterministic total order (confidence, then support, then canonical subject/attr) with no RNG and no domain knowledge (Principles 8, 9). For the arc `plan_toward(mind, REWARD_ATTR, REWARDS, params, cap)` is "what do I believe pays off, ranked," so a being pursues a goal from memory rather than only reacting to what it perceives. `Mind::frames()` added (a belief-frame iterator accessor). `planning.depth_cap` reserved-with-basis (the per-tick cognition budget, the depth beyond which marginal gain falls below the confidence noise). Proven by 3 unit tests. Crucible unchanged, world_build 16 and world_determinism 3 replay-identical, 661 sim lib tests green, fmt --all --check and clippy -D warnings clean. SINGLE-HOP by honest necessity (confirmed by the gate): the store holds PROPERTY beliefs and a value can never be another belief's subject, so no multi-hop graph exists yet; the planner is written goal-predicate-in / confidence-ranked-path-out so a relational belief kind slots in later without a rewrite.

FLAGGED NEXT-SUBSTRATE (the gate's call, the owner sequences): the RELATIONAL BELIEF TYPE. A belief the search can CHAIN (an edge whose head can be another belief's subject: "A causes B", "A yields resource X"), sibling to the property belief, is what turns the single-hop lookup into the arc's full vision (insight as finding an untraversed path, innovation as combining beliefs from disparate domains). It is a substrate (arguably its own arc), not a slice, so it is flagged rather than crammed into this arc. The owner's sequencing call: a relational-belief substrate versus the biosphere-into-run addendum as the next arc.

NEXT: slice 4b (WIRE, opt-in), the grounding gate on consumption, the LAST buildable slice of the arc. Per the handoff: a recalled or taught template (from `plan_toward`) becomes an executable candidate ONLY when the receiver's own sensorium currently perceives affordances that bind its wildcard slots (mirroring `avoidance_gradient`'s subject-match test), and only through a heritable action-bias weight lifted off founder-zero by selection; the gate lives in the planner's consumption step, NEVER in gossip (transmission stays "carry any committing (subject, attr) frame"). DESIGN to ground and likely surface (emergence-sensitive, overlaps the discovery/2c-2 machinery): how a recalled plan drives action distinct from the discovery PROPOSAL (deliberation, recalling a believed-good action not underfoot, versus exploration proposing a novel one), where the grounding gate binds the plan's affordance to a current percept, and the heritable action-bias weight (founder-zero, the deliberation analogue of the exploration propensity). Keep the flag-then-build discipline the gate has confirmed at each emergence-sensitive fork. FLAG A SEAM if the minimal wiring authors anything.

---

## 2026-07-06: IDEATION arc PIECE 3 COMPLETE (forward model + prediction-error exploration), slice 3b surprise-modulated exploration, on branch claude/ideation-discovery, PR #94

The gate SIGNED OFF slices 3a AND the 3b design (multiplicative modulation confirmed as the load-bearing founder-zero seam, the propensity realisation confirmed as the honest call over the deferred force-param perturbation). Piece 3 is now closed: a being predicts its actions' outcomes, and where the world defies the prediction it explores more. Slice 3b pushed (commit 1bb0d24). Pieces 1 (learn what works), 2 (discover novel actions), and 3 (explore where surprised) all stand; only piece 4 (belief-graph planning) remains in the arc.

SLICE 3b (WIRE, opt-in, hash-changing when armed, commit 1bb0d24): surprise-modulated exploration. `Walker::surprise` (dynamic prediction-error magnitude, zero by default, folds into `state_hash` when positive). `couple_conversation` scores `forward_model::prediction_error` on the being's last-enacted action (`decided_affordance`) against the reward it felt this tick, at the SAME site and one-tick interoceptive lag the reward credit uses, on the SAME primitive subject the credit records, and stores the magnitude as `w.surprise` (reset to zero when the being enacted nothing). The 2c-2 ENACT gate reads the MULTIPLICATIVE effective propensity `base * (1 + gain * surprise)` when the surprise clears the reserved threshold, else the bare base. MULTIPLICATIVE is the founder-zero seam: a founder (zero base) yields zero times any lift, so it never explores however surprised; surprise only ever AMPLIFIES an already-heritable drive (Principle 9). The modulation reuses the counter-keyed ENACT draw (only its threshold moves), so NO new phase or gaussian is needed: this is the propensity realisation the gate confirmed, not the handoff's force-param perturbation (deferred: no affordance consumes a graded action param yet). `DiscoveryCalib` gains `surprise_threshold` and `surprise_gain`, reserved-with-basis in `calibration/reserved.toml` (threshold basis: the interoceptive noise floor, so a sub-resolution mismatch is not surprise; gain basis: the EXPLORE scale and sensorium JND, a proportionate lift; note the categorical {-1,0,+1} surprise makes the threshold a presence gate until a graded reward magnitude is learned). Opt-in and byte-identical when unused: both the surprise update and the modulation are gated on the discovery loop being armed, and the fold on `surprise > ZERO` (crucible unchanged, world_build 16 and world_determinism 3 replay-identical; 658 sim lib tests green; fmt --all --check and clippy -D warnings clean). Proven by `surprise_lifts_a_beings_exploration_multiplicatively_and_never_lifts_a_founder` (a surprised being enacts strictly more than the calm control, a founder never enacts however surprised).

SEAM SURFACED for the gate (pre-existing, piece 2, NOT introduced by 3b; found by auditing the credit site while grounding 3b): the reward credit records the PRIMITIVE-only sequence subject (`target_bucket: 0`, `runner.rs` eligibility-trace record), but the discovery sampler (`discovery::candidate_weight`) reads the FULL channel subject (`target_bucket: channel`). The two coincide ONLY for channel-0 percepts (FracturePotential), so for channel >= 1 (Sharpness and beyond) the sampler's belief-biased exploit path never matches a committed belief and always explores at the floor, and the being learns primitive-level "this action pays off," channel-blind, rather than the channel-specific techniques the 1b design intended ("strike a fracturable thing" distinct from "strike a sharp thing"). 3b scores surprise on the primitive subject deliberately, matching the credit, so 3b is correct regardless. Aligning the credit subject with the sampler (record the full `proposed_action` subject in the trace, and read the primitive subject in `appetitive_salience`, or a consolidation of the three) is a separate hash-changing slice; recommended, flagged, the gate's call.

NEXT, three threads (the gate sequences): (1) PIECE 4 (belief-graph search: a generic planner over the belief store with a reserved depth cap, then the grounding gate on consumption so a taught template stays inert until the receiver's senses bind its wildcard slots), per docs/working/IDEATION_DISCOVERY_HANDOFF.md, the last piece of the arc. (2) The channel-blind-credit seam (above), a piece-2 hardening. (3) The Channel::Exploration EVOLVE follow-on and the deferred force-param perturbation (both scoped, non-blocking). Keep building ahead; the gate reviews PR #94 and pings each landing. FLAG A SEAM if the minimal wiring authors anything.

PIECE 4 GROUNDING (auditing the gate's "build piece 4" direction against the actual belief store, a scoping fork surfaced BEFORE building a planner of the wrong shape, the same flag-then-build pattern the gate confirmed for 2c-2 and 3b): the shipped belief store is `Mind::beliefs: BTreeMap<(subject, attr), InferenceFrame>` (crates/sim/src/agent.rs, evidence.rs), holding PROPERTY beliefs, a value on a (subject, attr): "action A pays off" (REWARD_ATTR), "feature F harms me" (HARM_ATTR), language/semantic beliefs. It holds NO RELATIONAL belief ("A causes B", "A yields resource X"), and a value is a small ValueId (a category index) while a subject is a StableId, so a value can never BE another belief's subject: there is no subject-to-subject edge and thus NO MULTI-HOP graph to traverse. So piece 4's "search the belief graph for a PATH" reduces, over the current store, to a SINGLE-HOP confidence-ranked lookup from a goal predicate (for the arc, `(REWARD_ATTR, REWARDS)`, "what do I believe pays off") to the actions believed to reach it, ranked by frame confidence (the commit margin / knowledge_level) and frame support (evidence count), bounded by the reserved depth cap. That single-hop planner is buildable now (a hash-neutral READ over committed_beliefs, reading only confidence + support + a depth cap, no domain knowledge), correct, and extensible, but "insight is finding an untraversed PATH; innovation is combining beliefs from disparate domains" (the handoff's piece-4 vision) needs a RELATIONAL belief type first (an edge a search can chain), which the arc has not built. RECOMMENDATION surfaced for the gate: build slice 4a as the generic bounded confidence-ranked goal search (the honest single-hop realisation over the current store, structured to extend to multi-hop), and treat the relational belief type (the substrate that makes multi-hop planning and cross-domain innovation real) as the piece-4 dependency to scope, either as slice 4a's own predecessor or a follow-on. Awaiting the gate's call on the piece-4 shape; the single-hop planner is ready to build on confirmation. NOT blocking: this is an audit-the-input flag on the last, most speculative piece, not a stop for a sign-off.

---

## 2026-07-06: IDEATION arc PIECE 3 BEGUN (forward model + prediction-error exploration), slice 3a the forward-model READ, on branch claude/ideation-discovery, PR #94

The gate SIGNED OFF slice 2c-2 and all of piece 2, and directed piece 3 next ("the forward model and prediction-error drive, which makes this exploration propensity surprise-responsive, then piece 4"). Piece 3 begun with slice 3a (the READ), pushed (commit ae28f6c). The gate confirmed the 2c-2 no-new-reserved-value finding ("No new reserved value here ... the reserved base and mutation scale ride the Channel::Exploration follow-on"); the Channel::Exploration EVOLVE follow-on is deferred (a later "ride the follow-on" item, not blocking), and my reserved.toml note that the follow-on also owes no new scalar (founder-zero base structural, mutation reuses genome.additive_mutation_step) stands for the gate to confirm when that follow-on lands.

SLICE 3a (READ, hash-neutral, off the run path, commit ae28f6c): the forward model and the prediction-error read, a new module `crates/sim/src/forward_model.rs`. `predicted_reward(mind, step, params) -> Fixed` reads the being's committed REWARDS belief about the step's `sequence_subject` (ONE if committed REWARDS, ZERO otherwise): the template's committed reward IS its prediction, `appetitive_salience` generalised from a single primitive to a full `SequenceStep` subject, so the model has no state of its own and reads the exact belief the piece-1 credit pass commits and the piece-2 sampler weights. `prediction_error(mind, step, felt_delta, reward_noise_floor, params) -> Fixed` is the felt reward (ONE when `is_reward_tick(felt_delta, floor)`, the SAME reduction the credit pass uses, else ZERO) minus the prediction, a signed surprise in {-ONE, ZERO, +ONE}: a well-predicted outcome reads zero, an expected reward that fails reads -ONE, an unexpected reward reads +ONE. No new engine (reads the shipped belief substrate + is_reward_tick), no reserved value (the surprise threshold and variance gain are slice 3b's). Proven by 3 unit tests. HONEST LIMIT surfaced in the module doc: the prediction is CATEGORICAL because the belief carries a category and confidence, not a learned reserve-delta magnitude, and this slice fabricates none; a graded refinement is a natural later step. Crucible unchanged, world_build 16 and world_determinism 3 replay-identical, 655 sim lib tests green (652 + 3), fmt --all --check and clippy -D warnings clean.

NEXT: slice 3b (WIRE, opt-in, hash-changing), variance inflation on surprise. GROUNDED, with a design point to get right (surfaced for the gate):
- The gate framed piece 3 as making the EXPLORATION PROPENSITY surprise-responsive. So slice 3b modulates the 2c-2 ENACT gate: a being's EFFECTIVE enact rate rises with its recent prediction error and falls as its predictions come true, so it explores more where the world is least understood.
- FOUNDER-ZERO CONSTRAINT (the seam to get right, load-bearing): the surprise modulation must be MULTIPLICATIVE on the heritable propensity, NOT additive. If surprise ADDED to the effective propensity, a founder (propensity zero) would explore when surprised, AUTHORING exploration and breaking founder-zero. Multiplicative (effective = base * (1 + gain * surprise), gated on surprise > threshold) keeps a founder at zero * anything = zero: a founder never explores however surprised, and only a being whose heritable propensity is lifted off zero explores MORE when surprised. This is the founder-zero-preserving realisation of "explore more when surprised".
- The handoff's literal "inflate motor variance on the param DIMENSION of the error" (perturb a force param) cannot apply yet: the enactment consumes no graded action param (the 2c-2 gate enacts with a fixed activation, and grasp/extract read grown strength, not the SequenceStep param_bucket). So the force-param perturbation is DEFERRED until a graded action param is consumed by the physics; slice 3b realises "explore more when surprised" as the propensity modulation on the built substrate. This divergence from the handoff's wording is surfaced for the gate.
- Mechanics: a per-being surprise memory on the Walker (folded into state_hash zero-by-default, so opted-out runs are byte-identical), updated each tick from `prediction_error` on the being's last-enacted action against its felt reserve delta (the one-tick interoceptive lag, mirroring slice 1c's eligibility credit); the ENACT gate reads the modulated effective propensity; the surprise draw stays counter-keyed (reuse ENACT or a disjoint phase). RESERVED (slice 3b, reserved-with-basis, fail-loud): the prediction-error threshold (basis: the interoceptive noise floor already defined for reward and harm, so a sub-resolution mismatch is not surprise) and the exploration variance gain (basis: the existing EXPLORE heading-noise scale and the sensorium JND, mapping a unit surprise to a proportionate propensity lift). Determinism: founder-zero (and surprise-zero) byte-identical; every fold canonical. FLAG A SEAM if the minimal wiring authors anything.

---

## 2026-07-06: IDEATION arc PIECE 2 COMPLETE (action-as-hypothesis: PERCEIVE, PROPOSE, ENACT), slice 2c-2 the enactment gate, on branch claude/ideation-discovery, PR #94

Piece 2 of the ideation arc is now closed end to end: a being PERCEIVES the physics-derived affordances of the matter around it, PROPOSES a candidate action against them (the binding graph plus the belief-biased sampler), and ENACTS the proposal when its own heritable exploration propensity fires, so a hypothesis becomes a tried action the reward learner can credit. Built opt-in and byte-identical when unused (crucible unchanged, world_build 16 and world_determinism 3 replay-identical; 654 sim lib tests green; fmt `--all --check` and clippy `--all-targets -D warnings` clean). The gate signed off 2a, 2b, and 2c-1; 2c-2 is freshly pushed (commit e46de74).

SLICE 2c-2 (WIRE, opt-in, hash-changing when armed and primed): the exploration ENACTMENT gate, the gate's resolved founder-zero design (its 2c-1 sign-off ruling). `crates/core/src/keys.rs`: `ENACT = Phase(0x1D)`, the enact draw's phase, disjoint from `HYPOTHESIZE` (0x1C) so the propose and enact draws never collide on a counter. `crates/sim/src/locomotion.rs`: `Walker::exploration: Fixed`, the being's heritable enact rate in `[0, 1]`, FOUNDER-ZERO (defaults to `Fixed::ZERO`), folded into `state_hash` only when positive (so a founder folds nothing and stays byte-identical). `crates/sim/src/runner.rs`: the gate block right after the discovery proposal write (the `(2a'')` block) - when discovery is armed, a being with a positive propensity draws `DrawKey::entity(w.id.0, tick, Phase::ENACT).rng(seed).unit_fixed(0) < w.exploration` and, on a proposed MATTER primitive (GRASP/EXTRACT/GEOPHAGE/CRAFT/DIG/RELEASE/SHELTER, the ones the deferred-action pass enacts), injects it into `deferred_actions` and records `decided_affordance`, so the SAME enactment path the controller uses executes it and the eligibility trace credits the sequence (propose, enact, feel, credit). A proposed MOVE or INGEST is the controller's own, already enacted in the locomotion step, so it is not re-injected; STRIKE's arm is left for the primitive-versus-verb consolidation (TODOs #60/#61), as the gate directed. Proven by `a_primed_being_enacts_its_proposal_while_a_founder_only_proposes_it`: a being on granite with a GRASP-only affordance registry and the affordance percepts installed proposes a grasp; PRIMED to `exploration = ONE` it enacts (its `decided_affordance` becomes GRASP through the gate), FOUNDER-ZERO it proposes the identical grasp but never enacts (its zeros controller decides nothing and its propensity never fires), the primed run replays bit-for-bit, and the propensity folds into the hash (canonical state). The physical grasp itself (matter into the load, bounded by strength) is material-substrate item 3's and proven there against a muscled body; this test isolates the GATE, whether a proposal becomes a tried action, read off `decided_affordance` rather than re-running item 3.

FINDING surfaced to the gate (NO new reserved value owed, on grounding, `calibration/reserved.toml` note after `discovery.exploration_floor`): the gate anticipated "the exploration-propensity base and its mutation scale" as reserved. On grounding against the actual mechanism, 2c-2 owes NO new reserved scalar. FOUNDER-ZERO makes the base a STRUCTURAL zero (Principle 9: exploration emerges by selection, never a number to set). The follow-on that makes exploration heritable adds a `Channel::Exploration` data row to the existing `genome.loci_per_channel` map and mutates it through the existing `genome.additive_mutation_step` (the standing per-locus additive step every quantitative allele already drifts at, reused for consistency, not a fresh dial). The expression to a `[0, 1]` enact rate is the genome-encoded reaction norm (the loci ARE the substrate, as for `Channel::Controller`), not a manifest scalar, and the `[0, 1]` clamp is structural (a probability). This slice proves the gate mechanism with a PRIMED `Walker::exploration` (a test fixture, not a manifest value). Recorded so the manifest documents WHY there is no 2c-2 entry rather than leaving a silent gap. (Prime directives 3 and 5: surfaced the honest finding rather than fabricating two reserved entries to match the anticipation.)

STOPPED: slice 2c-2 pushed to PR #94; piece 2 is complete (PERCEIVE + PROPOSE + ENACT). NEXT, two threads:
- The EVOLVE half of prime-then-evolve (the follow-on the 2c-2 finding scopes): a `Channel::Exploration` genome variant expressed at the birth path `Controller::express`/`runner.rs:3421` (a new single-locus scalar, founder-zero, mutated via the existing `genome.additive_mutation_step` under a keyed phase, expressed into `Walker::exploration`), so the propensity EVOLVES rather than being primed. This turns the primed 2c-2 mechanism into a selected one: a mutant with a lifted propensity tries the untried, and if trying pays off (piece 1 credits it), selection lifts the propensity across the lineage. No new reserved value (see the finding).
- PIECE 3 (forward model + prediction-error exploration) and PIECE 4 (belief-graph planning), the remaining ideation pieces per `docs/working/IDEATION_DISCOVERY_HANDOFF.md`. Piece 3 makes the exploration gate RESPONSIVE (explore more when surprised), a refinement of the same weight, not a new mechanism.
Keep building ahead; the gate reviews PR #94 and pings each landing. FLAG A SEAM if the minimal wiring authors anything (the 2c-2 wiring authored nothing: founder-zero holds, the enactment reuses the controller's own deferred-action path, and the finding surfaced the absence of a fabricated reserved value).

---

## 2026-07-06: IDEATION arc PIECE 1 COMPLETE (the appetitive learner: LEARN + REPEAT), slices 1b through 1d, on branch claude/ideation-discovery, PR #94

Piece 1 of the ideation arc is now closed end to end: a being LEARNS an action pays off from its own felt reward and REPEATS it, observed through the actual runner, the reward-side mirror of harm-learning (form the belief, then act on it through an evolved weight). Built on the shipped associative engine with no engine change, each slice opt-in and byte-identical when unused (crucible `254bc17c` unchanged; world_build 16, world_determinism 3 replay-identical; 644 sim lib tests green; fmt `--all --check` and clippy `--all-targets -D warnings` clean). The gate signed off 1a, 1b, 1c, and 1d-READ; 1d-WIRE is freshly pushed.

SLICE 1b (READ, hash-neutral): the sequence-template subject and the eligibility trace. `learn.rs`: `sequence_subject(&[SequenceStep{primitive,target_bucket,param_bucket}]) -> StableId`, a canonical RNG-free bit-pack in a NEW reserved band `SEQUENCE_SUBJECT_BASE = (1<<62)|(1<<61)` (bit 61 discriminates from the feature band `1<<62`), the wildcard-template belief key. `EligibilityTrace` (per-being `BTreeMap<StableId,Fixed>`, record→ONE, decay by the TD lambda + prune, `hash_into` folding `write_u64(subject.0)+write_fixed`), empty-by-default. Proven by sequence-subject canonical/disjoint + trace record/decay/prune/fold tests.

SLICE 1c (WIRE, opt-in): route delayed reward credit through the shipped Observe path. `locomotion.rs`: Walker carries `eligibility_trace` + transient `decided_affordance` (the head of the sequence, recorded each tick after `layout.decide`). `runner.rs`: `couple_conversation`, when the reward learner is armed (`Runner::set_reward_learning`, `reward_learning: Option<RewardLearningCalib>`), credits every eligible sequence toward REWARDS or NEUTRAL by the sign of the being's own reserve delta beyond the recovery floor (`is_reward_tick`), scaled by the eligibility factor and the heritable plasticity, emitted as `Stimulus::Observe{attr: REWARD_ATTR}` at `REWARD_LEARN_ORDINAL_BASE = 2_000_000` and applied via `mind.consider` (the identical path harm uses); then decays and re-records the trace AFTER the credit read (the one-tick interoceptive lag), identical in the pinned and scheduled orders. The eligibility trace folds into `state_hash` empty-by-default after the reserve memory. SNAPSHOT-GATE SEAM caught and fixed: the reserve-memory snapshot (`locomotion.rs`) was gated only on percepts declared (the harm learner keys the delta to the feature underfoot); the reward learner keys it to the executed ACTION, so the gate was broadened to `!percepts.is_empty() || !w.eligibility_trace.is_empty()` (the trace is populated only when the reward learner is armed, so a run using neither learner still takes no snapshot and stays bit-identical). Milestone test `a_being_forms_the_reward_belief_that_its_action_pays_off_through_the_runner` (a hungry being on modest replenished energy-dense food commits the ingest-pays-off belief; falsifier: unarmed forms none).

SLICE 1d (READ then WIRE, opt-in): the belief-to-behaviour feedback, so a being REPEATS the rewarded action, the appetitive mirror of `avoidance_gradient`. READ: `learn::appetitive_salience(mind, affordances, params) -> Vec<Fixed>`, ONE per affordance the being holds a committed REWARDS belief about (the single-primitive sequence subject), the per-AFFORDANCE mirror of avoidance's committed-belief test. WIRE: `ControllerLayout` grows an opt-in APPETITIVE input block (one channel per affordance, canonical id order, after the feature block, before the bias, `with_percepts_and_appetitive`/`appetitive_input_base`/`affordance_ids`/`build_input_with_features_and_appetitive`); founder expresses zero for the new weights, so only selection turns "I believe this pays off" into "do it again" (Principle 9, the feature-block discipline). `Embodiment::set_appetitive` opts a world in and rebuilds the layout (composing with `set_percepts` via a shared `rebuild_layout`). The runner computes the salience per being (before the mutable borrow, no RNG) and threads it through `step_with_field_dirs` into the appetitive block. Milestone `a_being_that_learns_its_ingest_pays_off_repeats_it_past_the_point_a_naive_being_stops`: two beings run the identical hunger-driven controller with the same primed appetitive weight, differing only in reward armed/unarmed; the armed being learns and REPEATS INGEST 11 times to the naive control's 4, because the lit appetitive channel and its evolved weight keep INGEST winning past hunger's satiety point. The belief is transient by design (fades once full, ingesting-at-satiety wins no reserve), so the test records that it was ever held.

PIECE 2 (action-as-hypothesis) BEGUN: slice 2a (READ, hash-neutral, off the run path) landed the affordance-percept registry, the sibling of `PerceptRegistry`, in `crates/sim/src/affordance_percept.rs`. Where `PerceptRegistry` senses substance CLASSES underfoot, this senses the physics-derived AFFORDANCE scalars of nearby matter and the being's equipment. `AffordancePerceptKind` is the closed Rust kernel enum (the `CapabilityKernel` model); the membership of which scalars a world perceives is registry data (Principle 11); empty-by-default, opt-in, hash-neutral. TWO kernels landed: (1) `FracturePotential`, the graded percept of the extraction contest, a reserved actionable stress over the matter's own greatest fracture strength (`SubstanceMix::fracture_hardness`, `mat.fracture_strength`), clamped to [0,1]: weak matter high, hard rock low. (2) `Sharpness`, the Pierce capability score (`civsim_compose::CapabilityKernel::Pierce`) over the being's WIELDED TOOL geometry+material, positive for a keen edge, zero with no tool. AUDIT-THE-INPUT CATCH (resolved): the handoff scoped sharpness as "the Pierce score over nearby matter", but loose cell matter carries only MATERIAL axes (`mat.*`); a keen edge is a property of GEOMETRY, which a shaped tool carries (`WieldedTool::contact_area`, set by the knapping CRAFT contest) and a pile of matter does not, so sharpness reads the tool, not the ground. Reserved `affordance_percept.reference_stress` surfaced-with-basis in `calibration/reserved.toml` (the actionable stem, NOT the axis theoretical max, which discriminates nothing); the sharpness Pierce read reuses the compose `CapabilityRefs`. Proven by 4 tests. Slice 2a signed off (fracture-potential leg) and the sharpness leg pushed (commits 57b3912, 940a034).

STOPPED: slice 2a (fracture-potential + sharpness) pushed to PR #94, both legs gate-signed-off. NEXT in piece 2, GROUNDED and ready to build: slice 2b (the binding graph + the belief-biased sampler). Design settled during grounding, to build fresh:

- The PRIMITIVE VOCABULARY (`crates/sim/src/homeostasis.rs`): MOVE(0), INGEST(1), STRIKE(2), GRASP(3), EXTRACT(4), GEOPHAGE(5), CRAFT(6), DIG(7), RELEASE(8), SHELTER(9), each with an `AffordanceParam` (Scalar or Directional). A candidate action is a `[SequenceStep{primitive, target_bucket, param_bucket}]` (the 1b template step): the primitive, the quantized KIND of thing acted on, and the quantized HOW.
- The BINDING GRAPH is the GENERIC cartesian of (the body's AFFORDED primitives, `AffordanceRegistry::afforded`) x (the being's PERCEIVED affordance-typed targets, the slice-2a affordance percepts quantized by `feature_bucket`), with NO coded primitive->affordance table. The being proposes "do primitive P against a thing presenting affordance-bucket B" for the combinations, and the reward learner (piece 1) sorts which pay off, so a technique (chop = grasp(sharp), strike(fracturable)) EMERGES as a high-confidence belief PATH, never a recipe (Principle 8). This is the emergence-critical choice: keep it a generic combination, never an `if primitive == STRIKE { target = fracturable }` branch.
- KEY DESIGN DETAIL to get right: `SequenceStep::target_bucket` must encode the affordance CHANNEL plus its value bucket (e.g. `channel * K + value_bucket`), so "strike-a-fracturable-thing" and "strike-a-sharp-thing" mint DISTINCT `sequence_subject`s and thus distinct templates/beliefs. A bare value bucket would alias them.
- The BINDING GRAPH landed (slice 2b-1, commit 24bf610, `crates/sim/src/discovery.rs`): `candidate_bindings(afforded, percepts) -> Vec<SequenceStep>`, the generic cartesian, pure and RNG-free, off the run path, 650 lib tests green. TWO tests prove the no-coded-table cartesian, the value-blind channel target (one template per primitive-channel, distinct per channel), and the present-only bound.
- The SAMPLER landed (slice 2b-2, commit 79340c9): `discovery::sample_candidate(candidates, mind, calib, params, being, tick, seed) -> Option<SequenceStep>`, a belief-weighted roulette. Each candidate weights `Fixed::ONE` when the being holds a committed REWARDS belief about its `sequence_subject` (exploit) and the reserved `discovery.exploration_floor` otherwise (explore, mirroring `appetitive_salience`'s binary test). The draw is `DrawKey::entity(being, tick, Phase::HYPOTHESIZE).rng(seed).unit_fixed(0)`, counter-keyed under a NEW registered phase `HYPOTHESIZE = Phase(0x1C)` (`crates/core/src/keys.rs`, disjoint from EXPLORE). `discovery.exploration_floor` surfaced reserved-with-basis in `calibration/reserved.toml` (the gate's basis: the baseline probability of trying a non-top-belief binding, the bounded explore-vs-exploit floor). Pure and off the run path, so hash-neutral; the core Phase const is unused on the run path (byte-identical). Proven by 2 tests (believed dominates at a zero floor, the floor keeps exploring, deterministic + counter-keyed). SLICE 2b COMPLETE (binding graph + sampler), both signed off / pushed. (Optional later refinement: scale the weight by NEED, a reserve near its floor, and by the percept VALUE.)

SLICE 2c-1 LANDED (WIRE, opt-in, commit 4dea23b): the discovery loop RUNS on the run path. `Embodiment::set_affordance_percepts(registry, refs)` and `Runner::set_discovery(DiscoveryCalib)` arm it opt-in (the reward-learner Option pattern); `Walker::proposed_action: Option<SequenceStep>` holds the sampled hypothesis, folded into `state_hash` as its sequence subject after the eligibility trace, None-by-default (byte-identical unarmed). `step_embodiment`, when armed AND the affordance-percept + material registries are installed, computes each being's proposal BEFORE the mutable borrow (mirroring the appetitive-salience pass, no borrow conflict): perceives the matter underfoot + the tool in hand (`affordance_percept::perceive`), reads the afforded primitives (`afford.afforded`/`afforded_structure`, the SAME call locomotion makes), enumerates `candidate_bindings`, samples one via `sample_candidate` under the counter-keyed HYPOTHESIZE phase keyed on (being, tick); a mutable pass records `proposed_action`. Proven by an acceptance test (a being on fracturable granite with the registry installed and the loop armed proposes a deterministic, replay-identical candidate; an unarmed being proposes nothing; folding it changes the hash). 653 lib tests green, crucible unchanged, world_build 16 + world_determinism 3 replay-identical.

STOPPED: slice 2c-1 pushed and SIGNED OFF. NEXT and LAST of piece 2: slice 2c-2 (WIRE, opt-in, hash-changing), the ENACTMENT that closes the loop. GATE RESOLVED THE DESIGN QUESTION (its 2c-1 sign-off comment, the definitive ruling):

- DO NOT enact the proposal on a coded "controller idles, so try it" trigger. That AUTHORS "a being with nothing to do explores" AND makes a FOUNDER explore (its blank controller idles, so it would try random proposals), which BREAKS founder-zero. A founder must do nothing novel until selection lifts it.
- DO gate proposal-enactment on a HERITABLE exploration weight, FOUNDER-ZERO, the way every affordance is gated. A being enacts its `proposed_action` only when its own evolved exploration propensity fires. A founder (zero propensity) never tries a proposal and stays byte-identical. Exploration BOOTSTRAPS through mutation: a mutant with a non-zero propensity tries proposals, and if trying pays off, selection lifts the propensity across the lineage. Exploration EMERGES, it is not switched on.
- THE LOOP CLOSES ITSELF THROUGH PIECE 1, so 2c-2 stays minimal: a being explores (gated by the heritable propensity), enacts a proposal, feels the reserve delta, the reward learner credits the sequence via the eligibility trace, and the appetitive salience (piece 1, already wired) biases the CONTROLLER toward the now-believed-rewarding action. So exploration DISCOVERS an action and the controller INTERNALISES it: once a being has learned a sequence pays off, its controller does it without the exploration gate, and the gate is only ever the doorway to the untried. Propose, enact, feel, credit, internalise, all founder-zero.
- The enactment itself replaces the `_ => idle` catch-all in `locomotion.rs` (currently STRIKE and any primitive with no enactment) with a pure physics-law application gated on the DERIVED affordance, mechanism only never policy. Piece 3 (prediction-error / surprise) later makes the gate RESPONSIVE (explore more when surprised), a refinement of the same weight, not a new mechanism.

GROUNDING for the build (verified 2026-07-06): heritable traits express via `GeneSet::express(genome, channel, env) -> Fixed`, founder-zero by default (`genome.rs:377`); they mutate on inheritance under a keyed Phase (`Channel::Controller` weights mutate via `Phase::CONTROLLER`, `evolve.rs:260,298`). TWO implementation options for the heritable exploration propensity, pick after fuller grounding: (A) a NEW `Channel::Exploration` genome variant (a single-locus scalar, `genes.express` founder-zero, mutated via a new or reused Phase, expressed at the founder/newborn build and STORED on the Walker like `w.controller`, then read by the runner to gate enactment via a counter-keyed draw); or (B) an EXPLORE output on the controller layout (reuses the whole controller expression/storage/mutation machinery, founder-zero blank-controller weights, but the output vector lives in the locomotion step so the enactment-gate read must be threaded up like the appetitive block). Option A is cleaner-conceptually but heavier (new birth-expression + storage); option B reuses machinery but adds a non-affordance output and a thread-up. BIRTH-PATH GROUNDING (verified 2026-07-06): the Walker carries the EXPRESSED `controller` but NOT its genome, and controllers are expressed at the birth path `Controller::express(&race.genes, genome, &emb.layout)` (`runner.rs:3421`, where the genome is in scope). So option A expresses the exploration propensity right there (a new `Walker::exploration: Fixed`, founder-zero) and the enactment gate reads `w.exploration`. TESTABLE WITHOUT MUTATION FIRST (the 1d pattern): prove the gate mechanism with a PRIMED `w.exploration` (a founder-zero being reads zero and is byte-identical; a primed being enacts its proposal and the reward learner credits it), then a follow-on adds the `Channel::Exploration` mutation that makes it EVOLVE. ENACTMENT-ROUTING CAUTION: a proposed matter primitive (GRASP/EXTRACT) enacts via the runner's `deferred_actions` pass (`runner.rs:~3209`), INGEST enacts in the locomotion step, so injecting a fired proposal means adding it to `deferred_actions` and setting `decided_affordance` (so the eligibility trace credits it). STRIKE has no enactment yet and its physics-law arm wades into the flagged primitive-vs-verb debt (TODOs #60/#61, STRIKE vs EXTRACT vs CRAFT), so 2c-2 can enact proposals over the ALREADY-enacted primitives (GRASP/EXTRACT/INGEST) and leave STRIKE's arm for the consolidation. RESERVED value: the exploration propensity base (and/or its mutation scale), reserved-with-basis (the baseline rate a lineage tries the untried, founder-zero), fail-loud. Determinism: the enactment gate's draw is counter-keyed under a phase disjoint from HYPOTHESIZE; the heritable trait folds into the genome/state canonically; founder-zero => byte-identical. FLAG A SEAM if the minimal wiring authors anything. Keep building ahead; the gate reviews PR #94 and pings each landing.

COUPLING (firm, upstream): the affordance-binding half has thin matter to bind until more materials-arc enactment arms are exercised; INGEST is demonstrable today. Keep building ahead; the gate reviews PR #94 and pings each landing.

## 2026-07-06: IDEATION / experiential-discovery arc BEGUN, slice 1a (the appetitive reward learner, READ), on branch claude/ideation-discovery, PR #94

The material-substrate cascade arc merged to main (PR #93, `45090f8`, close-out passed: workspace green, three multi-generation sims crash-free and replay-identical). The owner directs the next arc: IDEATION / EXPERIENTIAL-DISCOVERY (`docs/working/IDEATION_DISCOVERY_HANDOFF.md`), the reward complement of harm-learning that makes the built (dormant, founder-zero) affordances get USED, on a NEW branch `claude/ideation-discovery` and a NEW PR #94 (the materials PR channel closed on merge). Four pieces: (1) the appetitive learner, (2) action-as-hypothesis, (3) forward model + prediction-error exploration, (4) belief-graph planning. Read-then-wire slice cadence.

SLICE 1a (READ, hash-neutral, RNG-free, the reward twin of the harm signal), first commit on the branch. `crates/sim/src/homeostasis.rs`: `is_reward_tick(delta, reward_noise_floor) = delta > 0 && delta > reward_noise_floor`, the exact SIGN COMPLEMENT of `is_harm_tick`. `crates/sim/src/learn.rs`: `REWARD_ATTR = AttrKindId(u32::MAX - 3)` disjoint from `HARM_ATTR` (u32::MAX - 2); `REWARDS`/`NEUTRAL` mirror `HARMS`/`BENIGN`; `RewardLearningCalib` the exact mirror of `HarmLearningCalib` (from_manifest fail-loud, dev_default, observation_weight, certainty_clamp reusing evidence.log_odds_clamp); `reward_observations` a clone of `feature_observations` with `toward = REWARDS`. `calibration/reserved.toml`: the four reward reserved values, each the sign-mirror of a harm value, reserved-with-basis, no value fabricated. Off the run path (from_manifest uncalled until slice 1c), so byte-identical (world_build 16, world_determinism 3 replay-identical). Proven by 5 tests (sign-complement + never co-fire; observation points toward REWARDS/NEUTRAL; a mind commits a REWARDS belief on its own; a reward belief and a harm belief coexist on one subject via disjoint attrs). 638 sim lib tests plus integration binaries green, fmt (--all --check) and clippy (--all-targets -D warnings) clean.

STOPPED: slice 1a pushed, PR #94 opened. NEXT per the handoff: slice 1b (re-key the belief subject from the standing-on feature to the executed primitive SEQUENCE, in a new reserved band below 1<<63; add the eligibility trace that carries delayed credit, folding into state_hash empty-by-default), then slice 1c (WIRE, opt-in: route delayed credit through the shipped Observe path, `mind.consider` on the runner path harm uses, the arc's headline milestone: a being learns an affordance pays off and REPEATS it). KEY COUPLING: piece 1 is demonstrable on INGEST today (it already enacts and raises a reserve); crediting grasp/extract/etc. rides the materials enactment arms just merged. The gate reviews PR #94 and pings each landing.

## 2026-07-06: material-substrate CASCADE ARC COMPLETE (item 7 overhead-deposit technique + the force-and-manipulation primitive-completeness check), on branch claude/material-substrate, PR #93

THE MATERIAL-SUBSTRATE CASCADE ARC IS COMPLETE. The last two pieces landed: item 7's emergent build (a being raises its own roof) and the force-and-manipulation completeness check (the arc's last piece). Granular landings in `docs/working/CONSENSUS_ROADMAP.md` (the ITEM 7 OVERHEAD-DEPOSIT TECHNIQUE and FORCE-AND-MANIPULATION PRIMITIVE-COMPLETENESS CHECK paragraphs). Commit `30a9ef8` (overhead-deposit) plus this docs landing. Ready to signal the gate for the close-out (final tests plus a few full sims, then merge).

OVERHEAD-DEPOSIT TECHNIQUE (`30a9ef8`, item 7 complete): a being sets the insulating matter it carries down into the cell directly OVERHEAD (a new SHELTER affordance, AffordanceId 9, builder fixture only), so the roof it raises is one it CHOSE to build, and that self-built roof attenuates its own body-to-field thermal exchange (slice A's shelter read). Shelter EMERGES from need plus the deposit affordance plus carried matter, no shelter verb. `Embodiment::deposit_overhead` is the upward sibling of `release_underfoot` (no earthwork rises, the matter is a roof in the air); the locomotion decision layer defers a winning SHELTER, the enactment calls deposit_overhead. Proven (`a_being_builds_its_own_roof_overhead_and_that_self_built_roof_shelters_it`): a SHELTER-deciding being raises its oak as a roof and is buffered over a cold field while a blank founder that never builds cools. Opt-in / hash-neutral (fixture-only, crucible 254bc17c unchanged).

FORCE-AND-MANIPULATION PRIMITIVE-COMPLETENESS CHECK (the owner's addendum, reframed by the steering discipline "primitives coded, techniques emergent" into a completeness check, not a verb-build list). Delivered as its finding (the honest output of a check). The audited manipulation surface: ACQUIRE (GRASP, EXTRACT, DIG, GEOPHAGE), PLACE (RELEASE, SHELTER), CONSUME (INGEST, GEOPHAGE), SHAPE (CRAFT), DAMAGE (STRIKE), each body-grounded and physics-gated, so each a correctly-coded PRIMITIVE. The gap-list classifies: WEAVE/DAM/MIX/STACK/CACHE/GIVE/FORGE/FERMENT/CAST are TECHNIQUES that EMERGE from sequencing primitives (the arc proves the pattern: terracing from dig+release, the mining loop from mine+craft+mine, self-built shelter from carry+deposit-overhead), so none is coded. FINDING: the arc's manipulation primitive set is COMPLETE, techniques emerge from it, no new verb needed to land the arc. The one genuine PRIMITIVE gap is the FORCE-APPLICATION family (PUSH/DRAG/ROLL un-acquired matter, THROW a projectile, LEVER, STRIKE-WITH-A-HELD-OBJECT the owner's top pick, STRIKE reading only the natural weapon today), body-grounded and physics-gated but needing a facing, cross-cell matter motion or projectile travel, and a force-versus-resistance contest, so a coherent FOLLOW-ON ARC, named and scoped not faked. Three CONSOLIDATION DEBTS are the walk-back toward emergence (direction of travel, not blockers): the DEPOSIT verbs (RELEASE, SHELTER, put-down) collapse into one DEPOSIT primitive with a directional parameter (the gate's twice-flagged note, AffordanceParam::Directional already exists); DIG and EXTRACT unify into one ground-working primitive with a selective-versus-bulk parameter (TODOS #60); CRAFT emerges from strike-a-core (TODOS #61).

STOPPED: the cascade arc is complete and being recorded. NEXT: signal the gate to run the close-out (the whole-arc BLIND concept-verify plus a few full sims, then merge PR #93 to main). The three consolidation debts (#58 finding, #60, #61) and the force-application follow-on arc are the material-substrate direction of travel after the merge. Merge only on the gate's close-out sign-off.

## 2026-07-06: material-substrate item 8 PROPER decomposition arc COMPLETE (per-substance params + slice C1 located deposit + slice C2 fertilisation, the matter cycle CLOSED), on branch claude/material-substrate, PR #93

THE MATTER CYCLE IS CLOSED. Three landings this session took decomposition from a scalar sink to a closed loop into the food web: dead matter rots, its mass enriches the ground it fell on by nutrient class (conserved exactly), and that enrichment feeds producer productivity. The gate signed off slice B and each follow-on as it caught up; the standing build-ahead is in force. Granular landings in `docs/working/CONSENSUS_ROADMAP.md` (the ITEM 8 PROPER, SLICE C1, and SLICE C2 paragraphs). Commits `44d2c57` (per-substance params), `a92bdcc` (slice C1) + `caf2b2f` (fmt fix), `877ca09` (slice C2).

WHAT LANDED, in order:
1. PER-SUBSTANCE DECOMPOSITION PARAMS (`44d2c57`, the gate's slice-B direction note): the decomposition barrier and rate move from a GLOBAL calib to the substance's OWN physics-floor axes. The barrier is a per-substance CITED biology-floor axis (`bio.decomposition_barrier`, carrion 273 K, cited to the freezing point of its tissue water), read per-substance with NO global fallback (the barrier is the substance's physical gate). The rate is a per-substance biology-floor axis (`bio.decomposition_rate`, reserved fantasy-provenance timescale); no substance declares one yet, so the matter cycle falls back to the global reserved `MatterCycleCalib.decomposition_rate` (now the calib's only rate field). No value fabricated, behaviour-preserving (carrion's floor values equal the retired calib's), biology floor now 22 axes (floors count test updated).
2. SLICE C1, THE LOCATED DEPOSIT (`a92bdcc`): the decomposed mass re-materialises into a per-cell `SoilNutrientField` (a sparse per-cell per-class MASS store in `material.rs`, sibling to `FireField`, opt-in empty-default), located at the rot cell and split by the substance's own composition (mineral-ash fraction to a mineral class, remainder to an organic class). Mass-valued so the deposit is EXACT (no volume rounding); the organic share is the REMAINDER so mineral + organic equals the loss bit for bit. The `Embodiment.decomposed_mass` scalar becomes `soil: SoilNutrientField` (`decomposed_mass()` returns `soil.total()`); folds into `state_hash` only when non-empty. `sum(cell masses) + soil.total()` invariant.
3. SLICE C2, THE FERTILISATION (`877ca09`): `EnvironFields` gains a per-cell fertility vector filled from the soil store by `set_fertility_from` (scaled by a reserved `MatterCycleCalib.fertility_scale`, basis: the decomposed mass that raises a soil-limited cell's soil supply by one soil requirement; dev-fixture 1/1000, opt-in, no manifest key). `step_productivity` reads `soil_baseline + fertility` as the soil Liebig supply (the `soil_baseline` was a flagged placeholder until a per-cell soil field lands; this is that field, sourced by decomposition). The runner fills the fertility from the embodiment's soil store before `env.step`, only when the matter cycle is armed. Fertility is a derived read (not folded into `state_hash`; its effect enters via the capacity it shifts).

OPT-IN and hash-neutral throughout: every landing runs only when the matter cycle is armed, so the baseline is byte-identical (crucible `254bc17c`, world_build 16, world_determinism 3 replay-identical across all three). PROVEN: the conservation test asserts the located, class-split, exact-conserving deposit; a new environ test (`decomposed_soil_fertility_lifts_productivity_where_soil_is_the_limiting_factor`) shows a fertilised cell's capacity rise above zero under a soil-limited calibration while an equally-viable unfertilised cell stays barren. 819 sim tests plus 84 physics tests green, fmt (`--all --check`) and clippy (`--all-targets -D warnings`) clean.

PROCESS NOTE: `a92bdcc` passed my local `-p civsim-sim` fmt/clippy but failed CI's `cargo fmt --all --check` (a new test assertion exceeded the line width); `caf2b2f` fixed it. LESSON, now standing: run `cargo fmt --all --check` (whole workspace, no `-p`, no pipe that masks the exit code) before every push.

HONEST LIMITS: (1) fertility raises the soil supply CATALYTICALLY, the abstract Liebig source is not mass-conserved through plant uptake (the productivity model's existing limit), so the deposited nutrient is not consumed as producers grow; a mass-conserved uptake is a follow-on. (2) The organic share stays in the soil store; volatilising it to the air (a gas decomposition vents) is a follow-on refinement of the split. (3) CORROSION, the harder transform sibling (its oxide product is a substance-to-substance mapping not derivable from a Fixed axis, and it gains environmental oxygen so it conserves the metal ELEMENT not total mass), is not yet built.

STOPPED: decomposition matter cycle closed and pushed. NEXT: corrosion (the harder transform), the deferred overhead-deposit technique (a being builds a roof), the force-and-manipulation completeness check, then the whole-arc BLIND concept-verify and the gate's sign-off before merge PR #93 to main. Merge only at the very end on the gate's sign-off of the whole arc.

## 2026-07-06: material-substrate item 8 PROPER slice B (the matter cycle, decomposition, mass conserved exactly), on branch claude/material-substrate, PR #93

Item 8 proper's first observable, built exactly mass-conserving: a cell's organic matter decomposes over time when warm, and the total mass is invariant. The gate signed off slice A ("Keep building ahead: slice B and the rest of item 8"), and the standing build-ahead is in force. Granular landing in `docs/working/CONSENSUS_ROADMAP.md` (the ITEM 8 PROPER SLICE B paragraph). Commit `07e065b`.

WHAT LANDED: `crates/sim/src/runner.rs` gains `Runner::step_matter_cycle`, a beat inside `step_field` (so the pinned and scheduled tick orders both decompose against the settled temperature identically). For each cell whose substance carries `bio.mineral_ash_fraction` (the mark of organic matter, read from the substance's OWN composition physics, no tag and no race, Principles 8/9/11) with the cell temperature at or above the reserved decomposition barrier, the beat removes a reserved rate-fraction of that substance's volume and moves its EXACT per-substance mass decrease (the `MaterialField`'s own rounded `mass` reads, so their difference is exact) into a scalar `decomposed_mass: Fixed` sink on the `Embodiment`, matter dispersed to the environment. `Runner::set_matter_cycle` arms it opt-in; `Embodiment.decomposed_mass` and its accessor are new; the sink folds into `state_hash` only when non-zero.

DATA and RESERVED: `crates/physics/src/lib.rs` `ground()` now loads the ground floor LAST (after biology) so a z-column substance can carry cross-domain axes; a cited "carrion" substance (`mat.density`, `bio.mineral_ash_fraction`, USDA / food-composition data) is added to `ground_floor.toml` as the decomposable organic matter; `crates/sim/src/material.rs` `MatterCycleCalib` carries the reserved decomposition barrier (basis: the freezing point of biological water near 273 K, below which decomposition halts) and rate (basis: the decomposition timescale), surfaced-with-basis, dev-fixture only. No value invented.

CONSERVATION is EXACT by construction: the conserved projection is `sum(cell masses) + sink`, and what leaves a cell (the rounded per-substance mass decrease) exactly enters the sink, so there is no product-volume rounding. The `ConservationRegistry` (register `matter_mass = sum(cell masses) + sink`) sees the total invariant across many ticks in the test.

OPT-IN and hash-neutral: the beat runs only when the matter cycle is armed, so a scenario with none armed is byte-identical (crucible `254bc17c`, world_build 16 and world_determinism 3 replay-identical). Proven end to end (`physiology_embodiment.rs` `organic_matter_decomposes_when_warm_and_the_matter_cycle_conserves_mass`): warm carrion rots down while a frozen cell is preserved, the `ConservationRegistry` sees the total mass invariant, and the scheduled order matches the pinned. 818 sim tests plus 84 physics tests green, fmt and clippy (`--all-targets -D warnings`) clean, prose customs clean.

HONEST LIMIT: the decomposed mass disperses to a scalar sink (its gases and leached minerals leave the cell); re-materialising it into soil and air substances (the ash fraction to a mineral residue, the volatile to the `air` substance, which reintroduces the volume-rounding and needs a residual-mass mechanism) is slice C.

STOPPED: slice B pushed (`07e065b`), docs recorded. NEXT: slice C (the product re-materialization into soil and air), then corrosion (the harder sibling: its oxide product is a substance-to-substance mapping and it conserves the metal element not total mass), the deferred overhead-deposit technique (a being builds a roof), and the force-and-manipulation primitive-completeness check. Acceptance is a BLIND concept-verification on a run log at the matter-cycle milestone. Merge PR #93 to main only at the very end on the gate's sign-off of the whole arc.

---

## 2026-07-06: material-substrate item 8 PROPER slice A (the world registry carries the transform physics), on branch claude/material-substrate, PR #93

Item 8 proper (the matter cycle) begins: its foundational slice A, the registry integration that makes the transform physics available in the world material registry. The gate green-lit item 8 proper (agreed the read-substance-physics direction, no transform-kinds file). Granular landing in `docs/working/CONSENSUS_ROADMAP.md` (the ITEM 8 PROPER SLICE A paragraph). Commit `bfd6022`.

WHAT LANDED: `crates/physics/src/lib.rs` `PhysicsRegistry::ground()` now loads the full physics floor stack (fluids, chemistry/optics, electromagnetism, biology) onto the mechanical and ground floors, so a cell's substances carry the transform physics: corrosion electrode potentials and susceptibility (`chem.standard_potential`, `chem.corrosion_susceptibility`, `chem.acidity`), the combustion products' ash fraction (`bio.mineral_ash_fraction`), and the ambient fluids (`air`). The matter cycle reads a transform's parameters straight from the substance's own floor axes (the "dissolve into the floor" direction), not a transform-kinds file.

ADDITIVE / neutral: the material derivations read specific axes by id, so a fuller registry leaves them unchanged, and worldgen fills the z-column from the ground floor's strata alone. Verified byte-identical (physiology_embodiment 23, world_build 16, world_determinism 3, full sim suite 817, crucible unchanged; 84 physics tests green with the composed stack, no id conflicts). fmt and clippy (`--all-targets -D warnings`) clean, no new reserved value.

KEY GROUNDING for slice B (the transform application, the matter cycle's first observable): the biology and chem floors define transform AXES and LAWS but no SUBSTANCES (substances live in the mechanical and ground floors), and critically the transform PRODUCTS (what matter becomes) are a substance-to-substance relation, NOT a Fixed axis. DECOMPOSITION resolves this cleanly and is the right first transform: its products EMERGE from the substance's own composition physics (the `bio.mineral_ash_fraction` stays as a mineral residue, the organic fractions volatilize to the `air` substance), so it honors read-substance-physics for both params AND products, and it conserves TOTAL mass by splitting (no environmental inflow). It needs a new organic substance with cited composition, a decomposition rate/barrier (reserved-with-basis or a substance thermal axis), the beat, the composition-derived product routing, and `ConservationRegistry` mass balance. CORROSION is the harder sibling (its oxide product is a substance-to-substance mapping not derivable from a Fixed axis, and it gains environmental oxygen so it conserves the metal ELEMENT, not total mass), a follow-on.

SLICE B DESIGN REFINED (two frictions grounded and resolved, for a clean build): (1) EXACT CONSERVATION vs VOLUME-QUANTIZED STORAGE. The `ConservationRegistry` is bit-exact (i128 totals), but the `MaterialField` stores VOLUMES and mass is `volume * density`, so depositing a product by volume (`volume = mass/density`, floored) ROUNDS, and mass cannot be split exactly across different-density products. RESOLUTION for slice B: do NOT deposit products by volume yet; instead move the EXACT per-substance Fixed-mass DECREASE (old substance mass minus new substance mass, both the `MaterialField`'s own rounded `mass` reads, so their difference is exact) into a scalar `decomposed_mass: Fixed` SINK on the Embodiment (matter dispersed to the environment, folded into `state_hash` only when non-zero so it stays byte-identical). The conserved projection is then `sum(cell masses) + sink = const`, EXACT by construction (what leaves a cell exactly enters the sink, no product-volume rounding). The re-materialization of the sink into soil and air substances (the product routing, which reintroduces the volume-rounding and needs a residual-mass mechanism) is SLICE C. (2) FLOOR-LOAD ORDERING. An organic substance referencing `bio.mineral_ash_fraction` cannot live in `mechanical_floor` (loaded before `biology_floor`, so the axis is undefined at its load); it needs a floor loaded AFTER biology (reorder `ground_floor` to load LAST in `ground()`, add a cited "carrion" substance there with `mat.density` and `bio.mineral_ash_fraction`), and must not break any standalone single-floor validation test (check `quantities.rs` load-order tests first). SLICE B THEN: a `MatterCycleCalib` (reserved decomposition barrier, basis biological-water freezing ~273 K below which decomposition halts; reserved rate, basis the decomposition timescale) armed opt-in via `set_matter_cycle`; a `step_matter_cycle` beat that, for each cell substance carrying `bio.mineral_ash_fraction` (organic matter) with the cell temperature at or above the barrier, removes a `rate`-fraction of its volume and adds the exact mass decrease to the sink; a `ConservationRegistry` test (register `matter_mass = sum(cell masses) + sink`, check before/after the beat is equal) plus a run test (warm cell decomposes, frozen cell preserved, unarmed runner byte-identical).

STOPPED: slice A pushed (`bfd6022`), signed off; slice B designed and resolved (above). NEXT: slice B (the decomposition-to-sink transform, exact-conserved), then slice C (product re-materialization into soil and air), the deferred overhead-deposit technique (a being builds a roof), and the force-and-manipulation primitive-completeness check. Open emergence debts unchanged. Acceptance is a BLIND concept-verification on a run log. Merge PR #93 to main only at the very end on the gate's sign-off of the whole arc.

## 2026-07-06: material-substrate item 8 slice 1 (harden DecayLaw to the data-defined TransformKind), on branch claude/material-substrate, PR #93

Item 8 (the matter cycle) has its first slice, the owner-ruled seam hardening, built hash-neutral. The gate signed off item 6 slice B first, completing item 6 (live fire) end to end (A burns, B needs air, C spreads and burns out). Granular landing in `docs/working/CONSENSUS_ROADMAP.md` (the ITEM 8 SLICE 1 paragraph). Commit `5577d07`.

WHAT LANDED: `crates/sim/src/trace.rs` replaces the closed `DecayLaw` enum (Organic / Corroding / Static arms) with a data-defined `TransformKind`: a general `TransformKernelId` (the FIXED kernel set, Reaction / Corrosion / Static, each a general physical law in `civsim_physics::laws`, never a named transform) plus a params map keyed by name (the reserved values as data). A new transform mode (combustion, cooking, smelting) is now a new DATA ROW binding an existing kernel, never a new enum arm, sibling to the value/semantic/institution substrates (Principle 11). The two consumers (`organic_salience`, `corroding_salience`) dispatch by kernel id and read named params. `lib.rs` export updated (`TransformKind`, `TransformKernelId` replace `DecayLaw`). Note: `DecayLaw` is unrelated to `language::SalienceDecayLaw`, which is untouched.

HASH-NEUTRAL: `dev_default` and the tests build the same three transforms, so the salience is byte-identical (trace tests, world_build 16 and world_determinism 3 all replay-identical). The north star (owner ruling) is recorded in the type doc: the dispatch dissolves into the floor once transforms emerge from a substance's own physics. Proven (`a_new_transform_mode_is_a_data_row_over_the_fixed_kernel_not_a_new_arm`): an organic-decomposition row and a hotter combustion-like row both bind the one fixed Reaction kernel, differing only in data. 817 sim tests plus 84 physics tests green, fmt and clippy (`--all-targets -D warnings`) clean, no new reserved value.

CONFIRMED DIRECTION (gate asked, I answered, PR #93 comment): the transform-kind rows stay THIN kernel bindings; the parameters come from the substance data the transform acts on, NOT a separate transform-kinds data file (that would fork a second home for numbers already on the substance and move away from the north star). Two consumers differ by what they act on: TRACES (corpse, bloodstain) carry trace-kind reserved params because a trace is not a single physics substance (done as-is); the MATTER CYCLE reads each substance's OWN physics-floor axes (Reaction reads `therm.ignition_temperature` as the barrier and the reaction data; Corrosion reads `chem.standard_potential`, `chem.corrosion_susceptibility`, `chem.acidity`), so world variation lives in the substance rows and the transform kind carries only the kernel id. That is "dissolve into the floor" made concrete.

STOPPED: item 8 slice 1 pushed (`5577d07`), signed off; direction confirmed to the gate. GROUNDED the matter cycle for the next build: `ConservationRegistry<W>` already exists (`crates/sim/src/conservation.rs`, a generic before/after mass guard). The transform params are on the floor (ignition/reaction for Reaction; `chem.standard_potential`/`chem.corrosion_susceptibility`/`chem.acidity` for Corrosion, chem_optics_floor.toml). THE OPEN DESIGN PIECE (the matter cycle's crux): the mass-conserving PRODUCT ROUTING, what matter becomes when it transforms, is NOT yet in the floor as a product mapping. GROUNDED basis found: `bio.mineral_ash_fraction` (biology_floor.toml, cited, ~1.0 for bone) gives the organic split, the mineral fraction stays as ash residue and the volatile `1 - ash` goes to an air/gas class, so organic decomposition/combustion is groundable; metal corrosion's oxide product has no floor axis yet (a product model to design or a small flag). NEXT (item 8 proper, the MATTER CYCLE, the arc's most complex subsystem, kept for a fresh focused build): a beat applying the transform kinds to a cell's MaterialField substances over time, each reading the substance's OWN physics, the removed mass ROUTED to its product classes (ash residue plus a volatile air class via the ash fraction) and BALANCED in the `ConservationRegistry` (the guard a feature: a leak stops the run). Then the deferred overhead-deposit technique (a being builds a roof) and the force-and-manipulation primitive-completeness check. Open emergence debts unchanged. Acceptance is a BLIND concept-verification on a run log (Section B verbatim code). Merge PR #93 to main only at the very end on the gate's sign-off of the whole arc.

## 2026-07-06: material-substrate item 6 slice B (oxygen gate, item 6 COMPLETE) + item-8 owner-call RULED, on branch claude/material-substrate, PR #93

Item 6 (live fire) is now COMPLETE end to end, and the item-8 owner-call was surfaced, praised, and RULED. Roadmap landings: the ITEM 6 SLICE B OXYGEN GATE and ITEM 8 OWNER-CALL RULED paragraphs.

ITEM 6 SLICE B (oxygen gate, commit `e651759`): the combustion beat reads the OXIDISER the cell's medium supplies (the reserved `oxidiser_supply` times the medium's `respirable_at`) and passes it to `laws::combustion`, so an oxygen-demanding fuel burns in air and starves in a sealed or anoxic cell, while a self-oxidising fuel burns on temperature alone; a cell with no medium field reads open atmosphere (so the earlier combustion scenarios are unchanged). Oak gained its cited `therm.oxidiser_demand` (1.2), `CombustionCalib` gained the reserved `oxidiser_supply`. Opt-in / hash-neutral (crucible `254bc17c`, world_build/world_determinism identical). Proven (`an_oxygen_demanding_fire_burns_in_air_and_starves_in_an_anoxic_medium`). 816 sim + 84 physics tests green. Item 6 is COMPLETE: burns when hot (A), needs air (B), spreads and burns out (C).

THE ITEM-8 CATCH AND RULING. Auditing my own handoff against the source before building item 8, I caught that I had mis-written it as "owner-ruled" when `MATERIAL_SUBSTRATE_HANDOFF.md:141` still flagged it UNRESOLVED (Prime Directive 1). I corrected the docs, surfaced the owner-call (not resolved) with a recommendation via a PR comment, and kept building the independent oxygen gate. The owner CONFIRMED the catch was right ("you were right to distrust the framing... appreciated"), noted the endorsement had lived only in a memory/relay not the docs, and RULED, formalized in the source (commit `6e4aee7`). THE RULING: harden the closed `DecayLaw` enum (`trace.rs:77`) to a data-defined `TransformKindRegistry` keyed to a physics-kernel id, sibling to the value/semantic/institution substrates; each kernel a GENERAL physical law (oxidation, phase-change, biological-reduction), NEVER a named-transform arm (no combustion/cooking/decay arm); decomposition, corrosion, combustion, cooking, smelting are DATA ROWS over the fixed general-kernel set; north star, a thin dispatch that could later DISSOLVE into the floor once transforms emerge from the substance's own physics. Mass conservation a hard `ConservationRegistry` invariant across every transform.

GROUNDED ITEM-8 PLAN (for the next turn, the last cascade item, now UNBLOCKED). `DecayLaw` (`trace.rs:77`) has three arms: `Organic{barrier, decomposition_rate}` dispatching to `laws::reaction`; `Corroding{material_potential, susceptibility, acidity, corrosion_max}` dispatching to `laws::corrosion`; `Static` (no decay). Consumers `organic_salience` (`trace.rs:246`) and `corroding_salience` (`trace.rs:297`) match the arms to pull params. HARDENING SLICE 1 (hash-neutral): replace `DecayLaw` with a `TransformKind{ kernel: TransformKernelId, params: BTreeMap<String,Fixed> }` (or a params accessor), a fixed `TransformKernelId` set (Reaction, Corrosion, Static, extensible for phase-change / biological-reduction) as the FIXED kernel Rust, the membership and params DATA; rewrite the two consumers to dispatch by `kernel` id and read params from the row; keep `dev_default` producing the same three kinds so the salience values are byte-identical (trace tests unchanged). THEN the matter cycle proper: transforms apply to the MaterialField's substances over time (decomposition/corrosion/combustion as data rows over the general kernels), conserving mass in the `ConservationRegistry` (every removal/deposit/burn/decay balances; combustion's volatile fraction sinked to an air class). Acceptance is a BLIND concept-verification on a run log (Section B verbatim code, per memory).

STOPPED: oxygen gate + docs pushed (`e651759` and the doc commits); item 8 unblocked and grounded. THIS TURN built FIVE code increments (item 5 hydrology, item 6 A/B/C, item 7 A shelter) plus a citation fix and the Prime-Directive-1 catch that led to the owner formalizing the item-8 ruling. NEXT: item 8 slice 1 (the `DecayLaw` to `TransformKindRegistry` hardening, hash-neutral) then the matter cycle; plus the deferred overhead-deposit technique (a being builds a roof) and the force-and-manipulation primitive-completeness check. Open emergence debts unchanged. Merge PR #93 to main only at the very end on the gate's sign-off of the whole arc.

## 2026-07-05: material-substrate item 7 slice A (shelter: insulating matter buffers a being), on branch claude/material-substrate, PR #93

Item 7 (shelter) has its first slice, the fourth increment of this turn. A being under a roof of insulating matter is now buffered from a harsh field. Granular landing in `docs/working/CONSENSUS_ROADMAP.md` (the ITEM 7 SLICE A SHELTER paragraph). Commit `46bcaca`.

WHAT LANDED: `phase_body_exchange` (`crates/sim/src/runner.rs`) reads the matter in the air cells above a being (z >= 1 in its column), derives that matter's thermal RESISTANCE (volume over conductivity, summed), and divides the being's exchange rate by one plus that resistance times the reserved coupling (the series-thermal-resistance form), keyed off the substance's own conductivity with no shelter tag. Oak gained its cited `therm.conductivity` (0.17), and a `ShelterCalib` carries the reserved `insulation_coupling` (basis: the conduction-versus-convection resistance ratio; dev-fixture one-tenth), armed opt-in via `Runner::set_shelter`.

OPT-IN / hash-neutral: attenuation runs only when shelter is armed with enclosing matter present, so a scenario with no shelter or no overhead matter is byte-identical (crucible `254bc17c`, world_build 16 and world_determinism 3 replay-identical). Proven end to end (`a_roof_of_insulating_matter_shelters_a_being_from_a_harsh_field`): a being under an oak roof holds more of its warmth in a cold field than an exposed one, scheduled order bit-identical to pinned. 815 sim tests plus 84 physics tests green, fmt and clippy (`--all-targets -D warnings`) clean.

HONEST LIMIT: building the roof (a being placing matter overhead) is the deferred emergent technique; this proves the primitive. STOPPED: item 7 slice A pushed (`46bcaca`). THIS TURN built FOUR increments (item 5 hydrology coupling, item 6 slice A live fire, item 6 slice C heat-injection/spread, item 7 slice A shelter), each gate-quality, committed, pushed, documented; the gate signed off item 5 hydrology, item 6 slice A, and item 6 (complete, slice C).

CORRECTION (audit of my own handoff against the source, Prime Directive 1): earlier entries of mine wrote item 8 as "IMPLEMENT the owner-ruled data-defined `TransformKindRegistry`". That is WRONG framing that crept in. The source (`docs/working/MATERIAL_SUBSTRATE_HANDOFF.md:141`) states item 8's core is an OWNER CALL explicitly flagged per workflow 5b and NOT resolved: whether the closed `DecayLaw` enum (`trace.rs:77`) should become a data-defined `TransformKindRegistry` keyed to a physics-kernel id (sibling to the value/semantic/institution substrates) so decomposition, corrosion, combustion, cooking, and smelting are data rows over the fixed kernel set. The owner has NOT ruled. Item 8's matter cycle is built ON that decision, so it must NOT be built until the owner rules. SURFACED to the owner this turn with a recommendation (harden to the registry, since a live matter cycle would otherwise grow a new `DecayLaw` enum arm per transform mode, the closed-enum-where-content-should-emerge defect Principle 8/11 forbids). Awaiting the ruling.

NEXT, building ahead (all independent of the item-8 owner-call): item 6 slice B (the medium-oxygen gate so fire needs air, reads the medium's respirable content as the oxidiser and enriches oak's `therm.oxidiser_demand` from cited data), the overhead-deposit technique that lets a being build the roof, and the force-and-manipulation extension as a primitive-completeness check. Item 8 (the matter cycle) waits on the owner's ruling on the `DecayLaw` to `TransformKindRegistry` seam. Open emergence debts unchanged. Merge PR #93 to main only at the very end on the gate's sign-off of the whole arc.
