## 2026-07-05: material-substrate item 6 slice C (fire spreads and burns out), on branch claude/material-substrate, PR #93

The emergent payoff of live fire, the slice the gate steered to after signing off slice A. Fire now SPREADS cell to cell and EXTINGUISHES when fuel runs out, both emergent. Granular landing in `docs/working/CONSENSUS_ROADMAP.md` (the ITEM 6 SLICE C HEAT INJECTION paragraph). Commit `973b8b2`.

WHAT LANDED: a burning cell raises its own temperature by the heat its combustion releases (`Field::add_heat` in `crates/sim/src/runner.rs`), that heat spreads through the ordinary temperature diffusion, and a neighbouring fuel cell whose temperature crosses its ignition gate catches, so spread and extinction fall out of the combustion gate over the diffused temperature with no coded spread rule. The beat computes the cell's temperature rise as the reserved heat-retention fraction of the released energy over the cell's heat capacity (volume-weighted density times specific heat, `/1000` for the kJ/J unit match, overflow-guarded). Oak gained its cited `therm.specific_heat` (1700) and `CombustionCalib` gained the reserved `heat_fraction` (basis: combustion efficiency and the radiative-and-convective loss fraction, dev-fixture one-tenth).

OPT-IN / hash-neutral: the injection runs only inside the combustion beat, so a scenario with no combustion armed never touches the temperature field and stays byte-identical (crucible `254bc17c`, world_build 16 and world_determinism 3 replay-identical). Proven end to end (`fire_spreads_along_a_fuel_row_and_burns_out_behind_it`): fire spreads down a fuel row, the source dims as its fuel depletes and drops out of the fire field when it burns out, scheduled order bit-identical to pinned. 814 sim tests plus 84 physics tests green, fmt and clippy (`--all-targets -D warnings`) clean.

HONEST LIMITS: the lumped single-cell thermal model runs hotter than a real gas-plume flame, and the reserved heat fraction with the field's diffusion and relaxation rates jointly set spread readiness (owner-calibrated; the test uses a labelled fire-timescale field calib with slow relaxation). STOPPED: slice C pushed (`973b8b2`). This turn built THREE increments (item 5 hydrology coupling, item 6 slice A live fire, item 6 slice C heat injection), each gate-quality, committed, pushed, documented; the gate signed off item 5 hydrology and item 6 slice A. NEXT, building ahead: item 6 slice B (the medium-oxygen gate so fire needs air, reads the medium's respirable content as the oxidiser and enriches oak's `therm.oxidiser_demand` from the cited data), then item 7 (shelter), item 8 (the matter cycle, the owner-ruled data-defined `TransformKindRegistry` keyed to physics kernels), and the force-and-manipulation extension as a primitive-completeness check. Open emergence debts unchanged. Merge PR #93 to main only at the very end on the gate's sign-off of the whole arc.

## 2026-07-05: material-substrate item 6 STARTED (slice A live fire: combustible matter burns when hot), on branch claude/material-substrate, PR #93

Item 6 (live fire) has its first slice: the resolved combustion law, already floor-built in civsim_physics, now runs in the world as a field process, so hot fuel burns. Granular landing in `docs/working/CONSENSUS_ROADMAP.md` (the ITEM 6 SLICE A LIVE FIRE paragraph). Commit `70c8fa4`.

WHAT LANDED: `crates/sim/src/material.rs` gains a `FireField` (a sparse `Coord3`-to-intensity sibling of `EarthworkField`) recording the combustion energy each burning cell releases, plus a `CombustionCalib` (the reserved burn rate and energy cap, surfaced-with-basis on the struct like `ExtractionParams`). `crates/sim/src/runner.rs`: a `Runner::step_combustion` beat, run inside `step_field` (so both tick orders source the fire from the settled temperature), scans the material field for combustible matter and combusts a cell holding a substance that carries `therm.fuel_value` and stands at or above its `therm.ignition_temperature` through `laws::combustion`, consuming a bounded fraction of its fuel and lighting the fire field; the burned volume is the law's own energy-over-fuel-value relation back through density, no coded rate. Armed opt-in via `Runner::set_combustion`.

OPT-IN / hash-neutral: the beat runs only when a combustion calib is armed with a material registry present, and the fire field folds into `state_hash` only when non-empty, so a scenario with no combustion or nothing hot enough burns nothing and stays byte-identical (crucible unchanged at `254bc17c`, world_build 16 and world_determinism 3 replay-identical). Proven by a `FireField` unit test and an end-to-end runner test (oak in a hot field burns and lights the fire field while granite in the same cell and the same oak in a cold field do not, both tick orders). 632 sim lib tests plus all integration binaries green, fmt and clippy (`--all-targets -D warnings`) clean.

HONEST LIMIT: the oxidiser is not supplied, so a self-oxidising fuel (oak, which declares no oxidiser demand) burns on the current data and an oxygen-demanding fuel reads oxidiser-limited to nothing. STOPPED: item 6 slice A pushed (`70c8fa4`). NEXT slices, building ahead per the standing directive: item 6 slice B (the medium-oxygen gate that makes fire need air), slice C (the heat injection that lets fire warm the cell and spread through the temperature diffusion, needs `therm.specific_heat` on the fuel and the energy-to-temperature conversion), then item 7 (shelter), item 8 (the matter cycle, the owner-ruled data-defined `TransformKindRegistry` keyed to physics kernels, never named-transform arms), and the force-and-manipulation extension as a primitive-completeness check. Open emergence debts unchanged (ground-working affordance consolidation; CRAFT-from-strike-a-core). Merge PR #93 to main only at the very end on the gate's sign-off of the whole arc.

## 2026-07-05: material-substrate item 5 COMPLETE (hydrology coupling: a dug pit pools water), on branch claude/material-substrate, PR #93

Item 5 (modifiable terrain) closes: the terrain a being reshapes now feeds the water physics, so a dug pit ponds and a mound sheds. Granular landing in `docs/working/CONSENSUS_ROADMAP.md` (the ITEM 5 HYDROLOGY COUPLING paragraph). Commit `284cc4e`.

WHAT LANDED: `crates/sim/src/environ.rs` now keeps the frozen worldgen elevation (a static input beside moisture and light, where before it was dropped after the one-time downhill precompute) and gains `recouple_terrain(&EarthworkField)`, which rebuilds the downhill routing from the EFFECTIVE elevation (worldgen base plus the per-column earthwork delta) by the same pure fold (`compute_downhill`) that seeded it, so a dug pit that drops a cell below its neighbours becomes a basin that ponds its water and its salt, and a mound sheds them. A new `is_basin(x,y)` read exposes the routing. `crates/sim/src/runner.rs`: a `recouple_hydrology` beat rebuilds the routing after the embodiment step, called identically in the pinned (`step_inner`) and scheduled (`run_phase` SYS_EMBODIMENT) orders; the embodiment phase's `tick_systems` access now declares it writes `RES_FIELD` (the write reinforces the existing field-step serialization and changes no batch, so scheduled stays bit-identical to pinned).

CRUCIBLE-SAFE / OPT-IN: `recouple_terrain` skips an empty earthwork, so a non-digging run keeps the seeded worldgen routing byte-identical (the fold is pure, base plus zero equals base); only a reshaped column pays the rebuild. Crucible stays at `254bc17c`, world_build 16 and world_determinism 3 replay-identical. Proven three ways: an environ unit test (a plane whose interior drains until a dug centre becomes a basin its neighbours pond into, plus the empty-earthwork no-op guaranteeing byte-identity), the `is_basin` read, and an end-to-end runner test where a being digs through the FULL runner in both tick orders and its cell flips from draining to a basin while a blank being leaves the routing untouched (the test picks the map's minimum-margin non-basin cell so one realistic scoop tips it: a clean causal separation). 631 sim lib tests plus all integration binaries green, fmt and clippy (`--all-targets -D warnings`) clean, no new reserved value.

STOPPED: hydrology coupling pushed to PR #93 (`284cc4e`). Item 5 is done (dig lowers, release mounds, water finds the pit). NEXT in the cascade, building ahead per the owner's standing directive (gate catches up per slice): item 6 (live fire), item 7 (shelter), item 8 (the matter cycle, IMPLEMENTING the owner-ruled data-defined TransformKindRegistry keyed to physics kernels, never named-transform arms), and the force-and-manipulation extension as a primitive-completeness check (not a verb-build; techniques emerge). Open emergence debts (direction of travel, not blocking): consolidate EXTRACT/DIG/GRASP and RELEASE/put_down into one extract + one deposit primitive (elevation change a consequence), and let CRAFT/knapping emerge from strike-a-core physics. Merge PR #93 to main only at the very end on the gate's sign-off of the whole arc.

## 2026-07-05: material-substrate item 5 STARTED (earthwork field + DIG, terrain lowers); a CI -D-warnings blocker fixed, on branch claude/material-substrate

Item 5 (modifiable terrain) has its first observable: a being digs a pit. And a CI blocker was caught and fixed. Granular landings in `docs/working/CONSENSUS_ROADMAP.md` (the ITEM 5 SLICE A READ and SLICE B DIG paragraphs).

CI NOTE (process lesson): the item-5 READ half (`f6a290a`) went RED on CI. Root cause: CI runs `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` (deny warnings), and the earthwork test had `let mut h0` where `h0` is never mutated, an `unused_mut` that is a WARNING locally (so `cargo test -p civsim-sim` passed) but a DENIED ERROR in CI. Lesson for this repo: run `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo test -p civsim-sim --no-run` (or `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`) before pushing, because the local `cargo test`/`cargo clippy --lib` defaults do NOT deny warnings and will miss what CI fails on. Fixed (`let h0`) and bundled into the DIG push (`211643e`), which should turn CI green.

ITEM 5 SLICE A (READ): an `EarthworkField` (`material.rs`), a sparse per-column elevation-delta field keyed by the ground `Coord3`, a sibling of `MaterialField`, empty by default, off the run path, hash-neutral. `delta` reads zero for an unreworked column, `adjust` accumulates and prunes a column back at baseline, `hash_into` folds canonically. ITEM 5 SLICE B (DIG): a DIG affordance (`homeostasis.rs`, id 7, scalar) in a `dev_digger` fixture; the Embodiment carries the earthwork field (opt-in, folded into `state_hash` after the material layer); `Embodiment::dig_underfoot` excavates in the SAME extraction fracture contest (`extract_underfoot`, fracture gate plus spoil to carried) AND lowers the column by the volume removed, so a pit forms, the ground conserved between the carrier and the lowered terrain. Proven (`physiology_embodiment.rs a_being_digs_a_pit_lowering_the_terrain_only_through_an_evolved_weight`): a deciding being that can fracture the ground digs a pit (carried gains spoil, cell loses exactly that, column drops by exactly the excavated volume), a blank founder never digs. Founder-zero, physics-gated, opt-in and hash-neutral for existing scenarios, 633 lib tests plus all binaries green, fmt and clippy (`--all-targets -D warnings`) clean, no new reserved value.

STOPPED: DIG + the CI fix pushed to PR #93 (`211643e`); CI should go green. This session built ELEVEN code slices for the material arc (item-3 driver, item-4 extraction A/B/C, crafting seam, tool-in-extraction, geophagy, CRAFT, geophagy harm-half, item-5 earthwork READ, item-5 DIG), each committed, pushed, and gate-reviewed. NEXT, in order: (1) confirm CI green on `211643e`; (2) item 5 continued: the MOUND half (a deposit raising a column, `put_down` coupling to the earthwork) and the HYDROLOGY coupling (effective elevation = worldgen + delta recomputing the downhill target so a pit pools water and a mound sheds it), plus the ConservationRegistry and vertical z-adjacency the handoff names; (3) items 6-8 (live fire, shelter, the matter cycle with the owner-ruled data-defined `TransformKindRegistry`); (4) the force-and-manipulation extension (STRIKE-WITH-A-HELD-OBJECT and WEAVE the owner's top picks, then the rest, TODOS #58). Each milestone gets a blind concept-verify. Merge PR #93 only at the very end on the gate's sign-off of the whole arc.

## 2026-07-05: material-substrate item 4 GEOPHAGY HARM-HALF closed (a food that sickens); item 4 crafting+ingestion complete, on branch claude/material-substrate

The owner's ingestion seam is closed: eating now applies the toxin harm, not only the nutrient benefit. The granular landing is in `docs/working/CONSENSUS_ROADMAP.md` (the GEOPHAGY HARM-HALF paragraph). This completes item 4's crafting and ingestion work (extraction, the crafting seam, tool-in-extraction, geophagy, CRAFT, and now the harm-half), all gate-signed-off except the harm-half (just pushed, under review).

GEOPHAGY HARM-HALF: `Embodiment::geophage` (`runner.rs`) now tracks the substances it eats and, after the nutrient draw, harms the eater by each eaten substance's toxin concentration against its OWN inherited per-toxin-class tolerance and Hill exponent (`laws::net_harm` over the classes the being carries a tolerance for, read from each substance's registry vector, never its `mat.*` axes), the harm applied to CONDITION (a no-op with no CONDITION axis) so it feeds the EXISTING harm-learning loop. Symmetric with the exposure read: the same composition a being learns to AVOID standing on, it now feels when it EATS, per consumer, no per-substance poison label. Proven (`physiology_embodiment.rs eating_a_food_that_sickens_harms_the_sensitive_eater_and_spares_the_tolerant_one`): two beings differing only in salinity tolerance eat the same brackish food (a mineral nutrient laced with a salinity toxin); the salt-sensitive eater's CONDITION falls, the salt-tolerant one's holds, the same food read against each being's own tolerance. Opt-in and hash-neutral (`world_build`/`world_determinism` unchanged), 632 lib tests plus all binaries green, fmt and clippy clean, no new reserved value. HONEST LIMIT: the dose is the substance's toxin concentration (matching the exposure read), not yet scaled by amount eaten (a refinement); and the forage ingest's symmetric harm-half (the ResourceField path) rides the same pattern when wired, with care against double-counting the exposure harm that already applies the cell's toxins.

STOPPED: geophagy harm-half committed and pushed to PR #93 (`7b1a6bc`); the owner's seam is closed. This session built NINE code slices for the material arc (item-3 driver, item-4 extraction A/B/C, crafting seam, tool-in-extraction, geophagy, CRAFT, and the geophagy harm-half), each committed, pushed, and gate-reviewed. NEXT, in order: (1) the rest of the force-and-manipulation extension (STRIKE-WITH-A-HELD-OBJECT and WEAVE the owner's top picks, then THROW/PUSH/LEVER/DAM/FLOAT/BURROW/MIX/STACK/CACHE/GIVE, TODOS #58; most need a new target/object or combat mechanic, so each is a real slice); (2) the combined bonded-mineral mine-then-eat loop (a geophage-from-carried path or an EXTRACT that breaks a mineral loose IN the cell); (3) items 5-8 (item 5 modifiable terrain reuses the fracture contest and carried spoil, so it is the natural cleanest next big slice; then live fire, shelter, the matter cycle with the owner-ruled data-defined `TransformKindRegistry`). Each milestone gets a blind concept-verify. Merge PR #93 only at the very end on the gate's sign-off of the whole arc.

## 2026-07-05: material-substrate item 4 CRAFT/knapping closes the recursive tool loop; geophagy HARM-HALF seam flagged, on branch claude/material-substrate

CRAFT (knapping) is built, closing the recursive tool loop, and the owner flagged the symmetric harm-half of geophagy as the next slice. The granular CRAFT landing is in `docs/working/CONSENSUS_ROADMAP.md` (the CRAFT / KNAPPING paragraph).

CRAFT / KNAPPING: a being shapes its carried stone into a wielded tool, so the tool that multiplies extraction is MADE from mined stone, not fixture-set. A CRAFT affordance (`homeostasis.rs`, id 6, scalar, `requires: None`) in a `dev_toolmaker` fixture (move, ingest, extract, craft); a `CraftParams` struct (`material.rs`, opt-in via `set_craft_params`) carries the two reserved knapping values (`edge_area`, `tool_volume`); the `deferred_actions` path records CRAFT alongside GRASP/EXTRACT/GEOPHAGE; `Embodiment::craft_from_carried` consumes the reserved tool volume of the first carried substance (id order) and wields a tool of that substance with the reserved edge area; `Runner::embodiment_mut` exposes the write handle. Proven (`physiology_embodiment.rs`): the evolved-weight being shapes carried granite into a granite tool (founder-zero, a blank never crafts), and a being carrying flint that cannot fracture granite bare shapes a flint tool and, wielding it, breaks the granite (`a_crafted_tool_closes_the_loop_and_mines_rock_the_bare_body_cannot`, the recursive payoff end to end). Opt-in and hash-neutral (`world_build`/`world_determinism` unchanged), 631 lib tests plus all binaries green, fmt and clippy clean, two reserved values surfaced-with-basis. HONEST LIMIT: the tool geometry is a reserved fixture shape, not yet derived from the fracture of knapping (a refinement); CRAFT shapes the first carried substance by id order (a deterministic tie-break, the tool's derived capability lets selection favor the best stone without a preference in CRAFT).

OWNER SEAM FLAGGED (recorded TODOS #59): geophage and the forage ingest apply only `laws::satisfaction` (the nutrient benefit), never `edibility::assess` (the toxin harm), so eating a poison for a trace nutrient is free while standing on it harms, which is backwards, and the harm-learning arc's "food that sickens" case cannot happen yet. FIX (the harm-half of INGEST-FOR-COMPOSITION): route the ingest paths (geophage first, forage ingest for symmetry) through `edibility::assess` so the eaten substance's toxins harm the eater against its own inherited per-toxin-class tolerance (`laws::net_harm`), net of the nutrient, and the felt harm feeds the existing harm-learning loop. Integration surface: mapping an eaten SUBSTANCE (MaterialField volumes) to its toxin Composition (the substance registry vector's toxin-class axes; ground substances carry no toxin data yet). Does NOT unwind the geophagy sign-off; the benefit half is correct. Acceptance is a blind concept-verify (a being learns to avoid a food that sickens it while another with a different tolerance eats it safely).

STOPPED: CRAFT committed and pushed to PR #93 (`0e29c99`); the geophagy-harm seam is recorded as the prioritized next slice. NEXT, in order: (1) the geophagy HARM-HALF (the owner's just-flagged seam, TODOS #59); (2) the rest of the force-and-manipulation extension (STRIKE-WITH-A-HELD-OBJECT and WEAVE the owner's top picks, then THROW/PUSH/LEVER/DAM/FLOAT/BURROW/MIX/STACK/CACHE/GIVE, TODOS #58); (3) the combined bonded-mineral mine-then-eat loop (needs a geophage-from-carried path or an EXTRACT that breaks a mineral loose IN the cell); (4) items 5-8 (modifiable terrain, live fire, shelter, the matter cycle with the owner-ruled data-defined `TransformKindRegistry`). Each milestone gets a blind concept-verify. Merge PR #93 only at the very end on the gate's sign-off of the whole arc.

## 2026-07-05: material-substrate item 4 INGEST-FOR-COMPOSITION (geophagy) closes the mining-emergence loop; owner force-and-manipulation ADDENDUM logged, on branch claude/material-substrate

Two things this entry records: the geophagy slice (the emergence-closer), and the owner's scope-addition addendum that reshapes the remaining arc. The granular geophagy landing is in `docs/working/CONSENSUS_ROADMAP.md` (the INGEST-FOR-COMPOSITION paragraph).

The OWNER ADDENDUM (PR #93 comment; the survey is on main `7065d65`, `MATERIAL_SUBSTRATE_HANDOFF.md` tail): after item 4, build a FORCE-AND-MANIPULATION extension, each affordance on the one rule (an evolved affordance plus a drive plus matter-physics, founder-zero, physics-gated, no per-race table), each gated by a blind concept-verify. BUILD HERE: THROW, PUSH/DRAG/ROLL, LEVER, DAM/BLOCK (couples item 5 hydrology), STRIKE-WITH-A-HELD-OBJECT (TOP PICK, the recursive tool loop, rides the crafting seam just built), INGEST-FOR-COMPOSITION (geophagy, DONE below), FLOAT/RAFT, BURROW-THROUGH, MIX/KNEAD, STACK/ASSEMBLE, WEAVE/BIND (TOP PICK, cordage/nets/textiles), CACHE/HOARD (deposit plus place-memory retrieve), GIVE/TRANSFER (trade seed). DO NOT BUILD (bridge neighbour arcs, flag where they touch): COAT/WEAR/ADORN and CLIMB/GRIP (anatomy), MARK/SIGNAL with matter (communication), PLANT/SOW/TEND (biosphere). Recorded as TODOS #58. "Keep finishing the current cascade (items 5-8); fold this extension in when you reach the manipulation layer."

GEOPHAGY (INGEST-FOR-COMPOSITION) is built, the direct answer to the gate's standing note that mining will not EMERGE until the matter is worth something. A GEOPHAGE affordance (`homeostasis.rs`, id 5, scalar, `requires: None`) in a `dev_geophage` fixture; the `deferred_actions` path now records GRASP, EXTRACT, and GEOPHAGE; `Embodiment::geophage` draws, for each homeostatic axis whose backing substance the cell holds, through the SAME `laws::satisfaction` the forage ingest uses (the being's own assimilation and requirement, bounded by reserve room, grossed by the ingest efficiency, taken from the cell, the assimilated part credited). Proven (`physiology_embodiment.rs a_being_geophages_a_needed_mineral_and_outlives_one_that_does_not`): two beings, one draining halite-backed mineral reserve, one salt seam each, differ in one gene (the geophage weight); the evolved-weight being eats the salt and OUTLIVES the blank founder, which never eats and starves. So a mineral's fitness value is an evolved phenotype resolved by physiology against substance data, founder-zero, no per-race diet table. Opt-in and hash-neutral (`world_build`/`world_determinism` unchanged), 629 lib tests plus all binaries green, fmt and clippy clean, no new reserved value. ORDERING NOTE: geophage is enacted in the post-step matter pass, so it tops a reserve up for the NEXT tick rather than saving a being on the tick it would die (a being survives on its buffer, geophagy keeps it full), which is why the reserve must be organ-backed (a mineral-storing tissue) to hold a buffer.

STOPPED: geophagy committed and pushed to PR #93 (`3e7ecb5`); it lands the addendum's INGEST-FOR-COMPOSITION and closes the mining-emergence loop for the LOOSE-mineral case. NEXT, in order of value: (1) the combined bonded-mineral loop (a being MINES a bonded mineral vein because it needs it, then eats it), which needs a geophage-from-carried path or an EXTRACT that breaks the mineral loose IN the cell for geophagy to eat (an integration seam to settle); (2) CRAFT/knapping (shape a tool from carried/mined stone, a reserved knap geometry, closes the tool-making loop; the design fork on the tool geometry is a reserved fixture shape by default, geometry-from-fracture-physics a refinement); (3) the rest of the force-and-manipulation extension (STRIKE-WITH-A-HELD-OBJECT and WEAVE the owner's top picks, then THROW/PUSH/LEVER/DAM/FLOAT/BURROW/MIX/STACK/CACHE/GIVE); (4) items 5-8 (modifiable terrain, live fire, shelter, the matter cycle with the owner-ruled data-defined `TransformKindRegistry`). Each milestone gets a blind concept-verify. Merge PR #93 only at the very end on the gate's sign-off of the whole arc.

## 2026-07-05: material-substrate item 4 CRAFTING seam and tool-in-extraction (a wielded tool multiplies the affordance), on branch claude/material-substrate

Two more gated slices landed on PR #93, both signed off by the gate in-session, taking the arc from "matter can be broken and carried" into "a tool's function derives against what it works." The granular landings are in `docs/working/CONSENSUS_ROADMAP.md` (the ITEM 4 CRAFTING SEAM and TOOL-IN-EXTRACTION paragraphs).

The crafting seam (`crates/compose/src/capability.rs cut_capability_against_target`): the cut contest now reads the resistance from the TARGET's own material axes (`mat.indentation_hardness`, `mat.specific_cut_energy`) rather than the single global reference target `pierce` measured every part against, so the same edge parts soft hide and stalls on hard granite, and a harder sharper edge parts stone a softer one cannot, diverging on the target's substance DATA alone with no per-material branch. This is the seam the handoff called the load-bearing one for the whole arc; the gate confirmed it is "hardened exactly right." Opt-in beside `derive_capabilities` (no existing caller changed), so hash-neutral. The full retirement of `pierce`'s global reference, folding CUT into the `FunctionLawRegistry` as a data-bound kernel, is deferred to when a consumer reads it.

The tool-in-extraction payoff (`crates/sim`): a `WieldedTool` struct (`material.rs`) is the contest interface of a tool, its working `contact_area` geometry and its `substance` material (hardness read from the registry), exactly what a crafted `FormDef`-plus-`Substance` tool will populate. The `Walker` gains an opt-in `wielded: Option<WieldedTool>` (`locomotion.rs`, `None` by default, folds nothing into `state_hash`). `Embodiment::extract_underfoot` reads the wielded tool: the being's force presses over the tool's smaller contact area (a sharper edge concentrates it) and the effective pressure is capped by the tool's own hardness (a soft tool blunts; a tool declaring no hardness blunts to nothing). Proven (`physiology_embodiment.rs a_wielded_tool_multiplies_the_extraction_affordance_by_its_geometry_and_material`): the same being mines nothing bare-handed, fractures granite with a sharp hard flint tool, and mines nothing with a sharp SOFT chalk tool (it blunts), so the tool multiplies the affordance by both geometry and material. The gate signed this off as passing the emergence gate the same way the grasp and extract drivers do. Opt-in and hash-neutral, whole sim suite green (628 lib plus all integration binaries), fmt and clippy clean, no new reserved value.

STOPPED: seven gated slices pushed to PR #93 this session (the item-3 driver, item-4 extraction A/B/C, the crafting seam, and the tool-in-extraction), all signed off or under the gate's incremental review. NEXT: the CRAFTING that PRODUCES the tool from mined stone (knapping a `FormDef` geometry from a carried/mined `Substance`, its function derived by the same `cut_capability_against_target` read), which closes the full emergence loop (mine stone, make a better tool, mine harder rock, so mining pays off and the extract weight is selected up) and is where the first mining behaviour's blind concept-verification comes due. This has a design choice to settle (how a knapped tool's geometry is chosen: a reserved fixture shape, an evolved-controller output, or derived from the worked stone), to be decided at the slice. Then items 5 through 8 (modifiable terrain, live fire, shelter, the matter cycle where the owner-ruled data-defined `TransformKindRegistry` gets implemented). Merge PR #93 only at the very end on the gate's sign-off of the whole arc.

---

## 2026-07-05: material-substrate item 4 EXTRACTION MECHANISM complete (fracture read, kernel, run-wired driver), on branch claude/material-substrate

Cascade item 4, the extraction contest (the first big observable milestone, mining), is built end to end as a run of three gated slices on PR #93, and the gate signed off slices A and B in-session. The granular per-slice landings are in `docs/working/CONSENSUS_ROADMAP.md` (the ITEM 4 SLICE A/B/C landing paragraphs); this entry recovers the state. A being now breaks bonded rock loose and takes it, but only because its evolved controller decided to AND its contact pressure clears the rock's fracture strength.

Slice A (`crates/sim/src/material.rs`): `SubstanceMix::fracture_hardness` and `MaterialField::fracture_hardness` read a cell's FRACTURE-gating hardness as the GREATEST `mat.fracture_strength` among its constituents (a `constituent_max` helper), NOT the volume-weighted mean `bulk_hardness` takes, so ore in rock breaks at the rock. This landed the gate's item-1 note exactly (the gate confirmed it). Slice B (`extraction_yield`): the extraction physics, composing `laws::contact_pressure` and `laws::cut_penetrate`, so strength gates whether the rock breaks and energy sizes how much comes off; off the run path, proven in isolation. Slices A and B are hash-neutral (pure reads, no run-path consumer) and SIGNED OFF by the gate. Slice C (the driver): an EXTRACT affordance (`homeostasis.rs`, id 4, scalar, `requires: None`) in a `dev_miner` fixture; the per-affordance grasp-intent map generalised to a `deferred_actions` map of `(AffordanceId, activation)` so GRASP and EXTRACT ride one enactment path; `Embodiment::extract_underfoot` gates on the fracture contest (`being_muscle_force` over the reserved working area is a contact pressure; if it does not exceed the cell's `fracture_hardness` the rock holds) and, above the gate, takes a strength-bounded load through `grasp_underfoot` (extraction is fracture THEN carry). One reserved value added, `ExtractionParams.working_area` (opt-in on the Embodiment, surfaced-with-basis: the bearing area of the body's working surface, a geometry the anatomy arc derives; dev-fixture 0.001 m^2). Proven (`physiology_embodiment.rs a_being_mines_bonded_rock_only_when_it_decides_to_and_can_fracture_it`): a deciding being with a small working area fractures granite and mines a strength-bounded load, a blank founder never mines, and the same deciding being with a large working area mines nothing (the contest is pressure, not raw force). Opt-in and hash-neutral for existing scenarios (`world_build` and `world_determinism` unchanged), 628 lib tests plus all integration binaries green, fmt and clippy clean.

HONEST LIMIT and the emergence question (the gate's own framing, on record in the PR): mining will not EMERGE in a free run yet, because mined matter has no adaptive value until a downstream use gives it one, so nothing selects the extract weight up. The extraction MECHANISM is proven at the decision plus the physics contest; the EMERGENCE arrives when a use exists. The faithful next payoff is therefore CRAFTING (the handoff's item 4 proper): `derive_capabilities` over a worked object so a tool multiplies the affordance that made it, which is what gives mined stone adaptive value (mine stone, make a better tool, mine more). The energy-sized per-stroke yield (slice B's `extraction_yield`, built and proven) also wires in when the mineable substances carry a cited `mat.specific_cut_energy`.

STOPPED: item 4 slices A, B, C committed and pushed to PR #93 (A and B gate-signed-off, C pushed for review). NEXT: CRAFTING, the worked-object capability read (`capability.rs derive_capabilities` over a FormDef geometry plus `Substance` material, so a sharp hard edge reads a positive CUT/PIERCE and a made tool supplies the geo/mat closures that read a HIGHER capability than the bare limb), the load-bearing seam being that the contest must read the TARGET's material axes (a per-matter target), not one global reference (handoff item 4 seam). Then items 5 through 8 (modifiable terrain, live fire, shelter, the matter cycle). Each milestone gets a blind concept-verification; the first mining behaviour's blind verification comes due when crafting closes the need-plus-use loop. Merge PR #93 only at the very end on the gate's sign-off of the whole arc.

## 2026-07-05: material-substrate item 3 COMPLETE (the carry hinge, its physical cost, and the EVOLVED driver), on branch claude/material-substrate

Cascade item 3, carriable matter, is complete end to end, built as a run of small slices each proven and gated, all on PR #93. The granular per-slice landings are in `docs/working/CONSENSUS_ROADMAP.md` (the SUBSTRATE, ACTIONS, LOCOMOTION COUPLING, and DRIVER landing paragraphs); this entry recovers the state. The ground is populated (real `Substance` rows on a `ground_floor.toml` extension of the mechanical floor, worldbuild z-fill, opt-in), a being carries a per-being `SubstanceMix` inventory, `Embodiment::pick_up`/`put_down` move matter bounded by grown whole-body muscle force against physics-derived weight (gravity a cited physical constant, not reserved), a carried load slows movement (the one RESERVED value of the arc so far, `locomotion.load_penalty`, surfaced-with-basis, dev-fixture set), and the DRIVER now makes a being pick matter up only through an EVOLVED controller decision.

The driver is the emergence crux the gate flagged, and it holds: a GRASP affordance (`homeostasis.rs`, id 3, scalar, `requires: None` so the strength-versus-weight bound is the only gate, in a new `dev_carrier` fixture sibling to `dev_predator`) gives a carrier world's controller a grasp output; `step_with_field_dirs` records the grasp activation when GRASP wins a being's decision (a match arm beside MOVE and INGEST); and the embodiment step enacts the recorded grasps in id order through `Embodiment::grasp_underfoot`, lifting the cell's substances bounded by strength. The decision is an evolved phenotype, proven by `physiology_embodiment.rs a_being_grasps_matter_only_through_an_evolved_controller_weight`: two beings share one body plan, one strength, one identical granite heap each, and differ in ONE gene (the grasp-output weight); the lifted-weight being grasps (its cell loses exactly what it carries, bounded by strength so matter remains), the blank founder on the same matter affording the same operation grasps nothing. Opt-in and hash-neutral for existing scenarios (only `dev_carrier` carries the grasp output, only a nonzero grasp weight enacts a pick-up), so `world_build` and `world_determinism` are unchanged; 626 lib tests plus all integration binaries green, fmt and clippy clean.

HONEST LIMIT carried to item 4: the selection PRESSURE that would lift a founder's grasp weight (a homeostatic need the carried matter serves) does not exist in item 3 alone (raw carry only costs, through the load penalty), so the driver is proven at the DECISION, the same standard the STRIKE affordance holds. Item 4's extraction contest supplies the need and the payoff, where selection-for-carrying becomes demonstrable end to end.

STOPPED: item 3 DRIVER committed to branch `claude/material-substrate` and pushing to PR #93; the gate had just signed off the item-3 load-cost slice and named the driver as the next step, which this delivers. NEXT: item 4, the extraction contest (the first big observable milestone), where a homeostatic need drives a being to mine, the contact pressure (whole-body muscle force) works against the cell's FRACTURE-GATING hardness (granite `fracture_strength`, the load-bearing or hardest constituent, NOT the `bulk_hardness` mean, per the gate's item-4 note), and `derive_capabilities` over a worked object lets a tool multiply the affordance. Then items 5 through 8 (modifiable terrain, live fire, shelter, and the matter cycle where the owner-ruled data-defined `TransformKindRegistry` gets IMPLEMENTED). Merge PR #93 only at the very end on the gate's sign-off of the whole arc.

## 2026-07-05: material-substrate slice 1 READ SIGNED OFF; slice 1 WIRE (the hash fold) landed, on branch claude/material-substrate

Slice 1 READ (the `MaterialField`) was SIGNED OFF by the gate on PR #93: steering clean (P8, P9, P11 held exactly), determinism verified (the crucible unchanged at `254bc17c`), and the no-`PhysicsRegistry` grounding catch praised. One design note carried to item 4: the volume-weighted mean is physically correct for density but hardness does NOT average linearly, so when item 4 wires the extraction contest it must read the hardness the fracture law gates on (the load-bearing matrix or the hardest constituent, ore in rock breaks at the rock), not the `bulk_hardness` mean, which stands as a placeholder with no consumer until then.

Slice 1 WIRE landed on the gate's green light (the hash-changing fold, opt-in empty-default). `crates/sim/src/runner.rs`: the `Embodiment` carries a `material: MaterialField` (EMPTY by default) with a `set_material` opt-in setter (like `set_percepts` and `set_physiology`, needing no layout rebuild since matter does not touch the controller) and a `material()` read accessor; `state_hash` folds `emb.material.hash_into` beside `emb.resources` in canonical `(Coord3, substance-id, volume)` order. An empty material layer (every existing scenario) folds no bytes, so the run is byte-identical (the opt-in empty default, the same shape as the harm-learning `ReserveMemory` fold). The fold needs NO `PhysicsRegistry` (it folds substance ids and volumes, not derived properties), so threading a registry into the World defers to item 4's first consumer of derived hardness. Determinism proven (`physiology_embodiment.rs the_material_substrate_folds_into_state_hash_and_stays_deterministic`): a matter-bearing runner (a granite-and-soil ground cell plus a subsurface ore deposit) replays bit for bit and folds identically between the pinned order and the scheduler variant, and a populated layer changes the hash versus the empty opt-out. 622 lib tests plus all 36 integration binaries green, `world_build` and `world_determinism` unchanged, fmt and clippy and verify.sh clean. No new reserved value.

STOPPED: slice 1 WIRE committed to branch `claude/material-substrate` and pushed to PR #93; monitoring for the gate's review. NEXT: populate the ground (worldgen fills the empty z-column from real `Substance` rows added to the mechanical floor with cited density and hardness, opt-in per scenario), then item 3 (carriable matter) and item 4 (the extraction contest that first reads a cell's derived hardness, per the gate's item-4 note). Item 2 (organic harvest) stays partly gated on the biosphere-into-the-run arc; the mineral half is exercisable now.

## 2026-07-05: material-substrate arc STARTED; slice 1 READ (the MaterialField) landed, on branch claude/material-substrate

The next arc after harm-learning (which merged as PR #92), per docs/working/MATERIAL_SUBSTRATE_HANDOFF.md: give the world MATTER (located, structured substance off the physics floor) so mining, harvesting, carrying, crafting, digging, fire, shelter, and the matter cycle emerge from one equation, need plus physics-derived affordance plus structured matter. Eight cascade items in strict dependency order. The handoff flags one OWNER-CALL seam (item 8: whether the closed `DecayLaw` enum should become a data-defined `TransformKindRegistry` keyed to a physics kernel by id), deferred to that item and not resolved unilaterally.

Slice 1 READ landed (cascade item 1, substance in the ground, the read half). New module `crates/sim/src/material.rs`: a `MaterialField`, a Coord3-keyed sibling of `ResourceField`, whose per-cell `SubstanceMix` maps a physics `Substance` id to the VOLUME of that substance present; the cell's bulk density and indentation hardness are DERIVED by reading a `&PhysicsRegistry` (the volume-weighted mean of the substance rows' `mat.density` and `mat.indentation_hardness`, reducing to a pure substance's own value for a single-substance cell), never stored on the tile; the matter MASS is the volume-weighted density sum the carry and extraction contests weigh; `take`/`deposit` are id-sorted sequential draws with no fresh RNG (the `ResourceField::take` discipline); and `hash_into` is DEFINED in canonical (Coord3, substance-id, volume) order but not yet called on the run path. Off the run path and hash-neutral: no World field, no worldgen population, no `state_hash` fold, so every existing scenario replays bit-for-bit (622 lib tests plus all 36 integration binaries green, `world_build` and `world_determinism` unchanged, fmt and clippy and verify.sh clean). Eight new unit tests prove the derivation: a pure cell reads its substance's value at any volume, a mixture reads the volume-weighted mean, a substance missing an axis contributes zero to that property, an unregistered substance and an empty cell read zero, `take` clamps and prunes to void, the hash is canonical and insertion-order-independent, and an empty field folds nothing.

Grounding finding that shaped the slice: the running sim world carries NO `PhysicsRegistry` (physics is used only through `civsim_physics::laws` kernels), so the derivation takes the registry as a parameter exactly as `ResourceField::source` takes a `HomeostaticRegistry`, which keeps the READ half off the run path; threading a `PhysicsRegistry` into the World is the WIRE slice's job. Steering held: a matter kind is a `Substance` TOML row not a `Material` enum (P8), properties are registry-derived not tile-stored (P11), and the derivation reads only substance and floor-axis ids, never a race, kind, or role (P9). No new reserved value (`mat.density` and `mat.indentation_hardness` are already SET floor data); the volume-weighted-mean aggregation over a mixture is the stated modelling choice, surfaced for the gate (item 4 may specialise the extraction contest's read when it wires mining).

STOPPED: slice 1 READ committed to branch `claude/material-substrate`; opening a PR and monitoring for the gate's review, the same process as the harm-learning arc. NEXT: slice 1 WIRE, worldgen fills the empty z-column from substance rows and the `MaterialField` folds into `state_hash` behind the opt-in empty-default so existing scenarios stay byte-identical, then the first observable milestone, an extraction contest reading a cell's derived hardness, behind the flag.

## 2026-07-05: harm-learning arc MERGED to main (PR #92); the last authored belief is retired

The experiential-learning + belief-to-behaviour arc landed. The gate signed off slice (d) (the controlled acceptance): it re-verified the founder-layout fix (opted-out scenarios hash-neutral, world_build 16, the crucible re-baselined to `254bc17c` replay-identical, 781 tests green), and confirmed by reading the asserts that each leg is tested rather than claimed: LEARN (a naive low-tolerance being commits HARMS on salt through `couple_conversation`, no injection), the FALSIFIER (a fully tolerant being on the identical salt forms no such belief, because `is_harm_tick` fires only on a reserve fall, the one result an inject-on-presence hardcode cannot produce), AVOID plus SURVIVE (only the being with the evolved CONDITION-direction weight steers off the salt and keeps more condition), and TRANSMIT (the learned `(subject, attr)` frame rides the shipped overhearing to a co-located naive being). The owner gave the merge go; I merged with a merge commit (`1c571df`, matching the repo's arc-merge norm), and the session auto-unsubscribed from the PR.

What landed: the injected salt-flat `HAZARD_*` Observe is gone, replaced by a general primitive. A being correlates its own interoceptive harm signal (a reserve delta, `ReserveMemory`) with the raw perceived feature of its cell (`percept.rs`, the data-defined opt-in `PerceptRegistry`), forms the belief itself on the existing evidence engine (`learn.rs`, a per-feature subject, no structural change), acts on it through an evolved controller weight so avoidance emerges from selection rather than authored flight, survives, and transmits it. Nothing hazard-specific: the same loop learns any ground-kind, any good place, any food that sickens. Reserved-with-basis, unchanged and now carried in the manifest as `harm.*`: the feature granularity and the harm-noise floor.

The gate's independence note is recorded for the follow-on: a builder-run blind panel on a builder-written log is not fully independent (the packet-fidelity risk), so the load-bearing verification is the direct real-runner acceptance, and a fully independent blind panel on a NEUTRAL log-emitting run is a clean, non-blocking addition that rides with option (A)'s acceptance.

DEFERRED to the follow-on scenario-and-viability arc (owner-endorsed, gate-recommended): option (A), a scenario that routes a free-running population through the hazard so avoidance pays off and persistence emerges from selection (the default good foragers avoid the barren flats, so they rarely encounter the hazard); the pre-existing small-population collapse fix (the default small crucible goes extinct REGARDLESS of this arc, isolated against the pre-arc a2 commit); and observability part 2 (the transmission and promotion event stream). NEXT: scope and build that follow-on arc, or take the material-substrate arc, per the owner's order (see CONSENSUS_ROADMAP and TODOS).

---

## 2026-07-05: harm-learning arc BUILT end to end (slices a through d), on PR #92, awaiting the gate's slice-d sign-off

The experiential-learning + belief-to-behaviour arc (the conversation-persistence closer), branch `claude/harm-learning`, PR #92, per `docs/working/HARM_LEARNING_HANDOFF.md`. The injected `HAZARD_*` Observe is retired: a being now correlates its own interoceptive harm signal with a raw perceived feature, forms the belief itself, acts on it, survives, and transmits it, all through selection. Slices, each gate-reviewed on the PR: (a) the interoceptive reserve delta (`ReserveMemory`, homeostasis.rs) + the raw perceived-feature substrate (`percept.rs`, a data-defined `PerceptRegistry`, opt-in empty-default) growing the controller feature block (a2, the one hash-changing commit, hash-neutral for opted-out scenarios) SIGNED OFF; (b) the associative learner (`learn.rs`, the existing evidence engine keyed on a per-feature subject; `couple_conversation` replaces the salinity-dose injection; `HAZARD_*`/`hazard_*` calibs retired, manifest `harm.*` added) SIGNED OFF; (c) belief-to-behaviour, the avoidance gradient into CONDITION's dead direction slot so avoidance emerges only through the evolved weight (S path, `avoidance_gradient` + step_embodiment) SIGNED OFF; (d) the acceptance. An audit-the-run catch in (d): b2 declared percepts but the founders were seeded/expressed against the pre-feature layout so they barely foraged (the gate's `164131f0` was a broken-foraging run); fixed by aligning genesis-seed/expression/run on the one percept layout (worldbuild.rs + run_world.rs dawn_layout), crucible re-baselines to `254bc17c`, hash-neutral for empty-percept scenarios. Two honest findings surfaced to the owner: good foragers avoid the barren flats so they rarely ENCOUNTER the hazard (the mechanism is correct, the default scenario does not route beings through it), and the default small crucible collapses to extinction REGARDLESS of this arc (isolated against the pre-arc a2 commit). Gate ruling: controlled deterministic acceptance (B) showing the full loop; free-running-population form (A) plus the collapse flagged as a follow-on scenario-and-viability arc. The (d) acceptance (runner.rs tests) shows LEARN + FALSIFIER + AVOID/SURVIVE + TRANSMIT deterministically through the runner/world, and a 3-auditor blind concept-verification panel (blind to the code, section-8 method) returned REALIZED unanimously, each independently naming the falsifier (identical world, divergent internal signal, divergent belief) as what separates a reasoned belief from an injected one. 614 lib + 36 binaries green throughout. STOPPED: held off merging pending the gate's review of the (d) acceptance commits (`5dbb24e`, `a9fb962`) and the owner's call on whether to pull (A) into this arc. NEXT: on the gate's slice-d sign-off, merge; the scenario-on-path + small-population viability is the queued follow-on arc.

## 2026-07-05: emergent-anatomy Step 3 slice 2 CODE-VERIFIED; owner ruled scope; the derived-physiology grow built

The gate code-verified the metabolic-tier grow and held for three owner scope rulings; the owner ruled: (a) the metabolic scope is affirmed, (b) grow the remaining derived physiology in-arc, (c) scope decisions route through the PR handback (agreed; the interactive ask cannot deliver in the headless context). I relayed the rulings on the PR and built the derived-physiology grow, split 3a (read, off-path) then 3b (wire).

Slice 3a (grow the physiology composition axes + generalize the reads, hash-neutral): the `MorphogenProgram`'s `bio_axes` is generalized to `composition_axes` (it now holds the `mat.*` and `therm.*` metabolic axes too, not only `bio.*`). `dev_default` gains `bio.convective_surface`, `mat.fracture_strength`, and `therm.specific_heat`, placed BEFORE energy density and water fraction so a caller addressing the two reserve-backing axes by `param_count() - 2` and `- 1` stays stable (confirmed hash-neutral: B2b, viability, and the fully-grown metabolic tests replay bit for bit). `Structure::composition_sum(axis)` (the extensive Σ development times composition, for a reserve capacity, exchange surface, or muscle strength) and `composition_mean(axis)` (the intensive mean, for a specific heat or density) generalize the two Structure reads; `backed_capacity` and `whole_body_energy_density` delegate to them.

Slice 3b (re-source the derived physiology from grown tissue, hash-neutral for catalog): `derive_base_drain` and `derive_body_exchange_rate` keep their catalog signatures (no test churn) and delegate to new `base_drain_from` / `body_exchange_rate_from` cores that take the composition scalars (surface, specific heat) explicitly; the caller supplies them from a catalog organ set or a grown structure. `body_mass_kg` is made public so the run scales a grown muscle force. `runner.rs being_derived_drains` sources the exposed surface, energy density, and muscle force from the grown structure when the walker carries one (the surface and muscle from `composition_sum`, the muscle then scaled to mass, the energy density from `whole_body_energy_density`), its catalog organs otherwise; a `walker_exchange_rate` helper does the same for the body-medium exchange at the three embodiment sites. So a fully grown body is no longer thermally inert or exertion-free. Proven (`world_build.rs a_fully_grown_bodys_derived_physiology_is_read_from_its_grown_tissue`): a physiology-seeded fully grown founder's grown structure reads a positive convective surface, muscle strength, and specific heat directly, with no catalog organs; and on the run that grown surface is load-bearing, a physiological body pays the thermoregulatory drain its surface incurs and does not outlast a 20-tick run while the same race with the reserves but no grown surface survives (opposite outcomes for one race id: the run reads the grown tissue, never a catalog organ or a RaceId). Full sim suite green (36 binaries, no existing hash moved), strict clippy and fmt clean.

Honest limit stated: the respiratory surface (read only when a world adds the RESPIRATION axis, absent from the dev scenarios) still reads the catalog path; growing it is the same pattern when a grown race uses respiration. With this, a grown race's body, its function, its metabolism, and its physiology are all grown from its genome, the catalog body retired for a grown race. Next: hand slices 3a and 3b to the gate, then (owner scope permitting) the thaumic-floor track, before the arc merges.

---

## 2026-07-05: emergent-anatomy Step 3 slice 1 GATED; Step 3 slice 2 (the metabolic-tier grow) built

The gate signed off Step 3 slice 1 (the viability cull), praising the audit-the-input catch. On the metabolic-grow scope the gate had relayed to the owner, the interactive ask channel failed (a transport error in the async webhook context) and the owner ruled directly: "Metabolic grow if the q failed." So I took the metabolic-tier grow (retire the catalog body whole), split on the proven cadence into 2a (the read, off-path) and 2b (the wiring).

Slice 2a (grow bio.* tissue + the grown-reserve read, hash-neutral, off the run path): `MorphogenProgram` gains a `bio_axes` vector (`bio.energy_density`, `bio.water_fraction`), its parameters placed AFTER the branch and spawn params so adding a bio axis does not shift the geometry, material, branch, or spawn indices (the existing morphogen seeds and the B2b and viability tests replay byte-identical, verified). `grow` writes the bio composition into the same per-segment material map as `mech`/`opt` and a child inherits it; the function-law dispatch reads only `mech`/`mat`/`opt`, so the bio tissue is metabolic-only and blind to the affordance reads. `Structure::backed_capacity(axis_id)` sums the reserve capacity a grown body's tissue backs on a biology-floor axis (Σ over segments of per-segment development times tissue composition, mirroring `Homeostasis::new`'s organ sum but read directly off the grown segments, no organ kind id); per-segment development is `1/MASS_REFERENCE_SEGMENTS`, the same reference the digest's body_mass uses. `Homeostasis::from_structure(reg, structure)` builds the reserve set from a grown structure (backed axes from `backed_capacity`, derived axes unit). Proven: a grown body with energy and water tissue backs positive reserves and is birth-viable on its own reserves; an energy-less grown body backs zero and starves at birth.

Slice 2b (wire the grown metabolism, retire the catalog body, hash-neutral for existing scenarios): at both embodiment sites (`worldbuild.rs assemble_dawn_embodiment`, `runner.rs embody_newborn`) the `race.body` Some-guard is relaxed (embody if a catalog body OR a developmental program). A race with a catalog body keeps it as the metabolic aggregate and sources reserves from its catalog organs, whether or not it also grows a run structure (the B2b hybrid), so those scenarios are byte-identical. A FULLY GROWN race (no catalog body) sources both from its grown structure: the body is the digest, the reserves are `from_structure` off the grown tissue, so it needs no catalog body. The derived drain reads the grown energy density: `derive_base_drain`/`derive_exertion_coupling` now take `energy_density` as a parameter (the caller supplies it, a catalog body from `whole_body_energy_density` over its organs, a grown body from `Structure::whole_body_energy_density` over its grown tissue), so a fully grown body metabolizes off its own tissue instead of reading zero from the empty digest, which had saturated the drain to max and starved it; byte-identical for every catalog scenario. Blind-verify (`world_build.rs a_fully_grown_race_founds_bodies_with_no_catalog_body_and_starves_without_grown_tissue`): a race with NO catalog body founds eight embodied members, every walker carrying a grown body and no catalog organs; a nourished grown founder survives on its grown reserves, the same race whose tissue carries no energy is born with no reserve and starves through the ordinary cull, replaying bit for bit. Full sim suite green (593 lib plus all 36 integration binaries, no existing hash moved), strict clippy and fmt clean.

Honest limit surfaced: the reserve tier and the drain's energy density are grown, but the remaining derived-physiology reads (convective surface, muscle force, specific heat, body-medium thermal exchange) still read the empty digest and degrade to safe zeros (no heat loss, no exertion cost, no thermal exchange), so a fully grown body's thermoregulatory and exertion physiology is the follow-on; the reserve tier (the starvation blocker) is fully grown, which is what "a grown race needs no catalog body" requires. Next: hand slices 2a and 2b to the gate, then the remaining derived-physiology grow (surface/muscle/specific-heat from grown tissue) if the gate wants it in this arc, and the thaumic-floor track, before the arc merges.

---

## 2026-07-05: emergent-anatomy Step 2 B2b GATED; Step 3 slice 1 (the viability cull) built

The gate signed off slice B2b (the arc's thesis live: a founder's run-body grown from its genome, a grown morphology doing what its catalog kind never could, save/load determinism handled by regrowth, the metabolic-tier seam accepted as the correct Step 3 boundary) and directed Step 3 (the viability cull plus the metabolic-tier grow), relaying the metabolic-grow scope call to the owner. I took the viability cull as the first slice.

An audit-the-input catch first: the handoff's Step-3 mechanism ("generalize `birth_viable` ... dies through the runtime `Stock` collapse cull, epoch.rs:221") rests on a premise that does not hold. `birth_viable` is test-only (no runtime or pool-tier wiring exists), and that `Stock` cull is the pre-dawn biosphere pool tier, which is not wired to morphogen growth (a later arc). Grown bodies live at the run/individual tier, where the death path is the existing per-Walker reserve-floor cull. I surfaced this and the design fork (what defines "incoherent," and where the cull bites) to the owner, who ruled: functional viability at the run tier.

Slice 1 (functional viability at the run tier, hash-neutral for existing scenarios, built and proven): `Structure::whole_body_viability(fns, refs, caps)` (crates/sim/src/morphogen.rs) is the greatest capability any grown segment reads on ANY function law, a pure physics read over the same per-segment dispatch the affordance and speed reads use, blind to id (Principle 9). A body that reads a positive capability on some function is functionally coherent; one grown from a degenerate program reads no viable function and a whole-body viability of zero. The run sets a grown being's derived INTEGRITY reserve to this each tick (runner.rs `step_embodiment`, block 1a, mirroring how the TEMPERATURE reserve is set from body core temp), gated on the registry carrying the integrity axis and the walker carrying a grown structure, so a catalog body and a registry without integrity (every existing run scenario) are untouched: the cull is opt-in and hash-neutral. An incoherent grown body's integrity falls through the reserved death floor and it dies through the SAME `is_alive` to `metabolize` to `reconcile_lifecycle` to `remove_being` path as any other death, with no predicate that inspects morphology to reject it (Principle 8). Proven: a morphogen unit test (a functional body reads positive viability, an all-zero body reads zero), and a dawn-run integration test (`world_build.rs an_incoherent_grown_body_is_culled_through_the_ordinary_reserve_floor_path`) where one race id's founders SURVIVE when their genome grows a viable limb and are CULLED when it grows an inert body, the opposite verdicts for one race id proving the cull reads grown physics never a RaceId (Principle 9), and the run replays bit for bit. Full sim suite green (591 lib + all integration binaries, no existing hash moved), strict clippy and fmt clean. Reserved-with-basis: the integrity axis's death floor for a grown body, the minimum whole-body functional capability sustaining any life function (a small positive fraction, dev fixture 1/32). Honest limit: the read is a max over the per-law capabilities (functional viability, can the body do any function), not the negative compose safety fraction (structural buckling), which the owner chose deliberately; and the coherence read is over `mech/mat/opt` function-geometry, so metabolic coherence waits on the metabolic-tier grow (Step 3 slice 2). Next: the state-hash re-baseline note (none moved, opt-in), the metabolic-tier grow (grow `bio.*` tissue so the digest endows organs and the catalog body retires whole; the gate relayed its scope to the owner), and the thaumic-floor track before the arc merges.

---

## 2026-07-05: emergent-anatomy Step 2 slice B2b (the payoff): founders and newborns grow their run-body from the genome

The arc's thesis is live: a founder's run-body is now GROWN from its own genome at genesis, and the run reads the grown body. A race opts in with `Race::with_morphogen(program)` (`crates/sim/src/race.rs`, an `Option<MorphogenProgram>` sibling to `Race::body`, fail-quiet-until-declared like `articulation`/`body`). At the founder assembly (`worldbuild.rs assemble_dawn_embodiment`) and the newborn pairing (`runner.rs embody_newborn`), when the race carries a program the walker's grown structure is `grow(express_program(program, race.genes, world.genome_of(id)), seed, id)`, handed via `Walker::with_structure`; the newborn keys on the same `emb.seed`. Growth is a pure function of `(program, genome, seed, id)`, so it reproduces on replay and on a two-tier reload where the walker is regrown from the re-minted genome (walkers are never serialized; only the demographic snapshot is). The genesis seeding is the founder gene set path: `append_morphogen_block` after `append_controller_block`, its loci taking the next gene indices, feeding `Channel::Morphogen`, promoting and evolving through the existing pool with no per-channel code.

The scoped seam, surfaced for the gate to rule on: the gate directed "retiring the race-uniform `race.body.clone()`", and this retires the RUN-GOVERNING body (per-member, genome-derived, read for affordances and ground speed), while KEEPING the catalog `body` as the LOD-0 metabolic aggregate the reserves, physiology, and thermoregulation read. The reason is code-grounded: `Structure::digest()` endows no organs (a grown segment carries `mech/mat/opt` function-geometry, not the `bio.*` organ kinds the metabolic reserves are sourced from), and `Homeostasis::new` sums reserve capacity over `body.organs`, so a founder metabolised off the digest would carry zero reserves and starve at birth (the `mobile_body` fixtures state this explicitly). Deriving the metabolic tier from grown physics (grown `bio.*` tissue, so the digest can feed the metabolism and the catalog body retires whole) is the natural Step 3 boundary. Opt-in per race, so every existing catalog scenario is hash-neutral (the growth is guarded by `if let Some(program)`); a morphogen race re-baselines its own run.

Blind concept-verification, green on the first real run (`world_build.rs a_founders_genome_grows_a_locomotor_the_catalog_body_lacks`): a race whose CATALOG body is rooted (no locomotor) but whose GENOME grows a limb disperses from its dawn cell on that grown locomotor, a limb the catalog body never carried; the control, the same race whose genome grows no limb, stays put. The limbed founder's walker carries a structure reading LOCOMOTE > 0 while its catalog `body.locomotion` is empty. The run replays bit for bit, matches the scheduled order (worker invariance), and a rebuild reproduces each founder's grown structure byte-identically, including a direct regrow-from-re-minted-genome equality (the reload guarantee made explicit). Full sim suite green (590 lib + all integration binaries, no existing hash moved), strict clippy and fmt clean across the non-GPU crate set. The workspace GPU crate (`tracel-llvm-bundler`) fails its GitHub fetch in the sandbox as before (the 5090 escape hatch), unrelated to this slice. Next: the state-hash re-baseline note for the gate, the owner's promote/restrict and save/load replay tests, the 5090 re-confirm, then Step 3 (viability cull, and the metabolic-tier grow that retires the catalog body whole) and the thaumic-floor track before the arc merges.

---

## 2026-07-05: emergent-anatomy Step 2 slice B1 gated; slice B2a (run-read plumbing) built

The gate signed off slice B1 (the digest + direct-read bridge) and directed slice B2, the run wiring (the payoff). B2 splits into B2a (the run-read plumbing, hash-neutral, this increment) and B2b (the growth + genesis seeding + blind-verify, hash-changing, next).

Slice B2a (hash-neutral, unit-proven): `Walker` gains an `Option<Structure>` field (crates/sim/src/locomotion.rs; a `with_structure` builder so the founder/newborn embodiment can hand a grown body without changing `Walker::new`'s many callers). It is NOT folded into `state_hash` directly (a static derived body folds only through the dynamic state it drives, position and reserves, exactly as the catalog body does). The run reads route through it: `step_with_field_dirs`'s affordance read and ground-speed read, and the runner's exertion-drain velocity (runner.rs `being_derived_drains`), use `afforded_structure` / `locomotion_speed_structure` when the walker carries a grown structure, else the catalog path. Hash-neutral because every existing walker carries `None`, so the catalog path is unchanged and the full sim suite (including determinism/replay/worker-invariance) is green with no hash moved. Proven by a routing test: a walker whose GROWN structure is rooted stays put though its catalog body is a mobile walker, and one whose grown structure bears a limb moves, so the grown body governs the run, not the catalog. Next: slice B2b, the growth seams (grow each founder from `world.genome_of(id)` at worldbuild, the newborn at runner, seed the morphogen block into the founder gene set so growth is genome-driven, hand the grown Structure to `Walker::with_structure`), the state-hash re-baseline, replay/worker-invariance, the 5090 re-confirm, and the blind concept-verification (a lineage grows a novel morphology reading as a weapon/locomotor the catalog never had, observable on the run).

## 2026-07-05: emergent-anatomy Step 2 slice A gated; slice B1 (digest + direct-read bridge) built

The gate signed off Step 2 slice A (the morphogen growth kernel; it audited `grow()` line-by-line and confirmed the triple termination guarantee, the counter-keyed determinism, and the empty steering scan) and directed slice B, the run-path wiring. On the fork I surfaced (a grown Structure carries geometry inline with no catalog kind ids, but every run read resolves a part's kind id against the ONE shared organs registry), the owner ruled (2026-07-05): read the grown Structure DIRECTLY on the body (retire the kind-id indirection for grown bodies, not intern grown morphologies into the catalog), and SPLIT the work (digest + direct-read bridge first, hash-neutral; then the run wiring, hash-changing).

Slice B1 (this increment, hash-neutral, unit-proven, off the run path): `Structure::digest() -> BodyPlan` (crates/sim/src/morphogen.rs) is the LOD-0 aggregate, deriving the one morphology scalar the metabolism reads on the tick (`body_mass`, a coarse aggregate of the grown structure's extent over a reserved reference) and leaving the part-lists empty by design (a grown body's FUNCTION is read from the structure directly, not the catalog part-lists) and temperament/metabolic-organs to the caller (the documented hybrid: the morphogen kernel grows function-geometry `mech/mat/opt`, not `bio.*` tissue composition yet). The direct-read bridge: `AffordanceRegistry::afforded_structure` (crates/sim/src/homeostasis.rs) reads a grown body's MOVE/STRIKE affordance from the greatest capability any grown segment reads (no organs registry, no kind id), and `locomotion::locomotion_speed_structure` reads the ground speed from the structure's strongest LOCOMOTE segment (`Structure::best_locomotor_stride`, its capability and `mech.arm_length`), the activity factor supplied from the LOD-0 digest's temperament. Two new B1 tests (the digest aggregates a bigger grown structure to a greater body_mass; the run reads a grown body directly, a limbed one affords MOVE and moves, a rooted one affords neither, a weapon one affords STRIKE) plus the five slice-A tests are green; strict clippy and fmt clean; full sim suite green with no existing hash moved (the bridge functions are not called from the run yet). Next: slice B2, the run wiring, hash-changing, with the blind concept-verification (a grown body no catalog contains doing something its "kind" never would) the gate is holding for, the state-hash re-baseline, and the 5090 re-confirm.

## 2026-07-05: emergent-anatomy STEP 1 COMPLETE and gated; Step 2 slice A (the morphogen kernel) built

Step 1 is fully gated and complete (the gate signed off 1e-speed and re-confirmed on the 5090 oracle, `19fbf449→96804fc` replay-identical): strike (1b), sight/locomotion in the Body (1d), the run-path affordance gate (1e), and ground speed (1e-speed) all derive from physics; `MorphCategory`, the `F_*` tags, and `sqrt(body_mass)` are gone. The running world computes what a body can do, and how fast it moves, from the body's own physics, no authored list, no tags.

Step 2 slice A (the developmental-growth kernel) is built, unit-proven, and OFF the run path (hash-neutral), on the gate's cadence (kernel + genome channel first, run-path wiring a separate slice). New `crates/sim/src/morphogen.rs`: a `grow(program, params, seed, id) -> Structure` kernel grows a `Structure` (a graph of `Segment`s, each carrying `mech.*` geometry and `mat.*`/`opt.*` material in the same zero-for-absent `BTreeMap` shape a `KindDef` uses) from a genome's expressed growth program. Determinism is the tall pole and is met: integer Fixed-point, no float, every stochastic growth branch counter-keyed on a new `Phase::MORPHOGEN` (0x1B, `crates/core/src/keys.rs`, with the collision test extended) with declared R-RNG-COORD slots (spawn, jitter), hard-capped in depth and total segments (a termination guarantee, proven by a test that a max-branch/certain-spawn program still halts under the caps), and folded into `state_hash` via `Structure::hash_into` in canonical segment order. The Step-1 dispatch reads function off the grown graph unchanged (`Structure::max_capability`): a body grown weapon-like reads PIERCE and not LOCOMOTE, one grown limb-like reads LOCOMOTE and not PIERCE, one grown with a dense optical tissue reads REFRACT, all blind to any id. Growth is heritable through `Channel::Morphogen(MorphogenParamId)` (`crates/sim/src/genome.rs`, sibling to `Composition`/`Controller`; `append_morphogen_block` clones `append_controller_block`), expressed by the existing `GeneSet::express` and evolving through the existing `GenePool`/`GeneticScheme` with no per-channel code, so a body's SHAPE is a lineage's inheritance. The geometry-to-value mapping grounds each axis in the floor's own range (the genome expresses a `[0,1]` fraction that growth maps into `[lo,hi]`); the ranges, the depth/segment/branch caps, and the growth-factor bounds are reserved-with-basis labelled dev fixtures. Five morphogen unit tests plus the extended phase-collision test are green; strict clippy and fmt clean; the full sim suite is green and no existing hash moves (the channel/phase additions do not touch any existing path). Honest limit: growth is a single-rule scaling recursion; the L-system with several segment symbols (qualitatively distinct organs in one body) is the next increment, and slice B wires growth into the body assembly (the hash-changing run-path slice with the blind concept-verification the gate named: a grown morphology doing something its "kind" never would).

---

## 2026-07-05: emergent-anatomy step 1e-speed, the sqrt(body_mass) retirement (hash-changing slice on PR #91)

The last step-1 piece: the `sqrt(body_mass)` allometric proxy in `locomotion::locomotion_speed` is retired for a grown-limb physics read. The ground-speed size factor is now `stride * strength`, where `stride` is the strongest LOCOMOTE limb's `mech.arm_length` over a reserved `reference_leg_length` (dev fixture 0.5 m) clamped to [0,1], and `strength` is that limb's LOCOMOTE capability (one minus the bending utilization, from `derive_capabilities`), both a pure physics read over the organ registry blind to any kind or race id. `locomotion_speed` gained an `organs: &BodyPlanRegistry` parameter, threaded through `step_with_field_dirs` (the field-less `step` supplies the dev registry) and the runner's exertion-drain path; `LocomotionParams` gained `reference_leg_length`. The `is_mobile`/`ROOTED_MODE` mode-id proxy is gone (mobility is now `capability > 0`).

Gate deliverables the owner named, all met. (1) The run-path `state_hash` RE-BASELINES where a being moves: witnessed by a moving-being embodiment probe (physiology installed, explore controller), HEAD/sqrt `268026453859133546793723897305536602590` → new/grown-limb `32976508349879940520108993058705171020`, both replay-identical; the determinism-harness `unified_trace` hash is UNCHANGED because that scenario carries blank controllers and no physiology, so the change is confined to the moving path (replay-identity and worker-width invariance both green). (2) The four R-BEHAVIOR-EVOLVE proofs: only two went red on the ~15% slower scoring-body speed, both DELIBERATELY re-tuned with the timing-shift diagnosed, not chased green. `the_symmetric_scorer...`: the fixed-heading being random-walks the same seed-keyed trajectory whatever direction the water lies, and the slower step let seed `0xF00D`'s trajectory linger on the north band; the separation is speed-robust (holds for the vast majority of seeds), re-pinned to `0x5EED` whose trajectory still misses both perpendicular bands. `bidirectional_thermotaxis...`: the slower walk let an UNDIRECTED kinesis drift out of the shallow bowl without hitting the lethal centre, so the signed thermoreceptor looked unnecessary; `BOWL_START` 4→3 (one ring deeper) forces a directed climb (probed: a kinesis dies at every start there, a directed seeker/fleer survives), restoring the signed percept as load-bearing for the ORIGINAL seed 0x1007 (bi=200, bi_abl=154). (3) Blind concept-verification on the run-path step: `a_strong_limbed_lineage_disperses_faster_than_a_weak_limbed_one` (two beings, equal stride, stout vs slender section, same exploration seed) shows the stouter limb ends farther from the origin, so movement speed is a physics read of the limb. The GPU-oracle re-confirm is the owner's 5090 pass (the escape-hatch: CPU replay + worker-width invariance proven, new canonical hash noted for the owner to re-confirm bit-identical). Strict clippy and fmt clean, full sim suite green. Pushed to PR #91 for the gate; after sign-off, step 1 is complete and Step 2 (`Channel::Morphogen`) is next.

---

## 2026-07-04: emergent-anatomy step 1e, the run-path affordance gate (hash-neutral slice on PR #91)

Retired the authored `MorphCategory` anatomy category from the RUN-PATH affordance gate, the last authored-category read in the embodiment tick, so a body's MOVE (and, in the combat fixture, STRIKE) is now DERIVED from its parts' LOCOMOTE / PIERCE capability against the organ registry through the function-law dispatch (`crates/compose/src/capability.rs`), never a `MorphCategory::Locomotion`/`Weapon`/`Sense` label. `AffordanceDef.requires` became `Option<FunctionLawId>` with a `min_capability` threshold; `afforded`/`affords_id` and the private `affords`/`body_capability` now take `(organs: &BodyPlanRegistry, refs: &CapabilityRefs, caps: &CapabilityCaps)`; the context is threaded through `locomotion::step_with_field_dirs` (the field-less `step` supplies the labelled dev registry, being the fixture path) and the runner (a new `Embodiment.organs` field, `set_organs` setter, defaulted to the dev fixture in `new` and set to the world's own registry in `worldbuild`). The capability references and floor ceilings ride in `LocomotionParams` (`capability_refs`, `capability_caps`). `civsim_sim` now re-exports the compose capability types (they are in the affordance public signature). A `MIN_CAP` floor makes a present-but-zero-capability part not afford (so a zero `min_capability` still requires a positive reading).

Proven HASH-NEUTRAL two ways: a new unit proof (`the_capability_move_gate_reproduces_the_retired_rooted_mark_gate`) checks that for EVERY registry locomotion mode the derived MOVE gate returns the identical verdict the retired `m != rooted-mark` proxy did; and empirically the dawn `unified_trace` state_hash is byte-identical before and after (201795224112678309219212154553784599134). New Principle-9 run-path steering test in `biosphere_steering.rs` (`the_run_path_move_affordance_is_a_pure_physics_read_with_no_layer_or_race_key`). Full sim suite green (580 lib + integration), strict clippy and fmt clean.

SPLIT, on the gating agent's blessed hash-neutral/hash-changing cadence: the `locomotion_speed` `sqrt(body_mass)` allometric proxy is NOT retired here. Retiring it (stride derived from the LOCOMOTE limb's `mech.arm_length`) shifts the ground speed, which perturbs the tuned R-BEHAVIOR-EVOLVE scoring fixtures (four emergence proofs went red on the timing shift, e.g. slower exploration stumbles into the scored water band). That is a hash-changing slice needing a careful evolve-harness re-tune, so it is the next slice, flagged at the `locomotion_speed` doc comment. Pushed to PR #91 for the gate. Then: step 2 (`Channel::Morphogen` developmental growth), step 3 (viability cull), the thaumic-floor track; then merge when the arc is complete.

## 2026-07-04: base-liveliness landed and tested; conversation overhearing shipped; anatomy next

The base-liveliness arc merged (PR #89) and was verified: green on CPU (579 sim tests, verify.sh clean) and on the 5090 GPU oracle (every gate, the environmental field stack bit-identical). The world is alive and BOUNDED: dispersal, local scarcity biting at ~95% grazing pressure, population self-bounding near 55, salt tolerance selecting up as a gradient not a gate, promoted survival arcs, movement-coupled belief gossip. Ran the blind concept-verification acceptance gate (AGENTIC_ADDENDUM section 8) on the wired concepts: MOVEMENT partial (beings move but cluster on resource cells rather than disperse), SCARCITY partial (food-scarcity strong, water not yet a consumed resource, death cause untagged), CONVERSATION not realized (a belief spreads to ~12% of the founding cohort then decays to 0 by gen 3).

Belief transmission (PR #90, branch claude/belief-transmission): shipped the transmission-substrate improvement, gossip fan-out overhearing (a speaker reaches every co-located listener in id order, not one random draw), cross-promotion belief flow (a promoted holder reaches its non-promoted co-located listeners), and committed_beliefs so the max-keyed belief is not masked (test a_promoted_speaker_reaches_a_nonpromoted_listener_by_gossip). This did NOT close conversation-persistence, and forcing it was rejected as steering. Findings, owner-guided: the salt-flat belief is INJECTED first-hand (the being never reasons it); beings do not die in the flats (instrumented 125 deaths, 0 on hazard cells; the salt is a chronic drain, not an acute kill), so a corpse-observational-learning experiment was built and reverted; and the real closer is a general EXPERIENTIAL / ASSOCIATIVE-LEARNING primitive (a being correlates its own felt harm with its perceived environment, forms the belief itself, acts, transmits) plus belief->behavior, a later-arc capability, deferred not hacked.

Also this session: the read-no-high-level-fact gate became CLAUDE.md prime directive 4 (if a substrate reads a high-level social or emergent fact to produce behaviour it is authoring; replace with the general causal primitive plus a correlating proxy, kin-cooperation the template). Blind concept verification became the standing acceptance gate (AGENTIC_ADDENDUM section 8). Future arcs scoped and held: emergent-anatomy (THE next arc, docs/working/EMERGENT_ANATOMY_HANDOFF.md, ready, with the communication-reach synthesis sub-arc appended), then the material substrate (matter, mining, harvest, craft, fire, the matter cycle), the communication-reach substrate, biosphere-into-run, and the social layer as an EMERGENT layer over the lower substrates (cooperation, kinship, skill, affect, cumulative culture), not an arc of new mechanisms.

NEXT: hand off the emergent-anatomy arc (the handoff is ready). Merge PR #90 (conversation overhearing) when CI is green. Queue experiential/associative learning plus belief->behavior as the conversation-persistence closer.
