## 2026-07-02: the GPU stood up with CubeCL (`crates/gpu`), R-GPU-CANON-PIN signed off, consolidated, and rebased onto the moved main

The owner asked to stand up the GPU with CubeCL now that it is available and the RTX 5090 is a working part of the compute, and then to consolidate cleanly and PR and merge. On `claude/physics-substrate-fanout`.

**The GPU is stood up.** A new workspace crate `crates/gpu` (cubecl 0.10, CUDA backend) carries the canonical kernels as real Rust `#[cube]` functions, replacing the earlier Python cupy/NVRTC spikes. `stage0.rs`: the pinned Q32.32 sign-magnitude limb multiply (`emu_mul_kernel`) and the 96-step restoring divide (`emu_div_kernel`), confined to the u32 op set (u32 wrapping add/sub, u16-by-u16-to-u32, bitwise, constant shifts below 32, comparison, branchless `select`; no native 64-bit, no divide, no float), plus host launchers `gpu_mul`/`gpu_div`. `field.rs`: the fixed-point diffusion stencil (`diffuse_kernel`, the Part 5.5 workload) with the `q32_mul` limb-multiply helper and `gpu_diffuse`/`gpu_diffuse_tiled`. All gated bit-for-bit against `civsim_core::Fixed` and PROVEN on the 5090: multiply and divide bit-identical over corners + 1M random cases each; the diffusion field bit-identical CPU-vs-GPU over the whole grid and invariant to tile size (1/4/8/16/32); the 1024-square 200-step example checksum equals the CPU bench exactly (`0x3a385e7c`) at about 22x end-to-end throughput. Six device gate tests, all self-skipping without `CIVSIM_GPU` (CI-safe). Workspace hygiene held: clippy and rustdoc `-D warnings` clean, a no-float source-scan guard (sibling of the physics steering guards) always-on, and the crate is in `members` but excluded from `default-members`, so a plain `cargo build`/`cargo test` compiles zero cubecl (verified) and the base workspace is unchanged.

**Environment recipe (WSL2 + 5090, recorded in memory).** cubecl-cuda builds with no CUDA present (cudarc loads at runtime). At run time it needs the driver (`/usr/lib/wsl/lib/libcuda.so`, always present) plus NVRTC and the cudart headers, which the toolkit does not install here. Staged a self-contained CUDA home from the CUDA 12.9 pip packages: `CUDA_PATH=$HOME/.local/cuda` (with `include/cuda_runtime.h`, `lib/libnvrtc.so.12` + `libnvrtc-builtins.so.12.9` + an unversioned `libnvrtc.so` symlink) and `LD_LIBRARY_PATH=$HOME/.local/cuda/lib:/usr/lib/wsl/lib`. The original failure was `CUDA_PATH` with no `include/`, since cubecl-cuda passes `--include-path=$CUDA_PATH/include` to NVRTC.

**Adversarial red-team (folded).** A determinism/steering review found no bit-divergence from the oracle on CUDA and confirmed all three ports faithful (verified algebraically, not only by sweep). Five real seams folded: the `field.rs` doc comments overclaimed cross-backend bit-identity for the native-i64 form (corrected to CUDA-proven, cross-vendor a Stage 0 matter per the proposal's per-kernel-layout allowance); `q32_mul` was a second multiply copy exercised only over narrow field values, so a full corner + 1M-sweep gate was added (`gpu_fixed_mul` + `q32_mul_kernel`); the float guard was strengthened to the four float type tokens; and the CPU-panics-vs-GPU-wraps overflow and the `width*height < 2^32` index cap were documented as honest limits.

**R-GPU-CANON-PIN signed off and consolidated (resolved 28 to 29, open 37 to 36).** The owner signed off the recommendations (pins as specified, noise canonical, eikonal canonical-under-monotone-min-else-quarantined, CUDA-first backend set, mul-floors/div-truncates convention preserved), general and per-field differentiable, reserved values reserved. Consolidated: the Part 5.4 flag replaced with the mechanism plus a `> Decided and reserved.` blockquote, the pinned-convention note added at Part 3.1 and the resolution pointer at Part 3.4, record 62.23 (four paragraphs), a Part 63 bibliography group (CORDIC, Muller, Knuth vol 2, Goldberg, CubeCL), and cross-references reconciled (the wave-2 blockquote, the R-BIOSPHERE and R-TEMPORAL-LOD sibling lists in design and audit no longer call it open). Audit: Section 1 block 1y, the Section 3 bullet rewritten to resolved form (dropping it from the open count), Section 2 and the Section 7 remediation queue updated (the ten determinism items now three resolved, seven open), and the limitation twenty-nine resolved and thirty-six open. Verification suite clean: em dashes 0/0, banned adverbs 0/0, parts gapless 64, fences balanced, records 62.1 to 62.23 sequential, backlog count 35, no stale "open R-GPU" reference.

**Where it stopped, and what is next.** R-GPU-CANON-PIN is resolved and built; the closing action of this session is the PR of `claude/gpu-cubecl` (this GPU commit rebased onto the current main, which already carries the physics-substrate-fanout line plus later work) to `main` and the merge. The named remaining build (all open, tracked): the transcendental GPU `#[cube]` kernels (exp/ln/trig, specced and gated on their reserved precision targets, the CPU oracle already built); the multi-vendor Stage 0 run (only CUDA is proven); porting the real physics-law field kernels to CubeCL; and re-authoring the wave-2/wave-3 exact transcendental law forms now that the functions exist (replacing the folded proxies). The mul/div DRY (field's `q32_mul` duplicates `emu_mul_kernel`) is a noted drift hazard, both now gated over the full range. The physics fan-out and the scheduler cluster stay separate.

## 2026-07-02: the typological substrate built (R-LANG-TYPOLOGY), on `claude/working-model-setup-h5rzad`

The owner directed: "Check on the latest branch for the LANG_TYPOLOGY_SCOPED_PROPOSAL, and implement after a quick sanity check." The proposal lived on `claude/physics-fan-out-check-z10pw0` (commits 38dcca6, 2621f1e), a scoped pre-sign-off proposal awaiting the owner. This session brought it onto the working branch and built it.

**Branch alignment.** The working branch was two commits behind the engine line; fast-forwarded `claude/working-model-setup-h5rzad` to `origin/claude/physics-substrate-fanout` tip (81f718c, the CommandBuffer live-tick adoption), then took the proposal doc verbatim onto it (workspace green at the base, 535 tests).

**The sanity check (direct grounding, the Workflow classifier was intermittently down so it was run by hand).** Grounded the proposal against the real design.md Part 33.4: confirmed `GrammarParams` is the closed four-enum struct at the cited site (word_order, head_dir, morph_type, alignment), the 33.4 blockquote already reserves the word-order and morphological-type distributions and the disharmony probability, the flag blockquote requires the correlations as data, and record 62.13's modality substrate is the sibling shape the proposal mirrors. Scouted the code idioms (the `FeatureDimDef`/`FeatureValueDef` registry pattern, `DrawKey`/`Phase`, `GroundMetric`, the fail-loud `CalibrationManifest::require_fixed`, the sorted-vec-and-id-newtype discipline). Fact-checked every WALS count the proposal ships as data directly against wals.info (Dryer and Haspelmath eds. 2013): 83A, 85A, 86A, 87A, 88A, 89A, 90A, 20A, 21A, 22A, 95A, 98A, 99A, 100A all confirmed. One design decision recorded from the check: the six-way 81A dominant word order is NOT seeded as an independent parameter (sampling it beside the 83A anchor could draw contradictions); 83A is the anchor and the subject-order refinement waits for a coherence rule.

**Built (`crates/sim/src/typology.rs`, one feat commit).** The data-defined typological substrate: `TypologyRegistry` (the open parameter registry, each parameter carrying its value set and a data-defined `sample_priority`, the anchor-first order, ties broken by id); `TypologyPrior` (per-parameter integer language counts as data, the raw record so no normalisation division enters the draw, per-race overridable, entering only through data so the sampler is race-label-blind); `HarmonyModel` (the Greenberg/Dryer correlations as directional bias rows, a non-correlating pair carrying no row, the encoded negative result); `sample_profile` (the seeded sampler, keyed `DrawKey(culture, tick, Phase::LANG_TYPOLOGY, slot=canonical position)`, the prior tilted by the already-drawn conditions in Q32.32 with a saturation cap, the tilt gated by a reserved disharmony probability so a disharmonic language stays reachable; a pure function of seed, culture, and data); `TypologyProfile` (a culture's grammar as a canonical vector over the registry, carried by `Language`, inherited through `fork`); `typology_distance` (the 33.5 grammatical component over the generic profile under reserved per-parameter weights, with a `GroundMetric` hook); and `wals_seed` (thirteen WALS parameters as data with per-count provenance). New `Phase::LANG_TYPOLOGY` (0x14) in `crates/core`. Four reserved manifest entries surfaced with basis (`lang.typology_harmony_strong`/`_weak`, `lang.typology_disharmony`, `lang.typology_distance_weights`).

**Tested (11 typology tests).** The seed validates and samples every parameter; sampling replays bit for bit and keys on the culture; registration order never shows in a walk or a draw; harmony is a tendency not a rule (an open disharmony gate is bit-identical to an empty harmony model); the anchor conditions its dependents (a 2^24 tilt binds on a fixed seed, with the tilted weights checked bit-exact); value relabelling permutes the tilted weights exactly (the typology-permutation invariant, the modality-swap analogue); glosses and provenance never reach a draw (label-blindness); a forward-conditioning bias is a load error, not a silent no-op; load errors are loud (missing prior row, unknown value, self-condition, duplicate parameter); distance is symmetric, categorical, and weighted; and the tier weights read fail-loud from the manifest. Workspace green (547 tests, +12), clippy and rustdoc `-D warnings` clean.

**Where it stopped, and what is next.** The substrate is built, proven in isolation, and committed. The item stays OPEN (the R-CMD-ORDER precedent: mechanism built, item open until consolidated). The design-document consolidation (the Part 33.4 mechanism plus a Decided-and-reserved blockquote, record 62.23, a Part 63 bibliography group, the audit Section 1 block and the backlog-bullet rewrite, the counts 28 to 29 resolved and 36 to 35 open) was deferred pending the owner's sign-off on the five decisions the proposal lists as non-final (parameter-set breadth, per-race prior default, harmony granularity, drift-in-scope, genome coupling), which the build implemented as the proposal's recommended defaults; writing them into the design document as "signed off and generalized" without that confirmation would overstate the owner's commitment (Prime Directive 5). On the owner's confirmation the consolidation runs and the item closes; alternatively the owner may adjust a decision, which is data or one manifest entry, not a rebuild. The typological-drift operator (the proposal's section G) is the one deferred mechanism piece, held behind the static generator on the owner's sequencing call.

## 2026-07-01 (continued 13): hardcoded anti-steering guards built, R-DIM-HOMO flagged, R-GPU-CANON-PIN proposal produced

Two threads this stretch, on `claude/physics-substrate-fanout`, workspace green.

**The owner asked whether more hardcoded checks against hallucinated authoring and steering would be valuable, and approved the recommendation.** Built two guards as `crates/physics/tests/steering.rs` (7 physics test binaries now, clippy clean), turning the substrate's most-repeated prose invariants into build-enforced checks: matter is identified by physics not by label (`Substance::content_id` folds the vector, participations, and provenance but never `self.id`, so a race cannot get different substrate behaviour by renaming its iron; the test also confirms it stays physics-sensitive and assembly-order-independent); no value enters the substrate without a provenance (an unsourced axis fails to load as MissingProvenance); a symmetric kernel carries no hidden bias (Coulomb symmetric in the two charges); and the canonical kernel path is integer-only (a source scan of laws.rs rejects any f32/f64, enforcing Part 3.4). The honest ceiling is recorded in the module: these catch MECHANICAL steering (identity-keyed behaviour, an uncited value, a hidden asymmetry, a float leak), not SEMANTIC steering in the CHOICE of axes and laws, which the adversarial red-team still owns. The deeper follow-on, dimensional-homogeneity checking of laws, was flagged as its own research item **R-DIM-HOMO** (Part 58 blockquote, Section 3 bullet, Section 2 entry, open count 33 to 34): a law declares its output Dimension but nothing verifies it against the combination the kernel forms, so give each law a symbolic formula the loader reduces and rejects a mismatch, the axis-monomial discipline extended to laws.

**R-GPU-CANON-PIN research dive (owner chose "both heads toward resolution").** Ran the fan-out (three green domain teams, a determinism red-team recomputing every bit-identity claim, consumer checks, a max-effort synthesis; thirteen confirmed red findings folded) into the hardened proposal `docs/working/GPU_CANON_PIN_PROPOSAL.md`, AWAITING SIGN-OFF. Two heads, one item: the deterministic fixed-point transcendentals that unblock the deferred physics, and cross-vendor GPU bit-identity, answered by the same integer arithmetic. It verified the existing CPU oracle exact (Fixed::mul floors via i128 >>32 then wraps low-64, Fixed::div truncates toward zero, Fixed::sqrt exact isqrt; a 4000-plus-case sweep, zero mismatches, including the MIN corners), pinned the GPU 32-bit-limb sign-magnitude multiply and 96-step restoring divide with a proof they extract the same bits, pinned exp/ln/sin/cos/atan/asin (CORDIC ~32 iterations, range-reduced series) and real pow=exp(y*ln x) each buildable in crates/core sibling to Fixed::sqrt and gated on one reserved precision target apiece, ruled the worldgen noise canonical and the eikonal flow-field integer-canonical-or-quarantined, enumerated the backend/adapter set and the Stage 0 bit-identity gate, and surfaced the mul-floors/div-truncates asymmetry for the owner. It flagged the mul/div-in-a-parallel-reduction case as riding the open R-REDUCE-ORDER, not this item.

**Where it stopped.** The steering guards and the R-DIM-HOMO flag are committed. The GPU proposal is on the table for sign-off; on sign-off, consolidate (Part 3.4/5.4, record 62.23, an audit block, counts 28 to 29 resolved and 34 to 33 open) and build the crates/core transcendentals as the reference oracle (each gated on its owner-set precision target), then audit, which retires the deferred transcendentals across all four physics waves.

## 2026-07-01 (continued 14): built the R-CMD-ORDER key and the shared event-queue substrate (crates/core/src/command.rs)

The owner chose to build the event-queue substrate, first slice R-CMD-ORDER plus the queue, on the current branch (an AskUserQuestion pick, then the recommendation to do R-CMD-ORDER first because it is the shared prerequisite for both engine levers, is an already-open determinism item, and is pure engine mechanics with no reserved values). Grounding confirmed there is no command buffer in code yet (`command_sort_key` is design-only, Part 4.3; the world runs a serial tick with `Registry::mint`), so this is a fresh reusable core primitive built prototype-in-isolation, not a hardening of existing code.

**Built (`crates/core/src/command.rs`, one feat commit).** Three R-CMD-ORDER sub-mechanisms and the substrate: `CommandKey`, the total order (tick, primary `StableId`, kind, emission ordinal) compared lexicographically so any sequence keyed by it is a pure function of the command set independent of insertion order and worker count (the ordinal is the caller's uniqueness contract); `content_id`, deriving an id from canonical content via the FNV-1a `StateHasher` rather than emission order, so an event or spawned entity gets the same id on every replay whatever worker produced it; and `EventQueue<P>`, a `BTreeMap`-backed deterministic future-event list keyed by `CommandKey` with `schedule`/`peek`/`pop`/`drain_due(tick)`, a colliding key surfaced as a `Some` return rather than silently reordered. This is the one substrate both levers drain, agent wake queue (R-AGENT-EXEC) and cross-region core (R-TEMPORAL-LOD), at agent and region granularity.

**Tested.** Six tests: lexicographic ordering; insertion-order-independent drain (the determinism proof, since thread count differs only in insertion interleaving, so insertion-independence is thread-count-independence); `drain_due`; loud collision signalling; content-id purity and agreement with a direct hash; and insertion-independent spawn-id minting in `CommandKey` order (reusing the R-CANON-WALK `canonical_sorted` helper for the barrier mint). Exported from `lib.rs`. Workspace green (527 tests, core now 57), clippy and rustdoc `-D warnings` clean.

**Status of R-CMD-ORDER.** Built as a substrate, prototype-in-isolation, but it stays open in the backlog (like R-CANON-WALK and R-REDUCE-ORDER, whose helpers are built but whose items stay open until adopted): resolution needs the substrate wired into a real command buffer and the tick loop, minting spawn ids at the barrier and content-addressing event ids, plus the R-HARNESS-COVER bit-identity proof. No open count change.

**Addendum, same session (the owner then asked for a cited recommendation on the next order, direction "closed or step toward closed").** Recommended, with citations (audit R-CMD-ORDER; design Part 4.1 ActionStage/ActionApply/Events; the R-HARNESS-COVER coupling at Part 3.5; the PDES total-order determinism result Fujimoto 2017 and McGlohon-Carothers 2021; the R-RNG-COORD adoption precedent record 62.15), that the next order is to build the last missing mechanism piece rather than force a risky live-tick refactor. Grounding found the one live structural-change site is the dialogue write pass (world.rs:~1709), whose question-answer `in_reply_to` linkage uses positional indices into the pending vec, so a CommandKey reorder would invalidate them: live adoption is an intricate dialogue refactor that can change canonical dialogue ordering and couples to R-HARNESS-COVER, so it wants an explicit go, not an autonomous change. Built `CommandBuffer<C>`, the `ActionApply` barrier (design Part 4.1): workers push commands in any order, the barrier drains them in `CommandKey` order (reusing the R-CANON-WALK `canonical_sorted`) and mints spawn ids at the barrier, proven thread-count-independent by construction (a test pushes the same commands forward, reversed, and rotated and asserts identical application and identical minted spawn ids). This completes R-CMD-ORDER's mechanism (`CommandKey` + `content_id` + `EventQueue` + `CommandBuffer`). Workspace green (531 tests, core now 58), clippy and rustdoc clean.

**Where it stopped, and what is next.** R-CMD-ORDER's mechanism is complete and proven in isolation; the item stays open (no count change) until adopted in the live tick plus the R-HARNESS-COVER thread-sweep proof. The one adoption site is the dialogue write pass, which needs its positional `in_reply_to` linkage re-plumbed to survive a canonical reorder, and the change can shift the dialogue event and effect order, so it is the explicit next decision for the owner. Alternatives when the owner calls it: the self-scheduled wake layer over the substrate (the closed-form homeostatic-reserve crossing plus the place/percept subscription, R-AGENT-EXEC follow-on), or the per-subsystem coarse-step registry on the temporal-LOD side. The physics fan-out stays separate.

**Second addendum, same session (the owner asked for the decision explained and an order recommended; direction "closed or step toward closed").** Explained that R-CMD-ORDER cannot be fully closed now because it guards parallel-tick determinism and the parallel tick is deferred (Rayon held off per the scheduler pass), so the mechanism is ready and waiting and forcing closure means premature parallelism or a partial serial adoption. Recommended R-HARNESS-COVER as the highest-value "toward closed" move (it makes the whole determinism cluster provable and has standalone value as the CI contract), over the alternatives (the R-AGENT-EXEC wake layer as forward progress; the dialogue adoption as the risky partial; bringing the parallel tick forward as against the scheduler pass). The owner chose R-HARNESS-COVER "with a sight on Rayon."

**Built the harness (one test commit).** Grounding: the existing harness (`crates/core/tests/determinism.rs`) proved only order-INDEPENDENT machinery (per-entity accumulation, associative and near-overflow reduction, the last two including the C-05 case), which by construction cannot catch a command-ordering or iteration-order break; the thread-sweep scaffold (1/4/N via `std::thread::scope`) and `TwoTierWorld` snapshot support already existed. Added the order-SENSITIVE core case, `command_application_is_thread_count_independent`: worker threads produce commands concurrently round-robin (so the pre-barrier order scrambles differently at each thread count), the `CommandBuffer` barrier drains in `CommandKey` order and mints spawn ids, asserted bit-identical across 1/3/4/machine-width workers, the tick's ActionStage/ActionApply (Part 4.1) under real OS threads and the exact property Rayon will stress. Added the sim harness (`crates/sim/tests/determinism_harness.rs`): the real dawn tick (the `tick_bench` fixture) replayed bit for bit (state hash and event-log hash each tick over the gossip partition, dialogue write pass, naming game, and structural mutation), a seed-sensitivity counter-check, and a two-tier save/load/continued-replay cycle proving the snapshot restores the id high-water marks so a reload tracks the original bit for bit. Workspace green (535 tests, +4), clippy and rustdoc `-D warnings` clean.

**Status and next.** R-HARNESS-COVER now exercises every path it names except the tick-level thread sweep, which awaits the parallel tick; it stays open (no count change), built with that sight, so when Rayon lands the same replay harness runs the tick at a sweep of worker counts and closes both this and R-CMD-ORDER's adoption proof. So the determinism cluster (R-CMD-ORDER mechanism, R-CANON-WALK, R-REDUCE-ORDER helpers, R-HARNESS-COVER harness) is now all built and proving-ready, converging on one gate: the parallel (Rayon) tick, which the scheduler pass deferred pending a full-tick profile. The next natural decisions for the owner: bring the parallel tick forward now to close the cluster, or build forward on the R-AGENT-EXEC wake layer over the event-queue substrate. The physics fan-out stays separate.

## 2026-07-01 (continued 12): event-driven agent execution scoped and flagged (R-AGENT-EXEC), the breadth-axis lever

The owner picked event-driven agent execution as the next engine lever (an AskUserQuestion pick), then said "Go ahead." It is Part 57's second foundation, the agent execution model, and the breadth-axis companion to temporal LOD: temporal LOD makes deep time cheap by coarsening quiet spans, event-driven execution makes a dense present cheap by not stepping idle agents, and the two share one event-queue substrate at region and agent granularity. All on `claude/physics-substrate-fanout`.

**Grounding.** `World::tick` (crates/sim/src/world.rs:1210) is the poll-everything loop Part 57 flags: six phases, each a full sweep over every placed mind every tick. The profiling (85 us/tick at 100 beings, converse+language dominant) is the idle-agent tax it removes. Three pieces of the answer are already present: the tick applies a total-canonical-order stimulus batch (a proto event queue), the `DrawKey` schema keys each draw on its coordinate, and `colocated_index` is the spatial subscription.

**The scoping pass (`docs/event_driven_execution_research.md`).** Five sub-problems, each mapped to existing machinery: (1) the event queue and reproducible wake order are R-CMD-ORDER's total key on the time axis; (2) waking an idle agent is a self-scheduled crossing of a draining homeostatic reserve (R-BEHAVIOR-EVOLVE, solved in closed form) plus the co-location/percept subscription; (3) it is the fine-tier complement of temporal LOD over one shared event-queue substrate; (4) determinism across threads is the deterministic-scheduler-and-draw-key discipline on the woken subset; (5) the crossover where dense interaction makes a flat sweep win is the honest breadth limit (the twin of temporal LOD's busy-time bound). Build factors and prerequisites (R-CMD-ORDER, the deterministic scheduler, R-HARNESS-COVER) and reserved values (the dense-region crossover, the non-closed-form re-check interval) are enumerated.

**The external survey.** The deep-research harness failed mid-run on an internal StructuredOutput retry-cap error (not network), so the survey was run directly with WebSearch and each claim cross-checked. Verified and folded into "The external literature, verified": the reproducibility crux is simultaneous-event ordering, and the field's fix is a total tie-break key (the engine's R-CMD-ORDER route), with Parallel DEVS (Chow and Zeigler 1994) confirming it by replacing classic DEVS's ad-hoc select with the confluent transition function; the event queue is a solved O(1) structure (calendar queue, Brown 1988; ladder queue, Tang, Goh, Thng 2005); event-driven execution saves the idle majority and converges with time-stepping on the active crowd (hybrid tick-plus-event designs, MOSAIK 3.0). Sources cited with honest attributions (no invented author names for the title-only results).

**The formal flag (R-AGENT-EXEC, open count 34 to 35).** An additive Needs-research blockquote at the Part 57 site in `docs/design.md` (all existing text preserved, inserted before the R-REDUCE-ORDER blockquote), a Section 3 engine-fidelity backlog bullet in `docs/audit.md` (the subsection retitled to include Part 57 and note the breadth-axis fourth question), the Section 2 engine-fidelity paragraph extended, the Limitation count bumped to thirty-five with R-AGENT-EXEC named, and a TODOS open bullet plus the count line. Verified: 0 em dashes and 0 banned adverbs across design, audit, and the scoping doc; parts gapless (64); fences balanced (208); backlog count moved by exactly one (34 to 35); single backlog bullet for the new item.

**Where it stopped, and what is next.** Event-driven execution is scoped, surveyed, and flagged as open research. The build recognition that matters: R-AGENT-EXEC and R-TEMPORAL-LOD share the reproducible event-queue substrate, so if the owner directs a build, the first stage (the deterministic event queue keyed by the command-order key) serves both levers. R-CMD-ORDER is the near-term prerequisite for either. Next is the owner's call: build the shared event-queue substrate, take another lever, or return to the physics fan-out (kept separate; waves 0 to 3 resolved, wave 4 the electromagnetic-wave and electronics tier when called).

## 2026-07-01 (continued 11): temporal LOD deep-researched, verified against the primary sources, and formally flagged (R-TEMPORAL-LOD)

The owner asked to look into temporal level of detail, scope it, and deep-research it, preserving the existing documentation and intent, then said "when the research comes back take a look at what the next engine lever is." All on the branch the temporal work sits on (`claude/physics-substrate-fanout`; the owner's separate directive was to keep the physics fan-out separate, which it stays).

**The scoping pass (`docs/temporal_lod_research.md`, from the earlier part of this session).** Decomposes Part 32 into five sub-problems and maps each to existing machinery or a known external technique: the coarse population step is tau-leaping; coarse-fine agreement is conservation, not identity (R-TIER-CONSIST, resolved); cross-region reconciliation is conservative PDES; the schedule is Part 54's significance-and-seed schedule on the time axis; and the busy-time fast-forward limit is preserved exactly as the design states it. A companion section scopes R-VIEW-ELAB (the two halves of one bargain: run the world cheap, watch it in full). Nothing in Part 32, Part 54, or the R-VIEW-ELAB flag was changed; the pass decomposes and grounds.

**The deep-research verification (this continuation).** A fan-out deep-research pass (search, fetch, three-vote adversarial verification per claim) confirmed the technique mapping against the primary sources and sharpened three points, now folded into the doc's "The external literature, verified" section: the conservation-exact form is binomial tau-leaping (the binomial cap is the temporal analogue of R-TIER-CONSIST's exact conservation), tuned by a critical-firing threshold (about five to fifteen, Cao/Gillespie/Petzold 2005); cross-region reconciliation is conservative (Chandy-Misra-Bryant lookahead), not optimistic Time Warp (Jefferson 1985, whose rollback timing is the determinism hazard the engine cannot adopt), with the subtle Jefferson-Barnes result that even an optimistic simulator can be made externally deterministic noted but declined; and the fundamental limit is provable and shared, matching the design. Added the critical-firing threshold to the reserved-values section (surfaced with its basis, not set) and a Sources section citing the primary literature grouped by sub-problem.

**The formal flag (R-TEMPORAL-LOD, open count 33 to 34).** An additive Needs-research blockquote at the Part 32 site in `docs/design.md` (all existing Part 32 text preserved), a Section 3 engine-fidelity backlog bullet in `docs/audit.md` (the subsection retitled to include Part 32 and note the temporal clock as the third open engine-fidelity question), a Section 2 engine-fidelity paragraph, the Limitation count bumped to thirty-four with R-TEMPORAL-LOD named, a TODOS open bullet next to its sibling R-VIEW-ELAB, and the TODOS count line bumped. Verified: 0 em dashes and 0 banned adverbs across design, audit, and research; parts gapless (64); fences balanced (208); backlog count moved by exactly one (33 to 34); records 62 sequential; single flag site in the design.

**Where it stopped, and what is next.** Temporal LOD is scoped, verified, and flagged as open research ready to build (the per-subsystem coarse-step registry and the conservative cross-region synchronization core, each prototyped in isolation). The owner's standing instruction is to assess the next engine lever now the research is back; the leading candidate is event-driven agent execution (Part 57's second foundation, the biggest open lever for thousands of active agents), to be weighed against the coarse-processing sentient tier and data-parallelism (the scheduler plus Rayon) before recommending.

## 2026-07-01 (continued 10): wave 3 signed off, built, consolidated, and audited (R-PHYS-W3)

The owner signed off the wave-3 recommendations ("go ahead, I sign off on your recs"). Built, consolidated as resolved, and audited end to end on `claude/physics-substrate-fanout`, workspace green throughout.

**The fifth base dimension (S-ELEC-DIM), the owner's Principle-9 call, taken.** `Dimension` (crates/physics/src/lib.rs) gains a fifth i8 base, electric current (the SI ampere), because electricity is the first quantity dimensionally independent of the four earlier bases and the Gaussian half-integer alternative is unrepresentable in the integer monomial. Backward-compatible: `parse_dimension` accepts a four-tuple (current zero) or a five-tuple, so every earlier floor loads unchanged; Mul/Div and the content hash fold the fifth field; new named dims CURRENT, CHARGE, VOLTAGE; neutrality-by-construction preserved over five bases (proven by test). The wave-2 `chem.standard_potential` is promoted from its dimensionless proxy to the volt (dimension-and-scale only, no re-fit), unified with `elec.potential` and `elec.emf` (kept role-distinct on the owner's acceptance).

**Built (one feat commit, then an audit-fix commit).** The EM floor as data (`crates/physics/data/em_floor.toml`: 8 elec.* and 6 mag.* axes, 15 laws, copper and lodestone), a discoverable deepening tier (tier 2), merged onto the mechanical, fluids, and chem/optics floors (four-floor load proof: 81 axes, 60 laws, 6 substances). Fifteen closed-form fixed-point kernels in laws.rs: coulomb_force (sign-tracked inverse-square), ohm_voltage, circuit_current (short to the cap), power_dissipation, capacitor_energy, battery_emf (the galvanic unification, Daniell cell ~1.10 V), resistance (geometric, open to the cap), solenoid_field (mu_0 early, iron ~6.28 T), flux_linkage, motor_force, lorentz_force, dipole_torque (max, sin deferred), faraday_emf and inductive_emf (the per-tick delta signed by Lenz), inductor_energy. 21 EM tests.

**Consolidated as resolved (R-PHYS-W3, resolved 27 to 28).** design.md Part 41 wave-3 subsection and blockquote, Part 55 fifth-base note, record 62.22, a Part 63 group, audit block 1x, counts. A fourth partial advance of R-DEEPTECH-PHYSICS.

**Audited (a three-lens adversarial pass, all four confirmed findings fixed and regression-tested).** The load-bearing catch was a regression from the fifth-base change: `PhysicsRegistry::content_id` hashed only four of the five dimension exponents, so two registries differing only in a current exponent (e.g. the shipped voltage vs power law dimensions) hashed identically; fixed to fold `d.current` in both the axis and law paths, with a regression test. Also fixed: `capacitor_energy` (and its dual `inductor_energy`) carried a redundant voltage/current-only guard that spuriously capped a small capacitor or inductor at a high voltage or current (the staged checked multiplies already route overflow to the cap), the guards removed and `inductor_energy`'s now-dead i2_max param dropped, regression-tested; and the `parse_dimension` doc updated for the fifth exponent. Physics green (6 binaries), workspace green (36), clippy and rustdoc `-D warnings` clean.

**Where it stopped.** Wave 3 is complete: signed off, built, consolidated as resolved, and audited. Cleanly rebased over the parallel temporal-LOD and R-VIEW-ELAB scoping commits that landed meanwhile. The physics-substrate fan-out now has waves 0 to 3 resolved and built (biology; mechanics/materials/thermal; fluids/chemistry/optics; electricity/magnetism). Remaining named deferrals ride R-GPU-CANON-PIN and wave 4 (full Maxwell wave propagation, the AC frequency domain and the tuned LC resonant circuit toward radio, the dipole sin factor, the B-H saturation loop, and the Nernst/Arrhenius/Tafel transcendentals); the wave-2 colour vector stack and acoustic transmission slice-kernels remain specced-but-unbuilt; the discovery-mechanism coupling is R-DEEPTECH-SCIENCE. Next wave when the owner calls it: wave 4 (the electromagnetic-wave and electronics tier) or the deferred slice-kernels.

## 2026-07-01 (continued 9): the wave-3 electricity-and-magnetism proposal landed, awaiting sign-off

The wave-3 fan-out (two green domain teams, a red Steering-Audit per domain, four consumer-interface checks, a max-effort synthesis) produced `docs/working/ELECTROMAGNETISM_SUBSTRATE_PROPOSAL.md`, awaiting the owner's sign-off. Electrostatics and current (9 axes, 7 laws), magnetism and induction (4 axes, 5 laws), ten confirmed red findings folded. E and M is framed as a discoverable deepening tier under R-DEEPTECH-PHYSICS (three instrument-and-theory rungs, never a per-race unlock table), not a Tier-0 floor.

The load-bearing catch is **S-ELEC-DIM**, surfaced for the owner rather than decided: electricity is the first quantity dimensionally independent of length, mass, time, and temperature, so it forces a fifth base dimension into the locked `Dimension` monomial (Part 55). The Gaussian half-integer route is unrepresentable in the `i8` exponent, so the SI ampere (electric current) as the fifth base is recommended, backward-compatible with every earlier axis at current exponent 0, neutrality-by-construction preserved. Because it touches the locked representation, it is the owner's Principle-9 call and the gate on the whole wave. Also surfaced: the optional `elec.potential`/`elec.emf` collapse, the volt promotion of `chem.standard_potential` (dimension-only, no re-fit), and the reserved-values batch (all in TODOS). Deferred and named: full Maxwell wave propagation, AC/frequency-domain and the tuned LC circuit (radio, wave 4), the dipole sin(theta), and the Nernst/Tafel transcendentals.

Counts unchanged (27 resolved, 33 open): wave 3 stays a proposal until sign-off, then consolidates into Part 41/55/58 and builds on the wave-2 recipe. This is where the autonomous run stopped for morning review.

## 2026-07-01 (continued 8): wave 2 completeness-checked, consolidated, built, and audited; wave 3 in flight

The owner signed off the two wave-2 unification decisions and said "consolidate and implement, wire, and audit, then move on to the next wave autonomously," and to completeness-check the other domains (even wave 1). All on `claude/physics-substrate-fanout` (PR #7), workspace green throughout.

**Completeness passes (two consumer-lens fan-outs, strict-verified).** Fluids: 13 Tier-0 folds, the weather water cycle (`fluid.vapor_pressure`, an affine saturation-vapour proxy, bidirectional evaporation, thermal buoyancy, and the ideal-gas density coupling temperature to the density field) and acoustics (the speed of sound, catching that `Fixed::sqrt` was wrongly deferred, plus frequency/source-power/absorption axes and acoustic impedance and transmission for the acoustic channel and hearing). Chemistry/optics: 7 folds, the radiative-equilibrium temperature (the term that SETS a surface's temperature from absorbed light), a data-defined colour stack (vision, signalling, camouflage), and dissolution (medicine and leaching). Wave 1 takes NO addition: the bow's elastic energy composes from `mat.elastic_modulus` plus strain into `law.impact`, torque and gearing from `law.lever` and `law.power`, self-heating from `law.sensible_heat`. All banked in the proposal addenda (Sections 11 to 12).

**Consolidated as resolved (R-PHYS-W2, resolved 26 to 27).** design.md Part 41 wave-2 subsection plus the Decided-and-reserved blockquote, record 62.21, a Part 63 bibliography group, audit block 1w, the counts. The two unification decisions applied (the single density axis widened to gases, the canonical `therm.temperature` state axis).

**Built (three feat commits, then an audit-fix commit).** The fluids floor (`crates/physics/data/fluids_floor.toml`, 15 axes, 15 laws) and the chemistry-and-optics floor (`chem_optics_floor.toml`, 14 axes, 11 laws) as loadable data merged onto the wave-1 floor via a new `PhysicsRegistry::extend` (a wave loads onto the previous floor rather than duplicating the shared axes it reads). 26 closed-form fixed-point kernels in `laws.rs`, staged against the Q32.32 ceiling: the fluids set (hydrostatic and dynamic pressure, buoyancy, drag and aerodynamic lift, Reynolds, Laplace, compressibility, convective flux, Poiseuille, speed of sound via `sqrt` after a divide so water's modulus never overflows, ideal-gas density, thermal buoyancy, the saturation-vapour and evaporation proxies), the chemistry set (Hess reaction, corrosion, Carnot, dissolution), and the optics set (Stefan-Boltzmann radiant emission with sigma interleaved through the four temperature multiplies, Wien, inverse-square, the conserving interface split, optical depth, refractive contrast, and radiative-equilibrium temperature as two nested `Fixed::sqrt`). `mat.density` widened 100 to 0.08 for gases. 18 physics tests prove the merged load and physical values (air sound ~340, water ~1480, Carnot 0.4 at 500/300 K, radiative equilibrium ~255 K, R+A+T conserved) and determinism.

**Audited (a four-lens adversarial pass, all confirmed findings fixed and regression-tested).** Three low-severity adversarial-input panics fixed (a saturating `sat_abs` so `Fixed::MIN` velocity routes to the cap in dynamic_pressure, drag/lift, reynolds, and the wave-1 kinetic_energy; an i128 `sat_sub` so `reaction` cannot panic on opposite-signed saturated sums; `interface_split` clamps its fractions to `[0,1]`), and one medium discipline finding (the wave-2 tomls loaded ranges as owner-SET, so all 29 axes converted to `range_reserved`, fail-loud reserved-with-basis pending the owner's set, the discipline the wave-1 second-moment axis uses). Physics green (5 binaries), clippy and rustdoc `-D warnings` clean.

**Where it stopped.** Wave 2 is complete: completeness-checked, consolidated as resolved, built, and audited. Remaining wave-2 build (honest handoff): the colour vector stack (`spectral_response`, `chromatic_contrast`) and the acoustic impedance/transmission slice-kernels are specced in the proposal but not built (they need slice plumbing like `net_nutrition`); deeper consumer wiring (the body-arc temperature exchange via `convective_flux`/`radiant_emission`, fluid ignition via `combustion`) is mostly blocked on the located world and the unbuilt weather/light systems, the floor now loadable and its kernels callable being the wireable part. Wave 3 (electricity and magnetism) is in flight as a fan-out producing a hardened proposal (electrostatics, current, magnetism, induction; the volt unification promoting the wave-2 `chem.standard_potential` proxy to its real dimension; the motor, generator, battery; R-COMMS's signal half), for the owner's review; on sign-off it consolidates and builds on the wave-2 recipe.

## 2026-07-01 (continued 7): profiled the tick, and a 5x profile-guided win the scheduler would have missed

The owner asked for a before/after profiling test (a hundred people, a long run) to see how much the scheduler work improves things. Profiling first changed the answer, and it is the point of the pass. On `claude/physics-substrate-fanout`, all 275 sim tests pass, clippy clean.

**The profiler (`crates/sim/examples/tick_bench.rs`, `World::tick_timed`).** Seeds N beings across co-located bands into a running dawn `World` (language, dialogue, gossip installed, everyone promoted) and times the serial tick over many iterations, with a per-phase breakdown. `tick_timed` is a non-canonical profiling method (reads `Instant`, never enters state), so the reported `state_hash` is unchanged. Baseline (100 beings, 10 bands, 10000 ticks, release): 415 us/tick, 2407 ticks/sec, and 100 in-world years at full per-tick fidelity would take about 364 hours (the number that motivates temporal LOD).

**The finding.** The per-phase breakdown put about 99.5% of the tick in two phases, converse (dialogue) 52% and language (the naming game) 47%, and near-zero everywhere else. So the cross-system deterministic scheduler would not have helped this workload at all: the cost is inside two phases, not spread across independent systems. What the profile pointed at instead was an O(N^2) co-location scan: three phases (converse_language, converse, gossip) each had every speaker rescan all N minds to find its co-located listeners.

**The change (`crates/sim/src/world.rs`).** A `colocated_index` helper groups placed minds by place once per phase in canonical mind-id order, turning each scan from O(N^2) to O(N). The per-place lists are in the same mind-id order the old filter produced, so the draws that index into a listener list are unchanged and the tick replays bit for bit: the `state_hash` after the change is identical to before (`78d6f0c2...`), and every determinism, dawn, language, and dialogue test passes. Result at 100 beings: 415 to 85 us/tick, a 4.9x speedup (converse 8.5x, language 3.5x; language is now the dominant remaining cost, since the naming game is inherently sequential within a band). 100 in-world years at full fidelity dropped from 364 to 75 hours.

**The lesson, and where it leaves the scheduler.** Profile before optimizing (Part 13): the scheduler was the plausible next build, and the profile showed a 5x serial win it would have missed. The scheduler and Rayon remain the right substrate for parallelism (the design pass stands), but this workload's remaining cost is intra-phase data-parallelism across independent bands (language, sequential within a band, independent across bands), not cross-system scheduling, and the honest next step is to weigh that band-level data-parallelism against just running fewer minds per base tick under significance-driven fidelity. Nothing consolidated; the scheduler cluster stays open pending sign-off of `docs/deterministic_scheduler_design.md`.

---

## 2026-07-01 (continued 6): the deterministic-scheduler design pass and the canon-walk/reduce hardening

The owner asked for work orthogonal to the physics fan-out, picked the deterministic scheduler (Part 57), and asked to explore whether to adopt hecs and Rayon now or hold off. On `claude/physics-substrate-fanout`, workspace green, core clippy clean.

**Grounded the true state first (the backlog was stale).** The determinism red-team's original claims have partly been overtaken: every canonical id newtype now derives `Ord` (the R-CANON-WALK bullet's "PoolId and EventId lack Ord" is stale), and the C-05 parallel-sum seam is closed (`Fixed::sum_bits` folds in i128, order-independent even when a prefix would overflow). `world.rs` states the serial tick is deliberate and the parallel scheduler is left for R-CMD-ORDER/R-REDUCE-ORDER. So what is open is narrower: no unified canonical-iteration helper (per-container `BTreeMap` convention plus the one `Registry::entries_sorted`), no general `canonical_reduce`, and no scheduler.

**Design pass (`docs/deterministic_scheduler_design.md`), for owner sign-off.** The four items resolve as one substrate (Part 57 says R-REDUCE-ORDER settles with the scheduler's declared read/write sets): the scheduler derives a deterministic order plus safe parallel batches purely from each system's declared resource read/write sets, keyed on stable system ids, storage-agnostic; R-CANON-WALK becomes a structural rule plus one sanctioned iteration helper; R-REDUCE-ORDER a sort-then-fold primitive; R-CMD-ORDER a total command key specified now and wired when the command stage lands. **The hecs/Rayon call: hold off on both this pass.** The scheduler reasons over declared resources that map equally to a `BTreeMap` today or a hecs column later, so it needs neither; adopting hecs is the owner's deferred storage-at-scale call, and switching on Rayon before the guardrails are proven makes R-CMD/REDUCE/CANON live blockers at once (the red-team's own "stay serial"), and there is no profile yet showing the serial tick is the limit. Build the substrate storage- and parallelism-agnostic now, hecs- and Rayon-ready by design, adopt neither; both are unblocked, not required, by this pass.

**Built the buildable-now, open primitives (`crates/core/src/canonical.rs`).** `canonical_sorted` (the single sanctioned canonical-iteration path, R-CANON-WALK) and `canonical_reduce` (sort by total key then fold, the non-associative-combine primitive, R-REDUCE-ORDER), the general form of what `sum_bits` already does for the associative sum. Storage- and parallelism-agnostic, no reserved values (structural). Four tests including a non-associative combine proving the arrival-order nondeterminism is pinned out.

**Where it stopped.** The scheduler core (declared-access to layered-schedule, still over the serial tick with the phases expressed as declared-access systems) and R-CMD-ORDER's wiring wait on owner sign-off of the design pass. The items stay open (nothing consolidated yet). hecs and Rayon deliberately not touched.

## 2026-07-01 (continued 5): the owner's wiring calls, the hidden-width lever, and the wave-2 fan-out

Three things this stretch, all on `claude/physics-substrate-fanout` (PR #7), workspace green throughout.

**The controller hidden-width lever.** On a compute-tax exploration (`crates/sim/examples/controller_tax.rs`, a probe measuring per-tick and per-individual cost across the hidden-width sweep, with the evolved-controller literature for the sufficiency basis), the owner set `behavior.controller_hidden_width = 4` as a tuneable lever (audit 6d, reserved.toml `set`, the Part 8.4 reserved list annotated). The per-tick tax at h=4 is about 2x the reaction norm and negligible at focus scale; the express O(weight_count^2) path wants a channel index before h grows past single digits.

**The wiring decisions (owner).** On what it takes to integrate the biosphere, dawn, evolved controller, homeostasis, locomotion, and body arc into one running World: defer the quantitative breeding-value tier (25.10) and seed controllers at the dawn (evolving at the sampled-episode tier); keep the life cadence at 1 in-world year until 1 month is profiled; promote within the focus region (recommendation); stay StableId-keyed for the first slice and decide hecs-vs-grow at scale; the 20 body/behavior/physiology reserved values are the calibration batch for a fail-loud world. Recorded in TODOS. The honest headline: a dev-fixture demo slice needs no owner call; the production/at-scale wiring needs the batch and the entity-storage call.

**Wave 2 run (fluids, chemistry, optics).** The owner chose wave 2 as the next move. Ran the fan-out (three green domain teams, a red Steering-Audit per domain recomputing the Q32.32 by hand, six consumer-interface checks, a max-effort synthesis) and produced the hardened proposal `docs/working/FLUIDS_CHEMISTRY_OPTICS_SUBSTRATE_PROPOSAL.md`, awaiting sign-off. Fluids (9 axes, 9 laws), chemistry (8, 5), optics (10, 7); 19 confirmed red findings folded; six consumer contracts. It absorbs the wave-1 reach ceilings (hydrostatics, buoyancy, convective and radiant heat, the Carnot heat-to-work limit) and, on the owner's later ask, adds the dynamic-pressure force family: drag and aerodynamic lift (the layer that floors flight and realistic ballistics, as reduced-order lumped-coefficient laws, with turbulence the deferred categorical ceiling). Five unification decisions taken (one density axis widened to gases, a canonical `therm.temperature` state axis, one energy-per-mass scale, one activation-barrier concept, the pinned megapascal). The design docs and counts are unchanged (26 resolved, 33 open) since wave 2 stays a proposal until sign-off.

**Where it stopped.** Wave 2's proposal is on the table for sign-off. On sign-off: consolidate into Part 41/58/35 (records, audit blocks, counts) and build the kernels (the wave-1 recipe). The owner also asked about the deepening beyond wave 2 (wave 3 electricity-and-magnetism, then electronics/semiconductors, then nuclear/orbital, plus the tier-opening discovery mechanism that is the R-DEEPTECH-PHYSICS/R-DEEPTECH-SCIENCE coupling, with turbulence/chaos the permanent offline-proxy boundary) and where lift lands (wave 2's fluids floor, answered above).

## 2026-07-01 (continued 4): the body arc built and resolved end to end (R-BUILD-PHYS, R-WOUND, R-FLUID)

The owner set the controller hidden width to 4 (a tuneable lever on a compute-tax exploration; audit 6d, reserved.toml `set`), then chose to take the physiology/anatomy/wound arc as one dive and, autonomously, scope and wire all that is buildable into the engine and audit it, for morning review. Done, on `claude/physics-substrate-fanout` (PR #7), workspace green and clippy and rustdoc `-D warnings` clean throughout.

**Scope (autonomous forks recorded).** A grounding fan-out over Part 35, Part 20, Part 25.13, the resolved floors, and the built substrate produced `docs/working/BODY_ARC_SCOPE.md`. The fork calls, taken on the on-ideology endpoint (Part 25.13, a wound measured from the floor) and the standing defaults: a wound is measured not stored; damage modes a data registry keyed to a fixed physics measurement family; a fluid a data-defined substance with a conserved volume stock; integrity derived not stored (Part 35's own rule); the raw morphology primitive and the mechanical stats derived; a strike closes predator-prey.

**Built (`crates/sim/src/body.rs`, 11 unit tests; `tests/embodiment.rs`, 2 integration tests).** A per-part `Body` promoted from the Part 25.14 anatomy vector (R-BUILD-PHYS): typed parts of layered tissue materials (data over the R-PHYS-MECH `mat.*`/`therm.*` axes), a vital torso carrying the fluid vessel, a vital head, a limb per locomotion mode, a part per natural weapon; reach derived through the resolved reach law. Wounds measured (R-WOUND): `apply_insult` dispatches a data-defined `DamageMode` to a fixed `MeasureKind` (penetration walking the tissue layers so a bone core stops a weak cut, the dual fracture criterion on the strongest layer, constrained thermal stress), a cut clean through severs, a deep wound breaches the vessel, an exotic mode with no built law does nothing rather than fabricating. Fluids (R-FLUID): a data-defined `FluidKind` with a composition and a conserved volume `Stock`, bleeding from a breach netted against a clot rate, death past the critical fraction. Derived `integrity`/`is_alive` (no competing store). A `strike` enactment (weapon mass and blow through the kinetic-energy law). The loop to R-BEHAVIOR-EVOLVE closed: a derived integrity homeostatic axis refreshed from the body (`homeostasis::dev_embodied`, `set_level`) so the controller reads its condition, and a `dev_predator` affordance registry with a STRIKE output a controller can evolve to use. All race-blind, integer, float-free, no RNG in the measurement, so a body's condition replays bit for bit. Reserved values (`body.*`) surfaced with basis.

**Consolidated as resolved (autonomous, pending morning review).** design.md Part 35 rewritten from the authored `Wound`/`FluidKind` enums to the data-defined measured mechanism plus the Decided-and-reserved blockquote; record 62.20 (the three items as one arc, the language-cluster precedent); a Part 63 bibliography group; the R-BUILD-PHYS/R-WOUND cross-references at Parts 20, 25.13, 33 reconciled from open to resolved. audit.md consolidation block 1v, the three Section 3 bullets rewritten to resolved, the Limitation counts (23 to 26 resolved, 36 to 33 open). reserved.toml `body.*` block. TODOS mirrored. Verify suite clean: em dashes 0, banned adverbs 0, parts gapless 64, fences balanced, records sequential to 62.20, open backlog 33, no stale references, no discouraged constructions in the new prose.

**Audit.** A four-lens adversarial review (determinism, steering, arithmetic, doc-faithfulness) ran over the build; findings folded (see the commit). 

**Where it stopped.** The body arc is built into the engine and consolidated as resolved (26 resolved, 33 open), pending the owner's morning review of the autonomous fork calls (recorded in the scope doc). Honest limits carried in record 62.20: the penetration read caps depth by the layer walk rather than depleting energy layer by layer; the temperature axis is a reserved two-sided band whose environmental thermal exchange waits on the located world and a medium floor; corrosion and full fluid chemistry wait on wave-2; the digestion model R-BIOSPHERE deferred here is reserved. Next, on the owner's go: set the `body.*` and `behavior.*`/`physiology.*` reserved calibrations; the dawn-World-to-map bridge (put the walking, foraging, now woundable beings on the generated map); or wave-2 physics (fluids, chemistry, optics), which the fluid interactions and the temperature exchange want.

## 2026-07-01 (continued 3): branches consolidated, and R-BEHAVIOR-EVOLVE built and resolved end to end

Picked up mid-stream: got up to date, set the guiding principle (physics in, emergent behaviour out, Principle 9), consolidated the branch work, and built out the whole R-BEHAVIOR-EVOLVE block (Stages 2 to 4), consolidated it as resolved, and adversarially audited it. All on `claude/physics-substrate-fanout` (PR #7).

**Consolidation of the branches.** The evolved-behaviour work had landed on a newer branch, `claude/arbitrary-tickrate-step-6e32ma` (arbitrary tick rate and live playback, emergent locomotion, the R-BEHAVIOR-EVOLVE flag and design pass, and Stage 1). It was a strict superset of the PR branch, so a fast-forward of `claude/physics-substrate-fanout` absorbed all four commits under PR #7, unifying the line. No other branch carried orphan work (the older `tom-update-audit` commits are already content-merged). Workspace green (32 test binaries) after consolidation.

**R-BEHAVIOR-EVOLVE, Stages 2 to 4 (the owner's "implement all phases of this block, audit at the end").** The seam the design pass names: a data-defined action-and-drive repertoire is still authored, so a non-sentient creature's policy is steering (Principle 9). The fix is a policy that is not written but evolved. Stage 2 (`crates/sim/src/controller.rs`, genome `Channel::Controller(ControllerParamId)`): a heritable controller expressed from a new genome channel (sibling to cognition and composition), a data-defined layout over the Stage-1 homeostatic and affordance registries, evaluated as a fixed-point clamped-linear reaction norm (a per-axis reserve, an on-tile source flag, and a source direction in, an activation and heading per affordance out), order-independent i128 accumulation, no float, no RNG. Wired into `locomotion.rs`, retiring the authored `Behaviour` from that path (the `Walker` now carries a `Homeostasis` and a `Controller`; the `decision` layer stays the sentient deliberative tier's interface, untouched). The `walkers` example shows foragers survive and a blank-controller being dies of thirst. Stage 3 (`crates/sim/src/evolve.rs`, `Phase::CONTROLLER`): fitness is homeostatic survival (an episode through the movement-and-metabolism physics), never a resemblance to expected behaviour; an individual-based evolutionary loop (random founders, truncation selection, bounded controller-weight mutation) produces water-seeking behaviour from random controllers, proven by tests (mean survival rises, the evolved best heads for water when dry though no one wrote it); the coefficient wires into the aggregate-tier `GenePool::select`. Stage 4: the same plumbing graduates to a small fixed-point recurrent network (a carried hidden state, clamp the nonlinearity, the reserved hidden width the only change), and a full search-based dawn-tier episode scorer. 37 tests across the four modules, workspace green, clippy and rustdoc `-D warnings` clean.

**Consolidated as resolved (on the signed-off design pass and the completed build).** design.md: the mechanism as Part 8.4 with the Decided-and-reserved blockquote, record 62.19, a Part 63 bibliography group. audit.md: consolidation block 1u, the Section 3 bullet rewritten to resolved, the Limitation counts (22 to 23 resolved, 37 to 36 open). TODOS mirrored. `calibration/reserved.toml`: eight new reserved-with-basis entries (`physiology.intake_yield`, `behavior.*`). Verify suite clean: em dashes 0, banned adverbs 0, parts gapless 64, fences balanced, records sequential to 62.19, open backlog 36, no discouraged constructions in the new prose, no duplicate structs.

**Adversarial audit (four lenses: determinism, steering, arithmetic, doc-faithfulness, each finding verified).** No steering leaks (behaviour is evolved, fitness is survival), no determinism defects, no doc overclaim. Two confirmed latent defects, both fixed and re-verified: `ControllerParamId` was a `u16`, so a controller with more than 65536 weights would silently alias two weights onto one channel (widened to `u32`, free since `Channel` is already `u32`-sized via `TraitId`); and `evolve` panicked on a zero-size population (guarded with an early empty report, regression-tested).

**Where it stopped.** R-BEHAVIOR-EVOLVE is built end to end and consolidated as resolved (23 resolved, 36 open), the deepest form of the behaviour question answered: an animal's behaviour is now evolved from physics and selection, nobody having written "seek water when dry." Honest limits recorded in record 62.19: the pure-frequency deep-time controller expression couples to the deferred quantitative breeding-value tier (25.10) and open temporal LOD (Part 32), so the built proof is the individual and sampled-episode tier; the proxy's honesty is the reserved thing to validate at scale; the intake yield stands in for the R-PHYS-BIO edibility measure until wired to the located matter. Next, on the owner's go: set the reserved `behavior.*` and `physiology.*` calibrations; bring the evolved controller into the dawn `World` and the pre-dawn epoch proper (the pool-tier deep-time controller, which needs the quantitative tier); or continue the living-world track (the dawn-World-to-map bridge, so the walked, foraging beings sit on the generated map).

**Stage 1 (`crates/sim/src/homeostasis.rs`), workspace green, clippy clean.** The physical substrate an evolved controller will read and act through, built so a being's needs and options are consequences of its body, not an authored menu (Principle 9). Two data-defined registries (Principle 11, not closed enums): a `HomeostaticRegistry` of axes (energy, water as the dev fixture, extensible to an arcane charge or a heat store), each a Part 15 `Stock` that drains by metabolism (a consequence of body physics, exertion-coupled) and is restored only by intake whose yield the R-PHYS-BIO edibility floor measures, with death when any axis falls through its per-axis floor (you die of thirst though your energy is full); and an `AffordanceRegistry` of physical operations gated by morphology (move gated on a non-rooted locomotion organ, the walking-tree rule; ingest unconditional), the gates reading the fixed Part 25.14 anatomy categories while the affordance set is data. Deterministic (no RNG, reuses `Stock`), reserved values (capacity-per-mass, base drain, exertion coupling, death floor, per axis) surfaced in `calibration/reserved.toml` with bases. Nine tests, including the walking-tree and per-axis-death audits.

**Next.** Stage 2: the heritable controller as a new genome channel (sibling to cognition and composition), expressed per individual to a fixed-point reaction-norm mapping physiology plus percepts to an affordance primitive, retiring the authored `Behaviour` in the locomotion path. Then Stage 3 (homeostatic-survival selection in the epoch, with a proof behaviour shifts) and Stage 4 plus consolidation (the fixed-point network, then the design.md record and Part 62/63). The design.md consolidation and the Part 62 record wait until the mechanism settles across the stages.

## 2026-07-01 (continued): emergent locomotion, and three physics-in catches

Built the fine end's first slice, then hardened it against the owner's Principle-9 catches, all on `claude/arbitrary-tickrate-step-6e32ma`, workspace green and clippy -D warnings clean throughout.

**Owner set the reserved time batch (via question, confirmed by a test).** Base tick 1 in-world second, life cadence 1 in-world year (note: revisit 1 month if the compute budget allows), generation 10000 years. Recorded in `calibration/reserved.toml` (three `set` entries), reflected in `clock.rs` constants (`LIFE_CADENCE_TICKS`, `YEARS_PER_GENERATION`), wired into the radiation readout, and confirmed by `the_owner_set_time_values_are_what_was_confirmed`.

**Emergent locomotion (`crates/sim/src/locomotion.rs`, `examples/walkers.rs`).** Positioned beings (Coord3 plus subtile fraction) walk at a physics-bounded speed derived from body morphology (allometric size, temperament activity, having a locomotion organ) and terrain cost, reusing the decision layer for which drive is pressing. Deterministic, keyed on seed and tick. The `walkers` example shows a band searching a generated map and settling where water and forage meet.

**Three catches, each an application of physics-in-everything-else-emergent.** (1) Mobility is the body, not the kingdom: the hard `sessile = producer` rule in `sample_body_plan` is replaced by an emergent `rooted_prior` draw (a strong-but-not-absolute role bias, `producer_rooted_prior`/`consumer_rooted_prior` reserved on `GeneratorParams`), so a walking tree (mobile autotroph) and a sessile filter-feeder can both emerge. (2) A being is not a god: it navigates only by what it has perceived within a small true sensory range and remembered (`Walker.known`), and explores to discover what it does not know (heading keyed on `Phase::EXPLORE`), so it cannot head for water it has never seen. (3) Behaviour itself must not be authored: the drive-and-action `Behaviour` is a fixed repertoire chosen from outside the sim, which is steering; it is fixed in place as a placeholder and flagged as **R-BEHAVIOR-EVOLVE** (a heritable policy expressed from the genome, evolved under the epoch's selection, fitness from homeostatic survival), the layer beneath everything. Grounds in `genome.rs` and `epoch.rs`.

**Where it stopped.** The movement physics substrate is built and honest about its limits. The behaviour policy is the next and deepest layer: replacing the authored decision menu with an evolved heritable policy (R-BEHAVIOR-EVOLVE), the formal design.md flag and record still to be written in a dedicated pass. Also pending: the dawn-World-to-map bridge (so the running minds sit on the walked map), perception as line-of-sight and the full Part 9 belief store (being told of a place), and pathfinding (Part 13).

---

## 2026-07-01: arbitrary tick rate, the radiation as a stepper, and a live playback viewer

On branch `claude/arbitrary-tickrate-step-6e32ma`. The owner asked how we stand up an arbitrary tick rate, a tick step, and showing things happening instead of a fixed snapshot, then to build the radiation animation and scope the full vision (one in-world second per real second up to years per second), and asked what dictates walking around under "physics in, everything else emergent."

**Grounded first.** The load-bearing fact: the RNG is counter-based and tick-keyed with no running stream and no wall-clock in canonical state (`rng.rs`, `keys.rs`), so state at tick N is a pure function of (seed, N). An adversarial cross-check confirmed tick-purity (CONFIRMED, qualified) and surfaced two provisos: a live loop must advance only by whole ticks (skipping active ticks is the unsolved Part 32 problem), and must never drive a birth or promotion from a view-time path (those draws key on allocation-order ids, `id.rs`, safe only inside canonical tick logic). The spec already frames arbitrary tick rate as observer playback speed (Principle 10; Part 14.6; Part 54, design.md line 3058) and reserves the base-tick duration.

**Built (all determinism-safe, workspace green, clippy -D warnings clean).** (1) `crates/sim/src/clock.rs`: a `Steppable` trait, a `SimClock` carrying the reserved base-tick duration for the world-time readout, and a view-side `PlaybackDriver` that turns real seconds plus a chosen speed into whole ticks, with pause, single-step, speed control, and a per-frame catch-up cap that surfaces `lod_debt` (the honest temporal-LOD seam). Pure (no `Instant` inside), fully unit-tested. (2) `crates/sim/src/epoch.rs`: extracted `step_generation` and added a `Radiation` one-generation stepper (`impl Steppable`); `run` is now a wrapper; a test proves stepping equals the batch bit-for-bit in report and full allele state. (3) `crates/sim/src/genesis.rs`: a `WorldGenesis` staged driver (worldgen plus founders up front, one generation per step, `snapshot()` to a `LivingWorld`); a test proves stepped-to-completion equals batch `genesis`. (4) `crates/viewer/src/main.rs`: the viewer now holds `WorldGenesis` and advances it through the `PlaybackDriver` decoupled from the render fps, with space/`.`/`,`/`n` controls and a HUD; a headless `--radiate` mode traces the ecology unfolding and confirms it matches batch genesis.

**Scoped (design half).** `docs/time_and_playback.md`: the time/playback model as built and as specified, the two determinism provisos, the emergent-locomotion answer to "what dictates walking around" (physics authored in via anatomy, terrain, energetics; where and why emergent from drives, beliefs, decision, pathfinding, subtile movement, per Principle 9), the full spectrum (fine one-second-per-second walking needs the emergent locomotion path plus the dawn-to-map bridge; years-per-second needs Part 32 temporal LOD plus R-VIEW-ELAB, both open), and a reserved-values batch with recommendations and bases (base-tick duration recommend 1.0s; life-cadence period; generation-to-years mapping), for the owner to set and confirm via a test.

**Where it stopped.** The coarse deep-time end is live and watchable at an adjustable speed today; the fine "walking around" end is scoped as an emergent-locomotion build (not started). Next, per the owner's plan: bring the reserved batch to the owner to set and confirm; then build the emergent locomotion path and the dawn-world-to-map bridge for the fine end. Not yet committed/pushed at time of writing this line.

---

## 2026-06-30 (continued 7): the MCP harness, the living-world variables set, and the anatomy layer

Three things this stretch, all on PR #7, workspace green throughout.

**The civsim-harness MCP server.** Stood up `tools/civsim_harness_server.py` (the same stdlib-only JSON-RPC pattern as `projectops`), registered in `.mcp.json`, exposing `render` (a living-world frame as a PNG), `genesis_stats` (the deterministic summary), `test` (cargo test pass/fail), and `visual_diff` (render-versus-golden regression). Backed by the viewer's new headless `--render`/`--stats` modes. Verified end to end over JSON-RPC. Note: MCP servers load at session start, so it needs a Claude Code reload to become live tools.

**The living-world variables set.** Went over the variables that drive what is built and the owner ratified all four clusters (genetics, speciation firewall, niche shaping, edibility/terrain). Graduated the canonical genome and biosphere values to `set` in `calibration/reserved.toml` (reserved 67 to 57): genetics (loci 8, Ne 200, mutation 1e-4, recombination 0.5, heritability 0.5, selection 0.2), the speciation firewall (distance 0.30, incompatibilities 2, presence 0.9), and predawn generations 40. The ecology tuning levers stay in the crate dev fixtures at their ratified values. Recorded in audit Section 6c. Deferred: the quantitative-tier genome pieces and the physics-floor reference_tolerance (R-UNITS-PIN).

**The anatomy layer (the owner asked how to see creature diversity).** Grounded in the document first: added design.md Part 25.14 specifying a generated species' anatomy as an aggregate-tier morphological trait vector over the catalogue fauna axes, with the full Part 35 per-part Body on promotion (Principle 1), R-BUILD-PHYS and R-WOUND the open siblings. Built it: a `Morphology` (body mass, weaponry, armour, tissue toughness, sensory, encephalization, diet breadth, aggression) sampled per species and carried on the occupant, so creatures now differ anatomically. The viewer sizes each organism mark by body mass (big carnivore a large red mark, small plant a small green one) and the tile selector reads out the selected creature's anatomy at the cursor (kind, species, size, weapon, armour, intel, diet). Also gave the viewer a demo mode (auto-zoom, self-close) and an on-canvas 5x7-font label so the cursor names show on the map, not just the title bar.

**Where it stopped.** The living world is calibrated to the owner's variables, its creatures are anatomically distinct and inspectable, and the MCP harness is up. Next, per the owner's plan (variables, then the dawn): bring the people in, seeding sentient bands onto the map into the biosphere, wiring drives to edibility, and running the tick loop so a civilization begins living in the world. The anatomy trait vector is in place for the consumer wiring the dawn needs.

## 2026-06-30 (continued 6): a tile-selector cursor and the owner's value tuning

**Tile selector.** The viewer now has a mouse cursor: the hovered cell is outlined (`render::draw_outline`) and the window title reads out what is under it, the region cell and its dominant biome in the overview, and the tile, its biome, and the organisms on it (kind and species, "plant#12, herbivore#40") in the superfine. Works in both zoom regimes off the same mapping the paint uses; the `--ppm` snapshot draws it on the centre tile. Pure presentation.

**Value tuning (owner-set, dev fixtures, freely tunable with signoff).** Went over the runnable knobs together. The owner chose: a 256x192 default world, a richer ecology (`GeneratorParams` to 4 trophic layers and 9 niches per layer, so a region grows about 36 founders before radiation), and kept the radiation depth (40 generations) and harshness (extinction floor 0.12, selection 0.2) at the current moderate defaults. Genesis at that size runs about 2.3 seconds in release and about 16 MB. Fixed the stale species-cap test (the richer generator makes more founders than its old cap; the cap bounds the radiation, not the founders). The deeper canonical R-BIOSPHERE reserved calibrations remain a separate, more formal set to pin when wanted.

**Where it stopped.** The living-world viewer is tuned and has a tile selector; `cargo run --release -p civsim-viewer` opens the 256x192 world, pans and zooms from the whole coloured map to the superfine individuals, and the cursor reads out any tile. All on `claude/physics-substrate-fanout`, PR #7, the full workspace green.

## 2026-06-30 (continued 5): the full stack tuned up, the initial goal realized (superfine living map)

The owner asked to "tune it up so we can make the full stack work, and do what my initial goal was" (the large-scale map that zooms to the superfine where you see individual animals and plants). Done: the viewer now runs the whole world-genesis sequence and its zoom reveals the living content.

**The living-world viewer.** `crates/viewer` now builds a `LivingWorld` at startup (worldgen plus the pre-dawn biosphere epoch) and draws it. Below the whole-tile view it draws the biome quadtree overview; zooming in past it enters four superfine levels of magnified per-tile rendering. `render.rs`: the superfine paint draws each tile as a biome-coloured block with the located organisms as marks, coloured by trophic layer (plants green, herbivores amber, carnivores red) and jittered per species so each is a distinct individual form, a pure read of canon (Principle 10, the faithful-render half of R-VIEW-ELAB). A headless `--ppm` snapshot mode writes a superfine frame for display-free inspection (verified visually: biome terrain with organisms scattered as coloured dots across forest, grassland, coast, a lake, and desert). `civsim-sim` and `civsim-core` added as viewer deps.

**Genesis tuning.** Promoted organisms now scatter across each region by a deterministic per-species hash (so the superfine view reads as a spread ecology, not a clustered grid), and each occupant carries an `OccupantInfo` (species, trophic layer, region) so the view can draw it in its individual form. `LivingWorld` gains `occupant_info`.

**Verification.** 2 new render tests plus the genesis changes; the full workspace green, clippy `--all-targets -D warnings` clean, the genesis and living-world proofs still bit-identical. Committed and pushed (PR #7).

**Where it stopped.** The initial functional goal is realized: `cargo run -p civsim-viewer` opens a window that zooms from the whole coloured world down to the superfine, where the individual plants and animals of the self-made biosphere stand on their tiles, all deterministic from one seed. The map-program task (large-scale-to-superfine-individuals) is complete. Remaining tuning and depth (all optional, owner-directed): the reserved calibrations across the dev fixtures (`GeneratorParams`, `EpochParams`, `GenesisParams`, `FloorCaps`) await the owner's numbers; the full 86-axis trait registry as loadable data; the consumer physiology derived from Part 20 drives and Part 35 anatomy; the soil `Stock` worldgen field (a moisture stand-in for now); and richer superfine elaboration (R-VIEW-ELAB proper) if wanted.
