## 2026-06-30 (continued 4): R-BIOSPHERE built onto the substrate, phase 4 joined, and the full simulation proven

The owner said "add them to the build, all that is buildable now, then phase 4, then prove the full simulation." Done, all on `claude/physics-substrate-fanout` (PR #7), each chunk committed and pushed.

**R-BIOSPHERE prerequisites.** `crates/core/keys.rs`: registered the four new phases (BIOSPHERE_SAMPLE 0x0D, GENESIS 0x0E, FOUND 0x0F, SPECIATE 0x11). `crates/sim/src/genome.rs`: the axis-keyed `Channel::Composition(CompositionAxisId)` (F10, heritable tissue composition) and `GenePool::found` (the founder-fork, binomial sampling at a small effective size on `Phase::FOUND`).

**The biosphere mechanism** (`crates/sim`). `biosphere.rs`: the generate-and-validate seeder (Part 25.11), with `EnvProfile`/`Region`, the `Niche` and its Liebig-minimum piecewise-linear tent biome-fit, the `grounded()` least-fixed-point food-web closure, the seeded `generate()` filling trophic layers with a bounded resample and a closure-preserving fallback, and reserved `GeneratorParams`; `SourceRef` is the matter-eating interim (F5). `epoch.rs`: the pre-dawn radiation (Part 25.12), each generation applying the environment-to-coefficient selection kernel (clamped divide-safe), drifting every live pool, forking founders on a cadence into the parent-pointer lineage with an Orr-snowball incompatibility roll, and driving extinctions via the Part 15 population stock; reserved `EpochParams`. `edibility.rs`: edibility as a living relation (Part 25.13) reading the resolved floor (`civsim-physics`), a genesis stick-breaking composition against a per-race physiology, proving the same organism is food to a tolerant eater and poison to a sensitive one.

**Phase 4, the located-identity join.** `located.rs`: the two-way `LocationIndex` between a world `Coord3` and its occupants, entity-world-agnostic (`OccupantId` keyed by `StableId`, tagged Being or Organism), so the hecs-versus-grow fork stays open. `civsim-world` and `civsim-physics` added as `civsim-sim` dependencies (both core-only-plus, no cycle).

**The full simulation proven.** `genesis.rs`: the world-genesis sequence (worldgen becomes region `EnvProfile`s, each region generates a closed-food-web biosphere, the epoch radiates, and surviving organisms are promoted onto the map through the located join), a `LivingWorld` with a canonical `state_hash`. An example (`cargo run -p civsim-sim --example living_world`) renders it: the coloured map, the superfine zoom listing a region's species with their trophic layers and food-web edges and the organisms on its tiles, and the relational edibility. A 48x32 genesis yields 6 regions, 384 species (364 alive), 276 daughters radiated and 20 extinct over 40 generations, replaying bit-identically.

**Verification.** Across the build, 40-plus new tests; the full workspace is green (32 test binaries), clippy `--all-targets -D warnings` and rustdoc `-D warnings` clean. Every value stays reserved-with-basis in a labelled dev fixture (`GeneratorParams`, `EpochParams`, `GenesisParams`, `FloorCaps`); nothing is fabricated.

**Where it stopped.** R-BIOSPHERE is built and proven end to end onto the stock-and-lineage substrate, phase 4 joins sim to world, and the world-genesis sequence runs deterministically and viewably. Named follow-ons: the full 86-axis trait registry as loadable data (the generator uses a compact env-axis fixture); the consumer physiology derived from Part 20 drives and Part 35 anatomy (the interim uses direct `Physiology`); the three named build prerequisites are partly addressed (the soil field is a moisture-derived stand-in until the soil `Stock` worldgen field lands); the reserved calibrations await the owner; and the superfine individual render (R-VIEW-ELAB) so the viewer window draws the located organisms.

## 2026-06-30 (continued 3): R-BIOSPHERE research finished, piping built, and R-BIOSPHERE consolidated as resolved

Finished the R-BIOSPHERE dive the owner had chosen ("finish the research first and consolidate before building"), built the settled piping in parallel, and consolidated R-BIOSPHERE into the design docs as resolved on the owner's sign-off.

**Trait-axis catalogue (dive step 4).** An eight-scout ecology, nutrition, and toxicology fan-out produced one extensible trait-axis registry, synthesized and red-teamed. Eighty-six candidate axes across plant, animal, shared, composition-beyond-floor, and fantasy-profile-gated groups, every range reserved-with-basis (no fabrication), every stored verdict (successional role, fire response, trophic level, biome membership, domesticability) demoted to a read-time derivation, no floor duplication. The red team's fixes folded (`docs/working/BIOSPHERE_TRAIT_AXES.md`): the biological disease-resistance channel, the animal-tissue material and natural-weaponry and delivered-venom axes, and an aquatic niche dimension added; three consumer-less axes dropped to `TraitDef.correlations`; the sentient-referent axis re-keyed off the per-relationship referent; the terran-chemistry caveat on the soil-chemistry axes; and the `body_mass` scale (R-UNITS-PIN) and niche-kernel form (R-GPU-CANON-PIN, piecewise-linear tent recommended) surfaced as pinning couplings.

**Mechanism research (four clusters) and the red-team.** A four-cluster fan-out (generator, consumer wiring, pre-dawn epoch driver, dynamic edibility) synthesized the unified mechanism, red-teamed by a pass that recomputed the Q32.32 arithmetic. Verdict: sound to consolidate in substance, no fabricated number, the steering relocations hold, every law a bounded closed-form integer kernel, the stick-breaking additivity and least-fixed-point food-web closure provably conservative. One cluster agent (dynamic edibility) failed its structured-output retries, but the synthesis reconstructed that field adequately and the red team found no edibility-specific defect. The seven-item fix list and two steering caveats were folded (`docs/working/BIOSPHERE_MECHANISM.md`, section 13): the stock-normaliser citation (fixed by adding a real `Stock::occupancy()`), the sample-time trait-space distance, the select-clamp divide-wrap bound, the pool-mean `checked_mul`, the zero-denominator routes, the SPECIATE generation carry, and three unbuilt prerequisites named (the soil stock, the composition genome channel, `GenePool::found`).

**Piping built in parallel (unaffected by the research).** `crates/sim/src/stocks.rs`: the Part 15 `Stock` (logistic regeneration toward a carrying capacity, collapse under over-draw), `flow` (trophic transfer at an ecological efficiency with an exact `moved = delivered + lost` accounting), and `occupancy` (the `[0, capacity]` to `[0, ONE]` normaliser the biome-fit soil read cites), closed-form Q32.32, RNG-free. `crates/sim/src/lineage.rs`: the F7 parent-pointer species-lineage tree, generic over payload, monotone-id (a deterministic epoch builds a reproducible phylogeny), append-only, canonical-order. 26 new tests, workspace green.

**Consolidated R-BIOSPHERE as resolved (owner signed off).** design.md: the mechanism as Part 25 subsections 25.11 (the generator), 25.12 (the pre-dawn epoch and the world-genesis sequence), 25.13 (edibility as a living relation), with the Decided-and-reserved blockquote; record 62.18; a Part 63 bibliography group. audit.md: consolidation block 1t, a Section 3 fan-out resolved bullet, and the Limitation count 21 to 22 resolved (open holds at 36, R-BIOSPHERE being a former candidate not in the open count). TODOS mirrored. Verify suite clean: em dashes 0, banned adverbs 0, parts gapless 64, fences balanced, records sequential to 62.18, open backlog 36, no stale reference.

**Where it stopped.** R-BIOSPHERE is resolved and consolidated; the stock and lineage substrate is built. Twenty-two resolved, thirty-six open. Next, on the owner's go: build the R-BIOSPHERE mechanism onto the substrate (the trait-axis registry loader, the generate-and-validate seeder with the closure walk and biome-fit, the consumer wiring, and the pre-dawn epoch driver, adding the three named fixed-Rust prerequisites), or the map's phase 4 (the located-identity join) so the viewer can show individuals at the superfine zoom. All on `claude/physics-substrate-fanout`, PR #7.

## 2026-06-30 (continued 2): a windowed colour viewer, and R-BIOSPHERE scoped and forks confirmed

Two pieces this stretch: the owner asked to make the world window viewable as a real window with colour, then to scope the R-BIOSPHERE dive (the living-content gate they had chosen as the next effort).

**The colour viewer.** Added an `Rgb` presentation colour to `BiomeDef` (data-driven, dev-fixture palette, a `BiomeSet::color` accessor). Colour never enters the tile or quadtree state hash, so determinism (Principle 3) and observer-independence (Principle 10) hold. Three render forms now come off the same canonical reads: the plain glyph frame, a truecolor glyph frame (`whole_map_frame_color`, `Camera::frame_color`) for Windows Terminal, and an RGB pixel buffer (`Camera::paint`). New `civsim-viewer` binary crate opens a desktop window via `minifb` (Windows, macOS, Linux, WSLg) and pans and zooms a coloured map (`cargo run -p civsim-viewer`; arrows/WASD pan, +/- zoom, Home recentres, Esc quits); `minifb` is an observer-side dependency only, so core/physics/sim stay free of it. The `zoom_map` example prints in truecolour and a new headless `snapshot` example writes the coloured map as a PPM (verified visually: blue oceans, sandy coasts, green grassland, dark-green forest, brown mountains, white snowcap, pale tundra at the cold poles). 40 world tests pass (6 new), the full workspace green, clippy and rustdoc `-D warnings` clean. Committed 7604655.

**R-BIOSPHERE scoped.** The owner chose to scope the dive before diving. Ran a nine-scout grounding fan-out across the coupled systems and the built code, synthesized a scoping brief, and red-teamed it (the red team verified every build claim against the code with line numbers and found no fabricated value, then surfaced additions). Folded the additions and wrote `docs/working/BIOSPHERE_DIVE_SCOPE.md`: the dive is bounded to two gaps (generate-and-validate species seeding; wiring organisms and consumers to the resolved floor plus the pre-dawn epoch driver), since the edibility/nutrition/harm laws (`crates/physics/src/laws.rs`), the genome engine (`crates/sim/src/genome.rs`), and the map are built and must not be re-resolved. Seventeen forks, eighteen sub-questions, the determinism and steering constraints, two coherence risks (the interim biallelic distance metric the speciation keys off; the unbuilt cheap large-Ne path bounding the pre-dawn generation count), the anticipated reserved values, the couplings, and the proposed sequence. Committed 50aa3f0.

**Owner forks confirmed (select-confirm).** Food-web closure is the hybrid (topological at seed time, then stock dynamics cull and log the die-offs). No mandatory Tier-0 toxin floor: defensive chemistry emerges from selection so no plant-defense ecology is authored in (Principle 9). The intelligence-dial cut-points (which creatures carry minds) are per-world calibration data (Principle 11). Sentient races seed fresh at the dawn for now; the pre-dawn epoch is flora and fauna only, with full sentient pre-dawn co-evolution (heritable axiom seeds per lineage via the reserved `AXIOM_INHERIT` phase) recorded as a future candidate exploration. The other forks ride on the doc's recommendations (F4 DECOMPOSER and F13 AGG-CONSUMER-PROMOTION on provisional ideology-aligned defaults). Recorded in the scope doc section 4.0 and TODOS.

**Where it stopped.** The colour viewer is built, proven, and pushed. R-BIOSPHERE is scoped with the key forks confirmed; the scope doc is the dive's vehicle. The design docs (`design.md`, `audit.md`) are untouched this stretch (the scoping is a working-doc deliverable; R-BIOSPHERE stays a candidate in the backlog until the dive resolves it, so the resolved/open counts are unchanged at 21 and 36). Next, on the owner's go: run the dive's trait-axis catalogue research fan-out (sequence step 4), then harden the closed lists as the flora and fauna structs are first written, then the pre-dawn epoch driver. The map's phase 4 (the located-identity sim-to-world join) remains the buildable spatial next step in parallel.

## 2026-06-30 (continued): the zoomable-map spatial skeleton (the first functional goal), M1 complete

After the physics floors were built and the floor values set, the owner named the first functional goal: "a full fledged map, in large scale with zoomable tiles that themselves zoom down to the superfine where you can see animals and plants and whatnot in their individual forms, seeded from the starting basis we determine, and allowed to create full biospheres to view." A grounding fan-out turned that into a 13-phase living-map program on two tracks (a buildable spatial skeleton; a living-content track gated on R-BIOSPHERE and R-VIEW-ELAB), and three map forks were confirmed (i32 axes for large-scale worlds; a coordinate-backed located place; one canonical quadtree per Part 6.2). This continued session built phases 1 to 3, the spatial zoom skeleton, on the same `claude/physics-substrate-fanout` branch and PR.

**Built (all in `crates/world`).** Phase-1 widening: `topology.rs` `Coord3` and `FlatBounded` from i16 to i32 axes (Part 56), the cell key refolded by FNV-1a now a lossless 64-bit pack no longer fits, neighbours computed in i64 and squared distance in i128-saturate so an i32 edge cell cannot overflow (a new edge test pins this). Phase 2, `lod.rs`: the chunk grid (`ChunkCoord`, `CHUNK = 16`) and the one canonical level-of-detail quadtree over the tile map (Part 6.2), root a power-of-two square at or above the larger map dimension quartering to per-tile leaves, every node summarised by its dominant biome over the world tiles it covers (a `BTreeMap` count with a lowest-id tiebreak, off-world nodes `None`), and `QuadTree::state_hash` folding the whole tree by the core `StateHasher` in canonical level-then-row-major order. Phase 3, `view.rs`: `Camera { center, zoom }` and the two multi-zoom glyph reads, `whole_map_frame` (the overview ladder, one glyph at zoom 0 to per-tile at the deepest zoom) and `Camera::frame` (a fixed viewport centred on a world coordinate, panning past the edge drawing space not panicking), both pure functions of the tree and biome glyphs so the view reads canon and never writes it (Principle 10, Part 54). Wired into `lib.rs` with the public re-exports; `QuadTree::build` takes only the `TileMap` (the build reads biome ids, not glyphs), an honest-dependency cleanup caught by clippy.

**Proven.** 13 new unit tests across `lod` and `view` plus an end-to-end integration test (`tests/zoom.rs`, the seed-to-map-to-tree-to-camera pipeline replaying bit for bit, a different seed a different tree, the zoom ladder one-glyph-to-per-tile) and a runnable example (`cargo run -p civsim-world --example zoom_map`, the visible artifact: at zoom 0 one glyph, zoom 4 coherent continents and coastlines, zoom 7 the full per-tile world with mountain ranges, snowcaps, deserts, tundra). 34 world tests pass, the full workspace 31 test binaries green, clippy `--all-targets -D warnings` and rustdoc `-D warnings` clean. Committed (58e8e8b) and pushed to PR #7.

**Where it stopped.** M1 (a generated visual map, CPU, headless) is complete: topology, terrain and biome substrate, fixed-point worldgen, the chunk grid and quadtree, and the headless zoomable glyph view all landed and proven. The owner can run the example to see the map. Next on the living-map track: phase 4, the located-identity sim-to-world join (a tile to its occupants), kept StableId-keyed so the hecs-versus-grow entity-world fork stays open to phase 8; then the living-content track, gated on R-BIOSPHERE (generating species over the resolved biology floor, which needs the owner to confirm the research-dive scope) and R-VIEW-ELAB (the superfine, non-authoritative view of individuals). The owner's standing direction was "both of those" (continue the build and pursue R-BIOSPHERE); the build (phase 4) is buildable now with no new fork, the R-BIOSPHERE dive needs scope confirmation.

## 2026-06-30: Took over the physics-substrate fan-out; waves 0 (biology) and 1 (mechanical-and-materials) consolidated

Picked up the full physics-and-materials substrate fan-out per `docs/working/FANOUT_TAKEOVER_HANDOFF.md`. Branch `claude/physics-substrate-fanout` off main. The session landed wave 0 (the biology-and-composition floor) end to end and scoped wave 1.

**Audit the input first (the prime directive paid off).** Before asking the owner to sign off the wave-0 biology proposal (which the prior session marked sign-off ready), ran an independent adversarial re-verification (a four-lens Workflow fan-out plus two deeper second-pass finders) that recomputed the Q32.32 arithmetic rather than trusting the prior red team. It blocked the sign-off: the prior F1 ratio fix was incomplete (the dose ratio `r = dose/tolerance` wraps in the unchecked `div` before its guard can run, confirmed by hand: a ng/kg tolerance against a multi-thousand mg/kg dose gives r up to ~3.8e10, 18x over the representable ceiling; the reserved `r_sat` "one epsilon" basis 1625/65536 sits above the overflow-safe `2^(31/n)` bound 1290/46340, confirmed by exact cubes), plus two more div sites wrapping (the verdict margin, the trace-axis underflow misclassifying a potent toxin as inert), and the steering migrated one level down (the edibility law emitting a value-laden verdict, a food-side digestibility scalar and a global medicinal map re-importing a consumer-independent ranking, a toxin set labelled mechanism-keyed but enumerated by terran chemistry, no Tier-0 completeness criterion, a tier-tag contradiction).

**Owner forks (non-final, select-confirm), all answered to the recommendation.** The edibility law returns only the measured tuple and the gain-versus-danger valuation moves to the agent layer (Parts 8, 20); the consolidation records under a sub-identifier of R-DEEPTECH-PHYSICS; wave 1 is the mechanical-and-materials floor at Tier 0.

**Hardened and re-verified.** Rewrote `docs/working/BIOSPHERE_SUBSTRATE_SCOPED_PROPOSAL.md` folding all sixteen findings (Section R2 records the second pass): checked-div-and-saturate harm/margin/nutrition kernels, `r_sat` keyed to the overflow-safe `r^n` ceiling with the guard ordered before the power, per-(toxin-class, consumer) integer Hill exponent, per-quantity trace scales with not-applicable distinct from underflowed-zero, the food-digestibility scalar dropped, a relational medicinal credit (hormesis curve reserved, not consolidated as prose), the measured-tuple edibility law, the honest terran-chemistry toxin label with every per-class axis at Tier 1 and the coarse load their exact sum, a stated-and-met Tier-0 completeness criterion, residual children for partial lumps, and the cross-tier invariant scoped to additive quantities. A focused verification of the rewrite itself caught one self-introduced inconsistency (the additively-summed toxin axes placed on per-quantity scales); reconciled (the summed axes share the coarse scale, the individually-read axes carry their own). Both passes clean.

**Consolidated wave 0 (R-PHYS-BIO).** On sign-off, folded into the design document: the substrate representation (QuantityAxis/InteractionLaw/Substance) as a Part 58 foundational substrate; the biology-and-composition floor and the three laws as a Part 41 subsection with the Decided-and-reserved blockquote; record 62.16; a Part 63 bibliography group; forward-reference flags at Parts 16, 17, 19. Audit log: consolidation block 1r, Section 2 note, a new Section 3 fan-out subsection (R-PHYS-BIO resolved plus the two new open items R-SOURCE-VECTOR and R-BIO-REGISTRY), the R-DEEPTECH-PHYSICS bullet noted as partially advanced, the queue, and the Limitation counts. TODOS mirrored. Verify suite clean: em dashes 0, banned adverbs 0, parts gapless 64, fences balanced, records sequential to 62.16, open backlog 34 to 36 (the two new seams), 20 resolved.

**Wave 1 (mechanical-and-materials) run, hardened, and consolidated.** Scoped and ran the wave-1 fan-out as a green-to-red-to-green-to-red pipeline per domain (Mechanics, Materials, Energy/thermal, the second red pass recomputing the Q32.32 arithmetic), then three consumer-interface agents (Parts 19/41/43/45/35 and the Part 55 neutrality test), then synthesis (sixteen agents). The dominant finding was the parallel-integration problem: the three teams each defined overlapping axes at clashing scales, so the fold is one unified registry, one axis and one canonical scale per quantity, with the energy sub-domain owning the thermal stack and fuel value, fracture-as-stress the shared axis, and a hoisted gravitational axis. The wave-0 product-before-divide overflow reappeared in three mechanical kernels (cut, bend, lever) and was reordered. Wrote `docs/working/MECHANICAL_MATERIALS_SUBSTRATE_PROPOSAL.md` folding every finding (the unification, the three reorderings, the weight and mechanical-power laws, the combustion-limiter fix, the storable output scales, value-density to Part 19), verified the synthesis-write (which caught one self-introduced gap, the torque kilonewton-metre bridge, plus two presentation seams, all folded), surfaced it, and on owner sign-off consolidated R-PHYS-MECH into design.md (a Part 41 subsection plus the Decided-and-reserved blockquote, record 62.17, a Part 63 bibliography group, the Part 19 MaterialProps note) and audit.md (block 1s, the Section 3 fan-out subsection, the queue, the counts). About thirty-three axes and twenty-two laws, every value reserved-with-basis, the one canonical pressure scale (MPa recommended) the owner's pin under R-UNITS-PIN. The named reach ceilings (buoyancy and hydrostatics, radiant and convective heat, heat-to-work) are wave-2 and thermal-deepening scope under the open R-DEEPTECH-PHYSICS, not new counted items, so resolved went to 21 and open held at 36.

**Where it stopped.** Waves 0 and 1 consolidated; the verify suite is clean (parts gapless 64, records sequential to 62.17, em dashes and banned adverbs 0, fences balanced, open backlog 36, 21 resolved). On `claude/physics-substrate-fanout`, with the wave-0 consolidation committed (dcefc81) and the wave-1 proposal, consolidation, and log updates committed alongside, and a PR opened into main covering the effort. Next: wave 2 (the fluids, chemistry, and optics floors, which absorb wave 1's reach ceilings, buoyancy first) is the next fan-out, run under the same green/red recipe; the one owner pin still to confirm is the canonical pressure scale (MPa recommended, R-UNITS-PIN), and the Part 55 dimensionless Ratio class and derived-monomial registrations couple to it. The parallel engine track (the `QuantityAxis`/`InteractionLaw`/`Substance` registry per the locked representation, and the Phase-0 wiring) remains buildable and greenlit.

**Owner direction (post-consolidation, select-confirm).** Four decisions taken. The canonical pressure scale is pinned to the megapascal (R-UNITS-PIN, the first reserved value set in the fan-out; the wider unit-system pinning stays open), recorded in design.md and audit.md. The next effort is to build and prove the two floors rather than widen: build the `QuantityAxis`/`InteractionLaw`/`Substance` registry that loads them, prove the representation and the determinism in code (a Stage-14-style convergence check), then step toward R-BIOSPHERE and the living-world slice, pausing new substrate authoring until the representation is validated. Wave 2 leads with fluids (it absorbs wave 1's buoyancy and hydrostatics ceilings) when the fan-out resumes. The reserved values stay in the design records and audit blocks and seed `calibration/reserved.toml` when the engine is built to consume them, no orphan entries. So the next effort is the substrate machinery and its proof, not wave 2.

**The build, phases 1 to 3: the physics crate, built and proving the floors.** Built `crates/physics`, a new workspace member between `core` and its consumers. Phase 1: the `QuantityAxis`/`InteractionLaw`/`Substance` representation and `PhysicsRegistry` (a TOML loader, content-addressing via the core `StateHasher`, fail-loud reserved ranges, a Part 55 `Dimension` that is a monomial over the four base dimensions so the neutrality test holds by construction, and cross-reference validation), with `Fixed::from_decimal_str` promoted into `core` so the calibration manifest and the substrate share one lossless decimal reader. Phase 2: the 24 closed-form fixed-point law kernels (the 3 biology, the mechanical and material set, the 5 thermal; Hertzian contact a carried seam) keyed by law id, the reserved constants passed as parameters so no kernel bakes a value, behind a determinism harness that recomputes the red-team adversarial cases (the cut, bend, and lever reorderings compute where the product-first forms overflowed; harm routes an out-of-range ratio to the cap with the cube boundary at 1290; the mace and morningstar are one design resolved by contact geometry; the same food is poison to one consumer and safe to another; Euler buckling routes a slender column to zero; the combustion limiter reads fuel-limited; the reductions are order-independent). Phase 3: the two floors encoded as data (`data/mechanical_floor.toml`, 38 axes and 19 laws, the pressure-class axes on the pinned megapascal, iron and oak as cited materials; `data/biology_floor.toml`, 16 axes and 3 laws, no species) and an integration test proving they load with every cross-reference resolving, the reserved ranges the review queue, and deterministic content hashes. 41 physics tests pass; the full workspace (core, physics, units, sim, world) is green; clippy and rustdoc under `-D warnings` and fmt are clean. Commits 3dfc9fc, 87e00e5, ad5780a.

**Where it stopped (final).** Waves 0 and 1 consolidated and the physics floors built and proven in code (phases 1 to 3), all on `claude/physics-substrate-fanout`, PR #7 into main. The substrate engine now exists to consume the reserved values, so the natural next steps are seeding `calibration/reserved.toml` with the wave-0 and wave-1 reserved entries (the axis ranges, the law caps, the gravitational acceleration), wiring a consumer to read the floor (the world materials, or the sim body and biosphere), and R-BIOSPHERE generating species over the biology floor. Wave 2 (fluids leading) is the fan-out continuation when breadth resumes. The carried seams (Hertzian contact, the wear-coefficient split-range, the law output caps, and the reach ceilings) are recorded in the proposal and the audit fan-out subsection.

**Calibration: the floor values set (2026-06-30).** A select-confirm pass over seven grounded decisions (cited recommendations compiled against the datasheet and literature sources) set the reserved axis ranges of both floors as tunable levers: the open-envelope axes to their cited datasheet maxima, the theory-or-definition-fixed intervals ratified, gravity 9.80665 m/s^2 with a per-world override, energy_density stored gross, the harm-law tolerance-zero direction (not-applicable distinct from a present zero), and the law-cap policy. The ranges are set in the floor data files (`crates/physics/data/mechanical_floor.toml` and `biology_floor.toml`); the law caps and the engine-derived constants are recorded in the audit calibration log (Section 6b) and `crates/physics/src/laws.rs`. Two axes stay reserved on their per-quantity scale (R-UNITS-PIN): the geometry second moment of area and the per-class consumer reference tolerance. 41 physics tests green; commit a760870. The next steps are the physics caps manifest the kernels read when wired, a first consumer reading the floor (world materials, the sim body or biosphere), and R-BIOSPHERE generating species over the biology floor.

Fast-forwarded main to the designated branch tip (main was a strict ancestor, so no history was rewritten) and pushed. The red-team Steering Audit on the scoped biology proposal completed before this and its three blocking findings (the integer-Hill overflow/underflow, the hazard_load steering pre-verdict, the divide-by-zero panics) were folded into the proposal, which is now sign-off ready; three further findings (class-membership default, the reach gap, the risk weighting) are recorded for the registry and audit pass. The parallel engine track (Phase 0 plus the substrate machinery) remains buildable and greenlit, recorded in the handoff section 7.

After the three gap bricks, the owner asked for a full slice (build the world, seed creatures and flora, the dawn band exists and interacts and learns from it) and what the gates are. A grounding fan-out and the prime directive (audit the input) found that the slice rests on two unbuilt foundations, and that "build R-BIOSPHERE first" has a hidden prerequisite.

**The gates, grounded.** Three scouts read the actual parts. Finding: the existing `the_first_slice.rs` already stitches map plus band plus naming game plus dialogue, and the learning machinery (perceive to belief, gossip, dialogue, the fallible evidence engine, now channel-gated by the sensorium) is built and free; the gap is that what the band learns about is one hand-placed herd fixture, not living flora and fauna. The two foundations: (A) the physics-and-materials substrate (Parts 19, 40; vehicle `PHYSICS_SUBSTRATE_GUIDE.md`), representation locked but axes and laws reserved and unbuilt, the floor under R-BIOSPHERE's composition axes and under R-SENSORIUM's spatial reach; (B) the spatial/focus layer (Parts 6, 13, 42, 54), where a place is still an opaque `u32` with no coordinate, sub-tile, `Position`/`Velocity`, movement, or focus sim. Two closed-enum seams (Part 16 `GrowthInput`, Part 17 `FoodSource`) need hardening to data registries before species generation. The dependency-ordered program (Phase 0 engine wiring, Phase 1 the two foundations in parallel, Phase 2 R-BIOSPHERE, Phase 3 the living slice) is in the gates write-up.

**Owner decisions (non-final).** Build R-BIOSPHERE the faithful full way; a tile-with-zoomable-subtile spatial model where individuals move and go about their day; interaction is movement plus hunting/predation. Substrate research handles reserved axes/laws by proposing grounded cited values surfaced for the owner to set. Sequencing parallel, but scope the substrate fan-out shape first and surface for the owner's decision. The owner then chose: plan the full fan-out, perform the scoped facets now, and create the full fan-out target for the next effort.

**The substrate work landed this session.** Ran four scoped facet research agents (composition/nutrition axes, toxin/defense axes, consumer physiology axes, the three law forms), each grounded in cited science, reserved-with-basis, anti-steering and determinism aware. Synthesized them into `docs/working/BIOSPHERE_SUBSTRATE_SCOPED_PROPOSAL.md` (the wave-0 biology-and-composition floor: tiered composition axes, a mechanism-keyed toxin registry, paired consumer physiology, and three closed-form fixed-point laws, Liebig net-nutrition, integer-Hill harm, edibility verdict; the pairing map; every reserved value with its basis; the flagged steering seams; the open owner decisions). Authored the full-fan-out target `docs/working/PHYSICS_SUBSTRATE_FANOUT_PLAN.md` (green domain teams across the eight catalogue domains, consumer-interface agents, red Steering-Audit teams, the two-horizon waves, the output schema, the gates). A red-team Steering Audit on the scoped proposal is running. Committed adc0b50.

Deleted all five stale local branches; only the designated branch and main remain locally. The five stale remote branches could not be deleted: the org egress policy denies the delete push (403, per the proxy README, report not retry) and the GitHub tool here has no branch-delete. ## 2026-06-30: The three buildable gap bricks (affect, the life-process loop, the sensorium gate)

The owner asked to scope and build the three buildable gaps from the registered gaps cluster (R-EMOTION, R-AGING, R-SENSORIUM), so that deep end-to-end tests can run. Two forks were surfaced before building and the owner answered both (non-final): affect is a separate data-driven layer (not folded into drives or beliefs), and the appraisal is derived from the agent's own drives rather than an authored event-to-emotion table. All three are built as engine bricks; each stays an open research item, because the deeper questions and the reserved calibrations remain.

**R-EMOTION, the affect layer (`crates/sim/src/affect.rs`).** An `AffectAxisId` registry (sibling to the drive and value substrates, not a closed emotion enum) and a per-being `AffectState` of current-and-baseline values per axis in `[-1, 1]`: `apply` (clamped), `decay` toward baseline (the deterministic fade), and `harden` (when a sustained feeling's deviation exceeds a reserved threshold the baseline drifts toward it by a reserved fraction, the trauma residue ordinary decay no longer erases). The derived appraisal is `AppraisalBinding`, a per-race map from a `DriveId` change to a signed delta on an affect axis (the gain and the relief sign are data), so a relieved need and a worsened one push opposite ways with no authored reaction table. Wired into `World` as `appraise`/`affect_level`/`set_affect`/`decay_affect`/`harden_affect`. The follow-on named in the code: wiring affect into the Part 8 decision considerations so it modulates choice.

**R-AGING, the per-entity life-process loop (`world.rs`, `keys.rs`).** The keystone the gaps trace found missing: the engine had no clock-driven life beat. `World::age_step` advances every tracked being's age one life-cadence step (saturating); `World::apply_mortality(&Curve)` rolls each being in id order against an owner-supplied age-hazard curve (a rising hazard is the data-driven default, non-final), keyed deterministically on the being and its age under the new `Phase::MORTALITY` (the age occupies the tick coordinate, so a being faces the same hazard at the same age on replay and the pass is observer-independent and thread-count-invariant); `World::remove_being` prunes every per-being map (minds, placement, genome, intrinsic, affect, age, sensorium, drives, last action, lexicon, language, the promoted set, and every trust edge naming the being) so no dangling reference survives (the Part 58 referential-integrity guarantee). Age is seeded at the dawn and at birth. Still open and recorded: the pool-tier age distribution and symmetric pool-tier mortality the code `Pool` cannot yet carry, the reserved cadence period and hazard shape, and wiring the cadence into `World::tick` on that reserved period.

**R-SENSORIUM, the channel gate (`crates/sim/src/sensorium.rs`, `world.rs`).** Re-scoped on intake to the buildable half: the channel substrate (Part 33.3) is built, so the gap is split into a channel gate (built here) and spatial propagation (the coordinate-blocked follow-on). A `SenseChannelId` over the physics-channel substrate and a per-being `Sensorium` (channels-read and channel acuity). `Trace` now carries a `channel`; `World::perceive` gates on it: a being with an installed sensorium perceives a trace only on a channel it reads (its channel acuity scaling the roll), and a being with no sensorium reads every channel, so every existing place-based test is unchanged. The spatial reach-and-attenuation half waits on the physics-substrate medium and field set.

**The capstone.** `tests/dawn_lifecycle.rs` gains a generational-turnover test that runs `age_step` plus a birth plus `apply_mortality` for ten generations on the seeded being model: it proves births and deaths both occur, the population identity `founders + births - deaths` holds across every birth and death (referential integrity), and the whole turnover replays bit for bit. The being model now turns over generations end to end.

**Where it stopped.** Built on `claude/ground-truth-analysis-setup-pjux91`; full workspace green (fmt, clippy `-D warnings`, rustdoc `-D warnings`, all tests, 167 sim lib tests including the new affect/sensorium/aging/turnover cases), design parts gapless, audit em dashes 0, banned adverbs 0, backlog count unchanged at 34 (the three stay open with their buildable halves built). Audit Section 3 and TODOS carry an engine-status note per item. Next: the owner asked for a full slice (build the world, seed creatures and flora, the dawn band exists and interacts and learns from it); the gates to that are the next thing to scope.

## 2026-06-30: The gaps-and-holes audit, traced and registered

The owner handed a "Gaps and Holes" document (everything the world and engine models are missing) and asked to trace and implement it. By the document's own discipline ("Nothing here is a design... work its audit questions before its research, not assume the entry is right and build") and the prime directive to audit the input, the faithful implementation was to trace each entry against the actual design and register the verified gaps into the backlog, not to write engine code for unaudited gaps (most of which depend on unbuilt substrates).

**Trace.** Stored the vehicle at `docs/working/GAPS_AND_HOLES.md` and ran a three-scout read-only fan-out (agent-interior gaps, lifespan-process gaps, world-history plus the covered re-verification), then verified the load-bearing findings against the parts myself. The scouts confirmed every gap and corrected the document in three places: the sensorium gap is narrower than framed (the channel substrate of Part 33.3 is already built, so the real gap is spatial propagation, not the channel model); Part 42 is a numbered in-spec Part, not a candidate; and the childhood gap is weaker than claimed (Part 10.2 is its principal home and critical periods already live in the age-keyed plasticity curves of Parts 20 and 28). They also surfaced one clean root blocker for the whole lifespan layer (the engine has no clock-driven life-process loop; `World::tick` advances cognition only) and a code/design divergence (the code `Pool` lacks the age distribution the design's Part 11 and Part 17 pools carry).

**Register.** Added the nine confirmed gaps as counted backlog items in audit Section 3 (a new "Gaps and holes cluster" subsection), each with its verified status, sites, on-ideology constraints, buildable-now-vs-blocked, and audit-first note: R-EMOTION (most load-bearing, buildable now as an affect-axis registry plus a per-mind affect vector), R-AGING (the lifespan keystone, blocked on the life-process loop), R-REST, R-SENSORIUM, R-REPRO, R-CHILDHOOD, R-JUSTICE, R-LIVELINESS (cross-cutting), and R-TECTONICS (an enrichment, not load-bearing, by demand-closure). Updated the open count 25 to 34, the Limitation enumeration, a Section 2 tracking note, and the TODOS mirror with the buildable-vs-blocked map. The trace also re-confirmed the covered systems (hydrology, erosion, catastrophe volcanism, archaeology, art/medicine/money) against the parts, mapped day-and-night to the R-CELESTIAL candidate and meetings to the existing R-COMMS, and answered the covered entries' own audit questions from the text (money emerges from exchange; rediscovery feeds belief not truth; rivers shift course over deep time only through catastrophe).

**Per-site design-doc flags deferred.** Several gaps are absences (no emotion or aging mechanism to notate), and the gaps doc's discipline is audit-questions-first, so a per-site "Needs research" blockquote in the design document is deferred until each gap is taken into a design; the audit Section 3 cluster, the Section 2 note, and the vehicle are the tracking.

**Where it stopped.** Committed and signed (d54a1f6) on `claude/ground-truth-analysis-setup-pjux91`, pushed; audit em dashes 0, banned adverbs 0, backlog count 34, design parts gapless. No engine code written this entry. The buildable-now gaps, in rough order of value and independence: R-EMOTION (self-contained, most load-bearing), the R-AGING life-process loop (the keystone that unblocks R-REPRO and R-CHILDHOOD), and the R-SENSORIUM reach brick. The rest are blocked on the day-night system, the physics substrate, the Part 36 institution code, or each other.

## 2026-06-30: Calcification, the full-birth integration, the scenario loader, and a deep lifecycle test

The owner asked for all the remaining buildable pieces so deep end-to-end tests can run. Built three bricks plus a capstone, all on the established build-and-verify discipline; the blocked items stayed deferred (prestige needs a status system, the conformist continuous form and the integer-Gaussian mutation are owner/stress-test forks, the LOD aggregate-pool axiom regime needs the pool tier extended).

**Calcification (`axiom.rs`, `world.rs`).** `Axiom::calcify` raises an unchallenged axiom's entrenchment by a reserved rate toward a reserved cap; `Axiom::weaken` lowers it toward a floor under a near-miss challenge (brittleness). Because a higher rank raises the entrenchment gate, an unchallenged axiom hardens and resists what it once yielded to, the labile-to-calcified transition. `World::calcify_band` runs a quiet-phase pass. Committed (this batch).

**The full-birth integration (`world.rs`, `race.rs`).** `World::birth` bears a child of two parents that inherits both halves of its being: a genome recombined under the race's `GeneticScheme` (`reproduce`, keyed under `Phase::REPRODUCE`), a mind expressed from that genome (`Mind::from_genome`), and intrinsic beliefs inherited from a parent and the local band. The genome and axiom kernels first meet here. `Race` now carries its `GeneticScheme` and derives ploidy from the reproduction mode (the explicit ploidy field is gone); `inherit_child` and `birth` share an `inherited_beliefs` helper. Deterministic, with the Principle 10 caveat on the child-id belief keying noted.

**The scenario loader (`scenario.rs`).** `Scenario` parses a `scenarios/*.toml` into a typed profile (identity, race posture, magic posture, and per-dial `Direction` tokens real/high/low). The four canonical scenario files load and read as expected. Resolving a direction to a concrete value needs the reserved magnitudes, so this is the parse-and-represent step; layering a scenario over the manifest into a runnable `World` is the next step, gated on those magnitudes (never fabricated).

**The capstone deep lifecycle test (`tests/dawn_lifecycle.rs`).** Runs the integrated being model as one loop: dawn seeding, then six generations of bounded-confidence enculturation, calcification, and a birth each generation. Proves the whole composition replays bit for bit, and asserts the emergent signatures: population grows from five founders to eleven through births, the founders calcify to the cap, and the starting spread stays fractured into at least two sects rather than collapsing to consensus.

**Two self-caught defects during the build.** A unit test compared a mutated seed against a separately-rounded 0.4 and failed by one fixed-point bit (0.1 is not exact in Q32.32); switched to a tolerance check. A doc intra-link to `crate::genome::reproduce` failed because reproduce is a method; made it a plain code span.

**Where it stopped.** Commits a662b04 (genome dawn), then the schism (edd26a1), inheritance (2c8be61), the three-brick batch (429c731), and the capstone (954d9d7) on `claude/ground-truth-analysis-setup-pjux91`, signed and pushed; full workspace green (27 ok-lines), fmt/clippy/rustdoc clean. Research counts unchanged at 19 resolved and 25 open. The being model now runs end to end: a race, a genome and a mind from it, intrinsic beliefs, enculturation, schism, calcification, inheritance, and full births, all deterministic. The stress-test batch holds three forks (structured value-distance formula, evidence-ring hybrid, conformist continuous form). Remaining: the conformist and prestige biases, the integer-Gaussian mutation shape, the two LOD group regimes, the pre-dawn biosphere epoch, the scenario-over-manifest application, and wiring births into a tick loop.

---

## 2026-06-30: Bounded-confidence schism and axiom inheritance (two more axiom bricks)

**Schism (`axiom.rs`, `world.rs`).** The bounded-confidence mechanism that turns enculturation into clustering. `bounded_confidence_mean` is the confidence-weighted mean over only the neighbours within a reserved confidence band epsilon of a member's stance (the synchronous Hegselmann-Krause form), and `confidence_weighted_variance` is the fission signal. `World::enculturate_band_bounded` runs a per-member bounded round off one pre-update snapshot (so it replays bit for bit); `axiom_variance` and `is_fissioning` compare the spread against a reserved threshold; `stance_clusters` returns the sects as the 1-D epsilon-gap clusters, which are exactly the connected components of the within-band influence graph, sorted canonically by stance then id. Members far apart admit none of each other, so a spread band splits into clusters that never merge, the schism. epsilon and the fission threshold are caller-supplied reserved values. Deferred (consistent with the prestige deferral): the conformist and prestige transmission biases. The prestige arm is blocked until a status system exists; the conformist arm has no blocking dependency but its continuous-opinion form is underspecified (the literature's cubic is for discrete traits), so it is a stress-test-batch fork rather than a fabricated form. Actual band partitioning into new sub-bands is the band-management layer, also deferred; this brick detects and reports the sects. Committed and signed (edd26a1).

**Inheritance (`axiom.rs`, `world.rs`, `keys.rs`).** The last deferred single-axis brick. `inherit_seed` makes a child's innate seed the heritable-plus-encultured blend `h*parental_seed + (1-h)*local_mean` plus a bounded mutation in `[-spread,+spread]` from a counter-RNG unit draw, closing the loop with enculturation (the heritable term is the parent's own innate seed, the Friedkin-Johnsen prejudice). `World::inherit_child` mints a child and, per axiom the parent holds, blends the parent's seed with the band's local mean plus a mutation drawn under the new `Phase::AXIOM_INHERIT`, keyed on the child id and the axis so two axes never collide; the child copies the parent's epistemic stance and values and gets fresh evidence rings of the parent's capacity (added `EvidenceRing::cap`). It is the intrinsic-belief half of a birth; the genome half (`GeneticScheme::reproduce` plus `Mind::from_genome`) and combining the two into one birth are the integration follow-on, so the returned child carries beliefs but no mind or genome yet. Deterministic, with the Principle 10 caveat noted (keyed on the child's canonical id, safe while birth order is a deterministic function of canonical state). heritability and mutation spread are caller-supplied reserved values; per-axis heritability via the axis registry and the integer-Gaussian mutation shape (25.10) are refinements. Committed and signed (2c8be61).

**Two fixes during the inheritance build.** A unit test compared a mutated seed against a separately-rounded 0.4 and failed by one fixed-point bit (0.1 is not exact in Q32.32); changed to a tolerance check. A doc intra-link to `crate::genome::reproduce` failed because reproduce is a method, not a module item; made it a plain code span.

**Tests added.** Schism: 2 axiom unit tests (bounded mean admits only within-band neighbours; variance is zero for a uniform band and positive for a spread) and 4 integration tests (`tests/schism.rs`: two far clusters never mix, a within-band group stays one sect, variance signals fission, deterministic replay). Inheritance: 1 axiom unit test (blend plus bounded symmetric mutation) and 5 integration tests (`tests/inheritance.rs`: blend of parent and culture, heritability extremes, bounded mutation over generations, deterministic replay, a belief-less parent yields None).

**Where it stopped.** All on `claude/ground-truth-analysis-setup-pjux91`, signed and pushed; full workspace green, fmt/clippy/rustdoc clean. Research counts unchanged at 19 resolved and 25 open. The stress-test batch now holds three forks (structured value-distance formula, evidence-ring hybrid, conformist continuous form). Remaining axiom bricks: the conformist and prestige biases, calcification, the integer-Gaussian mutation shape, and the two LOD group regimes. Then the pre-dawn biosphere epoch and the scenario loader.

---

## 2026-06-30: Enculturation, the next axiom brick (Friedkin-Johnsen anchored average)

**What landed (`axiom.rs`, `world.rs`).** The enculturation brick of R-AXIOM (design Part 28). Two pure functions in `axiom.rs`: `confidence_weighted_mean`, the canonical group aggregate (sum of stance times confidence over a band on one axis, divided by the total confidence, accumulated in 128-bit integer space with a single final divide, so it is bit-identical regardless of member order or thread count, and `None` when the total confidence is zero); and `enculturate`, the Friedkin-Johnsen update `new = (1 - theta) * group_mean + theta * innate_seed`, with theta the member's effective stubbornness (the per-axiom base lifted by its epistemic temperament, from brick 1). `World::enculturate_band` wires them over a band: it computes the mean once from the members' pre-update stances (a synchronous round), then moves each member toward it anchored to its own seed; `World::set_intrinsic` lets a being's beliefs be set (by the dawn, by later inheritance, and by tests). Because each member anchors to its own seed by a positive theta, a band with distinct seeds reaches a persistent spread rather than collapsing to one stance, the lasting disagreement the design wants (DeGroot consensus is the theta=0 degenerate case). This is not a culture-level kernel firing: the band profile is the derived aggregate and only members move. The bounded-confidence neighbour selection and the conformist and prestige biases that sharpen this into schism are the deferred next brick.

**Tests.** Two new axiom unit tests (the confidence-weighted mean weights correctly and is order-independent; `enculturate` anchors by theta, DeGroot at theta zero and immovable at theta one) and four enculturation integration tests (`tests/enculturation.rs`: members move toward the mean but stay distinct; a population with distinct seeds never collapses to one point over twenty rounds; an aligned band does not drift; deterministic replay).

**Where it stopped.** Committed and signed (fbca716) on `claude/ground-truth-analysis-setup-pjux91`, pushed; full workspace green, fmt/clippy/rustdoc clean. Research counts unchanged at 19 resolved and 25 open (a build of a resolved item). Next deferred axiom bricks: bounded-confidence schism (prestige arm needs a status system), inheritance (new keyed `Phase` + mutation-spread reserved), calcification, and the two LOD group regimes; then the pre-dawn biosphere epoch, the scenario loader, and the two stress-test-batch formulas.

---

## 2026-06-30: seed_dawn_populations, the convergence point, and the R-CELESTIAL candidate

**The dawn convergence (`world.rs`, `race.rs`).** Built `World::seed_dawn_populations`, the step that replaces the abstract civilization placement of the old worldgen pass (design Part 28) and is the point where the deep being model's parts first run together: the map (where bands land), the genome (what a member inherits and expresses), the value substrate (Part 21), and the axiom kernel (Part 28). A new `Race` record (`race.rs`) bundles a race's per-race data: its `GeneSet` and aggregate `GenePool` (Part 25), its ploidy, its innate `IntrinsicBeliefs` disposition (Part 28), and its environment cognition baseline. A `BandSpec` is a race, a place, and a member count. For each band member the seeding mints an id, promotes a genome from the pool (Hardy-Weinberg, keyed by the being's id so members differ genetically, 25.8), expresses the mind from that genome through the gene set (`Mind::from_genome`, 25.6), seeds the innate disposition, and places it; `World` now carries per-being genome and intrinsic-belief maps with `genome_of`/`intrinsic_of`. It is genesis-time and deterministic (the seeding order fixes the ids and the id-keyed draws, reproducible from the seed and inputs; being genesis-time, the id-keyed draw is not observer-influenced, so the Principle 10 caveat does not bite). At the dawn every member of a race shares the innate belief seed; per-member divergence is the deferred inheritance and enculturation work. Cognition from a pool-promoted genome rides the race's environment baseline plus Mendelian dominance until the quantitative breeding-value tier lands. Three integration tests (`tests/dawn_seed.rs`): a mixed two-band seed with genomes, minds, beliefs, and places; genome-draw determinism; an unknown-race band skipped. Committed and signed (a662b04).

**R-CELESTIAL candidate recorded, references verified.** Stored the owner's celestial and day-night design exploration verbatim at `docs/working/CELESTIAL_AND_DAY_NIGHT_GUIDE.md` and registered it as a candidate exploration in TODOS (not in the open count): a celestial body as a generated point over property axes, the sky a generated profile-level configuration, and day/night, seasons, tides, phases, and eclipses as measured fixed-point consequences rather than stored states, with use (navigation, calendars, cosmology, magic) emerging through the existing perception, technique, belief, and magic systems. On the owner's instruction I verified every part reference against the design: all sound except the topology citation (the `Topology` enum including Spherical as a seeded start variable is Part 56 and Part 40, not Part 12, which is worldgen) and the emic measurement hook (precisely Part 55, "a span of time from a celestial cycle"); an editorial verification note recording the audit is appended to the vehicle. Its Section 8 research questions are summarized in the candidate entry. Sequenced alongside the physics-and-materials substrate (with R-BIOSPHERE), since its illumination and exotic axes read through it. Committed and signed (32fa1a0).

**Where it stopped.** All on `claude/ground-truth-analysis-setup-pjux91`, signed and pushed; full workspace green, fmt/clippy/rustdoc clean. Research counts unchanged at 19 resolved and 25 open (the dawn seeding is integration of resolved items; R-CELESTIAL is a candidate, not a flagged open item, so the four-candidate list does not change the open total). Next per the roadmap: the deferred axiom bricks (enculturation, schism, inheritance, calcification, group regimes), the pre-dawn biosphere epoch on the pool tier, the scenario loader, and the two stress-test-batch formulas (structured value-distance, evidence-ring hybrid).

## 2026-06-30: The axiom kernel (R-AXIOM) brick 1, fanned out and select-confirmed

**Approach.** Built the first brick of the axiom-belief kernel (design Part 28, the resolved R-AXIOM) the way the owner asked: a three-scout read-only fan-out (a fork miner over the research paper, a determinism-and-steering auditor against the principles, a dependency-and-scope scout over the codebase), then I verified their findings against the actual design myself before surfacing a batch of select-confirm, non-final forks. The mechanism was already decided (a multi-field axiom separating position from hold; an epistemic stance parametrizing one kernel; an AGM entrenchment gate; Friedkin-Johnsen enculturation; bounded-confidence schism; calcification; heritable+encultured inheritance); the forks were implementation-form, data-shape, and determinism seams the record leaves open at the code level.

**The four decisions (each non-final, in the stress-test batch).** AxiomDomain becomes a data registry seeded with the five standard domains, not a closed enum: the owner asked me to triple-check this against all the design docs first, and I did. Part 28 and the paper's `AxisDef` present the domain as a fixed tag set and locate emergence at the axis level, but the paper's own caveat (line 156) is decisive ("hard-coding any one taxonomy would be both unfaithful and un-emergent"), the kernel never dispatches on the domain (verified), and nothing sanctions the domain set as a deliberate hardcoding the way Part 33.2 sanctions the NSM primes, so the fixed enum was an unflagged gap and the registry resolves it where the design's own principle points. The Friedkin-Johnsen stubbornness anchor is a per-axiom base modulated by the epistemic stance (the design supports it: the struct carries the field and the paper says it "may be derived from the epistemic block"), with the positive base keeping theta above zero so a population holds lasting disagreement. The evidence ring evicts FIFO by recency (the paper's specified "ring buffer of recent tags"; my audit moved the recommendation off the owner's earlier hybrid lean toward FIFO, which the owner accepted, deferring the recency-weighted-pressure hybrid to the stress-test batch "if compute allows"). The revelation jump is emergent from the one pressure formula, which dissolves the separate revelation-jump-threshold reserved value.

**What landed (`crates/sim/src/axiom.rs`, 10 tests).** The representation (`Axiom`, `EpistemicStance` with unit-sum-normalized source weights and `effective_stubbornness`, `EvidenceTag`, `EvidenceRing` FIFO, `AxiomAxisDef`/`AxiomAxisRegistry`, `DomainDef`/`DomainRegistry` with the standard seed, `SourceModeDef`, `IntrinsicBeliefs`) and the single-mind appraisal/update kernel: pressure = salience x source_weight x (1 - dogmatism); the entrenchment gate assimilates below threshold (belief perseverance proven by test) and accommodates above (moving toward the evidence by a step that grows with pressure, so the revelation jump clears a deep entrenchment in one move, proven by test); `entrenchment_threshold` reads the reserved curve via `decision::Curve`. Source modes are a data registry with no hardcoded mode ids in the kernel (the anti-steering directive). Added no new reserved values (the consumed `axiom.entrenchment_curve` and `axiom.evidence_ring_capacity` already exist; the emergent jump removed one).

**Deferred to later bricks (dependencies unbuilt).** Enculturation (the FJ anchored average over a group), bounded-confidence schism (its prestige arm blocked until a status system exists), inheritance (the heritable+encultured seed blend on a new keyed `Phase`, which will add the mutation-spread reserved value), calcification of unchallenged axioms, and the two LOD group regimes. The dependency map and slice rationale are in TODOS.

**Steering boundary recorded.** The kernel is clean (an innate disposition is an authored input, like gene effect sizes, not an authored outcome), but the Steering Audit must bite at axiom *consumers* (Parts 8/19/36) so no system maps a stance to a named institution or religion. Tracked in TODOS.

**Where it stopped.** Committed and signed (6a22123) on `claude/ground-truth-analysis-setup-pjux91`, pushed; full workspace green, fmt/clippy/rustdoc clean. Research counts unchanged at 19 resolved and 25 open (a build of a resolved item). Stress-test batch now holds two entries: the structured value-distance formula and the axiom evidence-ring hybrid. Next per the roadmap: `seed_dawn_populations` (using `from_genome`, pool promotion, and intrinsic-belief seeding), then the deferred axiom bricks, the pre-dawn biosphere epoch, and the scenario loader.

## 2026-06-30: Roadmap continuation, the genome-to-Mind bridge and the value-distance metric core

**The genome-to-`Mind` bridge (`agent.rs`, the named next roadmap step).** A mind's cognition is now a phenotype, not an authored number. `Mind::from_genome(id, genes, genome, environment)` expresses the three cognition channels (reasoning acuity, memory capacity, belief plasticity) from a being's genes through the same deterministic float-free `GeneSet::express` every phenotype uses, so a mind's sharpness is its inheritance and differs by race and by individual from one rule (design Part 25.6). Acuity is the live channel (it scales every observation and the perception roll); memory and plasticity are carried as the genome-derived phenotype, their consumers (belief deterioration per Part 9, update-rate modulation per Part 20) the named follow-on. All three fold into the mind state hash. `Mind::new` keeps its acuity-only signature with neutral memory and plasticity for fixtures. A new test drives the chain end to end: a sharp genome concludes from a sub-threshold weight the same dull genome cannot. Committed and signed (6b0c80d).

**The value-distance metric core (`value.rs`, R-VALUE-METRIC build), with one seam flagged on the owner's call.** Grounding the build in record 62.3, I found the mechanism pinned in shape but the structured-distance algebra unpinned (the record reserves the structured weighted form as a design choice and lists weighted-versus-transport agreement as still to be proven). I surfaced this and the owner chose "build core, flag formula." Built: per-race `ValueStructure` (independent axes, weighted graph, relationship matrix); the offline `GroundMetric` compiler, an exact-integer all-pairs shortest-path table (Floyd-Warshall over integer edge costs) proven against hand-computed rings and paths; the pinned independent-axes Euclidean distance over the shared axes; the cross-race etic plumbing (`EticSubstrate`, `EmicProjection`, `project_to_etic`, `cross_race_distance` with the reserved incommensurability floor, an untranslatable emic axis a theory-of-mind blind spot); and `conflict_pressure` with the reserved coefficient and floor caller-supplied, never invented. The structured (graph/relationship) weighted distance returns `None` rather than running on a fabricated formula; the `GroundMetric` it will consume is built and proven, ready for the formula when settled. The candidate forms (tree/graph Wasserstein versus a Mahalanobis quadratic) are named in the module doc and the TODOS build-seams section. Committed and signed (524447d).

**`Fixed::sqrt` added to core.** The independent-axes Euclidean distance needs a square root, and `conflict_pressure` adds the distance linearly (so a squared form would not do). Added a deterministic `Fixed::sqrt`: an integer square root over the 128-bit radicand `bits << FRAC_BITS`, no float, bit-identical across machines, returning zero for a negative input (a sum of squares cannot be negative). Tested on perfect squares, the floor property, and determinism.

**Where it stopped.** Three commits on `claude/ground-truth-analysis-setup-pjux91` (the genome bridge, the value-metric core, both riding `Fixed::sqrt`), all signed and pushed; full workspace green, fmt/clippy/rustdoc clean, both Steering Audit invariants hold. Research counts unchanged at 19 resolved and 25 open (both items are builds of resolved designs; the structured-distance formula is a flagged build seam within R-VALUE-METRIC, tracked in TODOS, not a new open research item). Next per the roadmap: the axiom kernel (R-AXIOM, Part 28; gauge its own seams first), then `seed_dawn_populations` (using `from_genome` and pool promotion to seed minds from per-race gene sets), then the pre-dawn biosphere epoch on the pool tier, and the scenario loader. Open owner decision queued: the structured value-distance formula.

## 2026-06-30: The genome-evolution engine completed, the four canonical test worlds, the provisional-tuning principle

**The genome-evolution engine, finished.** Closed out the M2 deep-genetics build on the already-resolved R-GENOME (Part 25). On top of the representation and expression brick from the prior session, this session's prior commits had landed the per-race `GeneticScheme` inheritance (segregation, recombination, discrete-state mutation; 25.2/25.4/25.5) and the aggregate-tier `GenePool` (Wright-Fisher drift, directional selection, frequency-distance, declared speciation, Hardy-Weinberg promotion and demotion; 25.7/25.8). The keystone added now is the Dobzhansky-Muller incompatibility layer (25.7): `IncompatibilityKind` (a fixed mechanism affordance, Sterilizing or Lethal, on the `ForceKind`/`DominanceKind` footing), `HybridOutcome` (Viable/Sterile/Inviable, severity-ordered), `Incompatibility` (a complementary allele pair at two loci), and the data `IncompatibilityTable` registry. `GenePool` gained `carries` (a binary-state presence test against a threshold) and `reproductively_isolated` (distance threshold OR cross-pool active-incompatibility count). The kinds are fixed Rust, the table is data that grows with the world (Principle 11). The four method-fork decisions the owner confirmed are now all built: exact Wright-Fisher drift (the large-Ne Gaussian approximation deferred), the interim frequency-distance measure, speciation by distance OR incompatibility count, and a pure-aggregate pre-dawn tier. 22 genome tests green; full workspace green; fmt, clippy, rustdoc clean. Committed and signed (01bd2ab), pushed.

**A manifest duplicate fixed in passing.** The `dawn_band` calibrated-manifest test was failing on a duplicate `genome.effective_population_size` id (a coarse early placeholder and the implemented detailed entry both present; the loader rejects duplicates). Folded the two superseded coarse genome placeholders (`effective_population_size`, `speciation_thresholds`) into the implemented detailed set, kept `genome.fertility_curve` as a distinct future tuneable (the soft genetic-distance-to-fertility falloff beyond the built binary hybrid outcome). Surfaced two new reserved values with bases: `genome.speciation_incompatibilities` (the active count that declares isolation) and `genome.allele_presence_threshold` (the per-pool carry threshold).

**The four canonical test worlds.** On the owner's request, defined the canonical starting scenarios the engine is built and tuned against, which also serve as determinism and Steering-Audit stress tests. `docs/working/TEST_WORLDS.md` is the design-of-record; `scenarios/` holds one TOML per world plus a README. Mirror: grounded baseline, no magic, the control. Tempest: grounded, no magic, every change-and-extremes dial cranked high (the stress test of the change engines). Arcanum: high-magic stress test, minimally grounded (carries the most placeholders since Part 34 magic is least built). Confluence: grounded reality with abundant magic, the headline target whose couplings are the hardest to balance. Each is a profile over real dials with the direction named (`real`/`high`/`low`) and the magnitude reserved, so no value is fabricated; the magic and some change dials in Arcanum and Confluence are named by mechanism until those parts land. The scenario loader that reads a `scenarios/*.toml` into a `World` as a named manifest override set is the named next build.

**The provisional-tuning principle (owner standing instruction).** Recorded the owner's working principle that nothing chosen now is final, for the numbers and the method forks alike: a setting is assumed not to survive scale unchanged, every dial rides a sliding scale between fidelity and cost (tune detail down where a system eats the compute budget, add it back where there is headroom), and a value set today is a hypothesis awaiting a playtest. Recorded in the `calibration/reserved.toml` header, `docs/working/TEST_WORLDS.md`, and `TODOS.md`.

**Branch note.** This session's designated branch is `claude/ground-truth-analysis-setup-pjux91`, which was an ancestor of the continuity branch `claude/determinism-keying-and-map` where all prior work sits. Fast-forwarded the designated branch to carry the full history plus this work (725ad77..01bd2ab, no force, nothing lost) and continued on it.

**Where it stopped.** All code committed, signed, pushed to `claude/ground-truth-analysis-setup-pjux91`; full workspace green, fmt/clippy/rustdoc clean, both Steering Audit invariants hold. The docs (`TEST_WORLDS.md`, `scenarios/`, the reserved.toml header note, `TODOS.md`, this entry) are the remaining uncommitted change to land next. Research counts unchanged at 19 resolved and 25 open (the genome work is build of a resolved item; the test worlds are config, not a research item). Next per the roadmap: attach a genome to a `Mind` (cognition channels feeding acuity, memory, belief plasticity), then the axiom kernel (R-AXIOM) and the value-distance metric (R-VALUE-METRIC), then `seed_dawn_populations`, then the pre-dawn biosphere epoch on the pool tier (R-BIOSPHERE), and the scenario loader.

## 2026-06-30: Faithfulness and steering audit of the implemented engine, and R-BIOSPHERE candidate

**The audit.** Ran four independent skeptical reviewers (the Agent fan-out, not a workflow) over the implemented code (core, world, sim), one lens each: steering (P9/P8/P11), determinism (P3), never-fabricate, and faithfulness to the resolved design. Then verified every surviving finding against the code myself before acting, since I wrote the code. Headline: the engine is faithful and clean. No CRITICAL or MAJOR steering, fabrication, or determinism-of-computation defects. The content gate has real teeth (no magnitude field exists to smuggle an outcome), move magnitudes come from the reserved `gossip.*` values not the substrate, felicity gates rather than weights, the anti-projection rule and clamp-at-read hold, the two Part 41 invariants are real passing tests, and the worldgen/biome/move starters are all labelled fixtures.

**What the verification corrected (the prime directive paying off).** The determinism reviewer's two MAJOR findings were to fold the move log and the dialogue substrate into `state_hash`. Verified that this would BREAK both Part 41 invariants: content-blindness asserts permuted move-kind ids hash equal, and channel-swap asserts different channel ids hash equal, and both ids live in the move payload. So `state_hash` is deliberately an outcome-state hash. Documented that boundary at the function so it is not "fixed" wrongly later, and added a separate `World::event_log_hash` for move-sequence replay integrity (used in the replay tests, never in the invariants).

**The one real behavioural catch (fixed).** Faithfulness finding: the inquiry-answer leg hard-coded the deception verdict to `false`, bypassing the Part 37 sincerity frame, so an answer that conflicts with the asker's witnessed model of the answerer was grounded blindly. Now the asker judges an answer exactly as the INFORM path does (`detects_lie`), so it is seen through. New test `an_answer_that_conflicts_with_the_askers_model_is_seen_through`.

**Other fixes.** The payload `Cursor`'s `u32`/`u64` reads are total by construction now (no `try_into().unwrap()`). Two doc-precision fixes: the `ForceKind` "starting menu" prose now distinguishes data entries from fixed kinds, and `primes.rs` cites 33.1/33.9 for the prime inventory and reserves the 33.2 "one sanctioned hardcoding" for the lemma.

**Deferred follow-ups (latent or policy-bound, tracked in TODOS).** First-order `EvidenceRef` is not channel-tagged (latent: the load-bearing guarantee holds via the nested frame; tagging touches the hot path broadly). Drift applies form-change rules in vector order (fine while one rule per generation; sort when multi-rule). The dialogue reserved values (felicity bands, uptake step, budget, promotion thresholds) are surfaced in design record 62.14 but not yet in `reserved.toml`, which is consistent with the manifest's policy of adding entries as the sub-features are built. `World::from_manifest`'s Calibrated gate covers the nine cognition/gossip keys but not language/dialogue (those fail loud per-subsystem at install).

**R-BIOSPHERE candidate recorded.** Stored the owner's biosphere-generation guide verbatim at `docs/working/BIOSPHERE_GENERATION_GUIDE.md` and registered it as a candidate exploration in TODOS (not in the open count): species as generated points over trait axes with food-web-closure and biome-fit validation then deep-time diversification, and edibility/nutrition/toxicity/medicine as a measured relation between an organism's composition and a consumer's physiology rather than a stored flag. It directly informs Track 1.

**Where it stopped.** All committed to `claude/determinism-keying-and-map` and pushed; full workspace green, fmt/clippy/rustdoc clean, both Steering Audit invariants hold. Research counts unchanged at 19 resolved and 25 open. The four next-direction tracks are queued (Track 1 living world, Track 2 deep being model, Track 3 emergent concepts, Track 4 group dialogue and meetings); the owner chose to start Track 1 after this audit. The R-BIOSPHERE candidate is the design vehicle for the ecology and edibility part of Track 1.

## 2026-06-30: Conversation refinements (inquiry, redundancy suppression, coined words)

Three improvements to the dialogue scene, on the owner's pick, before resuming deep M2.

**Inquiry and redundancy suppression (`agent.rs`, `world.rs`).** The converse step is no longer a broadcast. A `Mind` carries a `wondering` set (the inquiry goals of 9.13). Per promoted speaker the step chooses an intent: INFORM a committed belief a peer lacks, else INQUIRE an open question it cannot answer by asking a co-located peer. A question records a move and seeds the inquiry goal in the hearer (being asked makes you wonder too); if the asked peer holds the belief it answers and the asker grounds it. Redundancy suppression is via theory of mind: a speaker informs a peer only while it does not already model that peer as holding the value, so after a couple of said-acceptances the model commits and the speaker stops; once everyone is modelled as knowing and no question is open, the conversation falls silent. New `Mind` methods (wonder, is_wondering, open_questions, committed_beliefs, shared_belief), `World::set_wondering`/`is_wondering`, a `MoveEffect::Inquire`, and a `reply_as_prior` flag so an answer keeps its belief content while an acceptance points at the prior move. The wondering set folds into the mind state hash.

**Speak in the band's coined words (`language.rs`, `world.rs`).** `Lexicon::utterance` renders an ordered sequence of concepts as a mind's coined words (placeholder for any not yet coined); `World::lexicon` exposes a mind's lexicon. The full-slice example tags the herd belief's subject, attribute, and value with content concepts the band names alongside the primes, so a move renders as a primitive sentence in the emergent language ("wotuka halo nene"), the English gist alongside. The tagging is a fixture mapping; grounding the content as a drifting substrate region remains deep-M2 work.

**Scenes.** `conversation` now seeds two seekers, so the run shows questions, answers, and the talk falling silent after a few ticks. `the_first_slice` speaks the herd talk in coined words. Both replay bit for bit; the two Steering Audit invariants still hold.

**Where it stopped.** Three commits on `claude/determinism-keying-and-map`, pushed; full workspace green, fmt, clippy and rustdoc (`-D warnings`) clean. Research counts unchanged at 19 resolved and 25 open. Next, per the owner: resume deep M2 (the genome inheritance brick, then attaching a genome to a `Mind` so acuity, memory, and belief plasticity come from genes, then the axiom kernel R-AXIOM and the value metric R-VALUE-METRIC). The gate to conversation improvement #4 (disagreement and persuasion over values and axioms) is exactly that agent-tier opinion kernel; belief-level disagreement is already reachable through the defeasible inference engine.

## 2026-06-30: Autonomous build, the full-slice capstone and the genome representation brick

**The capstone (`crates/sim/examples/the_first_slice.rs`).** The first end-to-end emergent slice in one run, stitched from the milestones built so far: a generated world (M1), a band seeded onto a habitable cell with the cell as its place (lean M2), the naming game over all sixty-five NSM primes coining the band's own lexicon (M3), and then an emergent conversation (M4) in which a scout's witnessed knowledge of where the herd ranges grounds across the band as first-class move events reassembled into one conversation. The map is rendered with the band marked, the coined lexicon and the talk shown, all replaying bit for bit. The one honest seam, noted in the example and the roadmap, is the next build: the conversation content renders through the English gist, not the band's coined word, because a belief value is not yet grounded as a concept the naming game named (the `ConceptId`-as-substrate-region work of deep M2).

**Deep M2 begun: the genome representation (`crates/sim/src/genome.rs`).** The foundation brick of R-GENOME (Part 25): what a genome is (25.1) and how a genotype expresses a phenotype channel (25.3, 25.4), as a self-contained, data-driven, pure-integer module with no tick-path change. A multi-locus quantitative spine (alleles carry an additive value and a discrete Mendelian state; a haplotype is the alleles in gene order; a genome is one or two haplotypes plus a scheme id). `GeneDef`/`GeneSet` are data; `Channel` is the fixed mechanism interface the genotype expresses into (a Part 20 trait setpoint by data trait id, or a cognition, build, imbued, or life-history channel), on the `EvidenceOrder`/`ForceKind` footing, with anatomy intentionally absent. `GeneSet::express` is the deterministic integer reduction (summed allele additives plus the heterozygote dominance deviation, times the gene's weight, plus the environment), which collapses to the weighted allele sum in the additive limit, reconciling with the Part 20 inheritance rule. 8 tests. Deferred to follow-on bricks: inheritance and recombination, mutation, epistasis, the two-tier allele-frequency pool with Hardy-Weinberg promotion, and deep-time drift and selection (25.5, 25.7, 25.8).

**Where it stopped.** Both committed to `claude/determinism-keying-and-map` and pushed; full workspace green, fmt, clippy (`-D warnings`), and rustdoc (`-D warnings`) clean. Research counts unchanged at 19 resolved and 25 open (both are implementation behind already-resolved designs). Next deep-M2 bricks in order: the per-race `GeneticScheme` and inheritance (segregation and recombination, the counter-RNG draws keyed on the schema), mutation, then the two-tier pool with promotion and demotion, then attaching a genome to a `Mind` (the cognition channels feeding acuity, memory, and belief plasticity) and `seed_dawn_populations` onto the map. The intrinsic-belief and axiom kernel (R-AXIOM, Part 28) and the value-distance metric (R-VALUE-METRIC, Part 21) are the other deep-M2 tracks. The `ConceptId`-as-substrate-region grounding that would let a witnessed fact be spoken in the band's coined word is the seam the capstone surfaced.
